IRVING, EDWARD A.—Joined Perak, F.M.S., service, 23rd Mar., 1891; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1891; passed final exam. in Chinese (Hakka dialect), Feb., 1895; ag. collr. land rev., Kinta dist., June, 1895; prot. of Chinese, Larut dist., July, 1896; warden of mines, Selangor, Feb., 1898; warden of mines, Perak, Jan., 1899; passed final exam. in law, July, 1900; inspr. of schools, Hong Kong, Apr., 1901; mem. of educn. comtce., 1901-2; passed final exam. in Cantonese, May, 1905; ag. registr.-gen. and M.L.C., July to Sept., 1905; ag. registr.-gen., Sept., to Oct., 1906; ag. registr.-gen. and M.L.C., Apr., 1908, to Mar., 1909, and Aug. to Sept., 1910.

IRVING, HENRY EDWARD.—Ed. at Charterhouse and Handelsehranstalt, Leipzig; trooper, 9th Lancers, 1899-1902; served in S. African campaign; ch. clk. to central repatriation bd., O.R.C., Mar., 1902; clk. to col. sec., Oct., 1902.

IRVING, SIR HENRY T., G.C.M.G. (1888), K.C.M.G. (1878), C.M.G. (1874).—B. 1833; clk. in C.O., Nov., 1854; promoted to 3rd class, Oct., 1859; priv. sec. to Sir F. Rogers, now Lord Blatchford, 1862; promoted to 2nd class, Apr., 1863; col. sec. of Jamaica, June, 1866; col. sec., Ceylon, May, 1869; gov. Leeward Is., 1873; gov. Trinidad, July, 1874; gov. Br. Guiana, 1882; ret. 1888.

IRVING, HON. PAULUS AMILMIUS, M.A., D.C.L.—B. 1857; ed. Trin. Coll. Schi., Port Hope, and Trin. Coll., Toronto; called to the bar of Ontario, 1880; of B. Columbia, 1882; dep. atty.-gen., B. Columbia, 1883; judge of sup. ct. of B. Columbia, 1897; spec. commr. in Atlin Dist., 1899; judge of the ct. of appeal, B. Columbia, 1909.

IRVING, WILLIAM HOWE—Apptd. stock inspr., Queensland, 1866; junr. offr., custom dept., Brisbane, 1867; 2nd offr., shipping master, etc., Maryborough, 1870; ch. clk., Brisbane, 1883; landing survr. on amalgamn. of offices, Apr., 1886; collr. of customs and ch. inspr. of distilleries, Queensland, 1893; mem. of imgrm. bd., 1894.

ISAAC, CHARLES POWYS.—Ck., coll. branch, ex. and audit dept., Aug., 1890; ck. in charge of accts., Jan., 1894; detached for service as loc. auditor, Uganda Prot., June, 1895, to Sept., 1897; and again as loc. audr., N. Nig., May, 1900, to Aug., 1901; prin. clk., Transvaal aud. off., Apr., 1902; ast. aud.-gen., Transvaal, Feb., 1903; ag. aud.-gen., Dec., 1903, to May, 1904; aud. to I.C.C., S. Africa, Oct., 1904.
ISAAC, FRANCIS WHITMORE.—3rd ast., Uganda Prot., 8th Dec., 1897; coll., E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1902.

ISAACS, HENRY BABER.—B. 1857; ck. of petty sessions and sub-collr. of taxes, dist. No. 3, Tobago, July, 1878; ag. inland rev. offr., Leeward Dist., Oct., 1878, to Dec., 1878; ag. 2nd rev. offr., Mar., 1880; 1st rev. offr., May, 1882; rev. offr., and registr. of births and deaths, Carriacou, Nov., 1884; is a J.P.; ag. pol. mag., Carriacou, for 2 months in 1887; 1st landing waiter and port offr., St. Vincent, Oct., 1889; pol. mag. and asts. treas., 4th dist., St. Vincent, July, 1898; pol. mag. and coroner, 2nd dist., St. Vincent, Apr., 1902.

ISAACS, HON. ISAAC ALFRED.—B. 1855; called to the bar, Victoria, 1880; Q.C., 1889; M.L.A., Victoria, 1892-1901; solr.-gen., 1893; atty.-gen., 1894-9; and again in 1900; mem. of Aust. Fed. Convention, 1897-8; and of first Federal parlt.; atty.-gen. in Deakin ministry, 1906-6; judge of High Ct. of Australia, Oct., 1906.

ISLINGTON, 1ST BARON (cr. 1910), Rt. Hon. SIR JOHN POYNDER DICKSON-POYNDER, K.BART., K.C.M.G. (1911); D.S.O. (1900); J.P.-B. 1866; ed. at Harrow and Christ Church Oxford; major Wilts Yeomanry; served in S. Africa, 1900 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); lieut., 3rd Royal Scots; mem. L.C.C., 1898-194; M.P., Chippenham, Wilts., 1892-1910; gw. New Zealand, 1910.

ISRAEL, JOHN WM., I.S.O. (1910).—B. 1871; ent. Tasmanian govt. serv., on transfer of rly. mkt. to govt., Aug., 1872; was successively rly. mkt., statn. master, and acctnt. of rly.; transit audit off., as chief clk., 1882; subsequently asp. dep. aud.; aud.-gen. of Tasmania, 1895; trans. to serv. of Com. of Austr., as aud.-gen., 1st Jan., 1901.
IZARD, REV. HERBERT CRAWFORD—M.A. (Oxon); B. 1869; col. chaplain, Malaca & Stltlms., 19th Oct., 1901; ag. col. chap., Sep., Oct., 1904; confirmed, May, 1905.
JACKMAN, HON. EDWARD MICHAEL—B. 1866; M.H.A. for Placentia and St. Mary's, Newfoundland, 1900; min. of finance and cust., 1900; re-elected to Assembly, 1904.

JACKMAN, HENRY THOS.—Crystal Pkss. engng. schl., 1890-3; pupil and ast. on water & drainage wks. to Messrs. McLandborough & Prenton, civ. engrs., 1893-6; in charge of sewage wks., Ripon, 1896-7; ast. dist. eng. on electric tramways, drainage wks., etc., Sheffield, 1897-1903; exec. engr. on water & drainage wks., 1903-1910; ag. san. survr., 1905; seconded for spec. serv. in China, 1908.

JACKS, PHILIP.—Ed. Plymouth Coll.; adm. solr., sup. ct., England, May, 1900; ast. aud. offr., Hong Kong, Aug., 1905; J.P., Oct., 1906; commr. for oaths, Oct., 1905; ag. land offr., Apr., 1907, to Apr., 1908.

JACKSON, BEDVER BLAINE LOWTHER—Eton and Univ. Coll., Oxford; clk. to atty.-gen., O.R.C., July, 1901; clk. to legis. coun., July, 1902; 1st clk. to atty.-gen., July, 1904; ed. editor of law-book, O.R.C., 1906.

JACKSON, CECIL GOWER, J.P., F.R.C.L.—B. 1872; ed. private tuition; silver medal, Eng. literature exam., 1891; student interpreter and clk., sec. native affairs off., and clk. in Ixopo and Newcastle magistrates from Oct., 1888; 3rd cls. clk., Newcastle, 1st July, 1892; clk. of ct., Zulu interp., sub-distributor of stamp and dep. clk. of peace, Impendhle, 12th Oct., 1894; clk. of ct., etc., Weenen, 8th July, 1897; 2nd cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1898; frequently asted mag., Impendhle and Weenen, 1895-1901; clk. ct. and ag. ast. mag., Ladysmith, 1st Jan., 1902; ag. mag., Ladysmith, May-Nov., 1902; ag. ast. mag., Umzinto, Feb., 1903; ag. ast. mag., Newcastle, Aug., 1903; mag. 1st Jan., 1904; 2nd cls. mag., Durban, Jan., 1904 to Nov., 1905; mag. Weenen, 11th Nov., 1905; marriage offr., 5th Dec., 1905; mag., Mahlabatini (for spec. purposes), 14th July, 1908; representative native affs. dept. on native educn. advisory bd., Mar., 1909; delimited new magisterial boundaries, June, 1909; adjusted other divisional boundaries, June, 1909; commr. to enquire into sentences on native rebels, July, 1909; mag., Ndawandwe (for spec. purposes), 21st Sept., 1909; promoted 2nd div. of mag., 1st Jan., 1910; judge, native high ct., 19th May, 1910; served in S. African campaign, 1899-1902; and in Natal native rebellion, 1906.

JACKSON, FREDERICK JOHN—C.B. (1889); C.M.G. (1902); ed. at Shrewsbury Schi. and Jesu Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. ast., Uganda Prot., July... 1894; vice-consul, May, 1895; dep. comsnr., Apr., 1901; dep. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1902; lieut.-gov., 1907; East and Cent. African medal with clasp, Uganda, 1897-98, and Luba and African gen. ser. medal, Uganda, 1900; mem. of exec. and leg. couns., E. Africa Prot., 1907.
JACKSON, HENRY.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1906; chmn. of comtees, 1910.

JACKSON, COL. HUGH MELBOURNE, R.E.—B. 1858; survey of India, 1883-95; Burmese expedit., 1885-9 (ment. in desps.); ordnance survey of Great Britain, 1895-9; S. African war, 1899-1902; attached to army headqtrs. staff for mapping and reconnaissance; A.A.G. topography, Nov., 1900, to end of war (ment. in desps. and brevet); pres. of Natal-Transvaal boundary, comsnr., 1902; survr.-gen., Transvaal, Mar., 1903, to May, 1905; attached to intell. dep., S. Africa, June-Nov., 1905; re-jd. ordnace, surv., Mar., 1906; brevet-col., Apr., 1905; survr.-gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1908.
JACKSON, T.—Curator, botanic station, Antigua, Leeward Is., 1905.

JACKSON, THOS. BEDDARD.—B. 1874; ed. at Bromsgrove coll.; govt. shorthand writer, Trinidad and Tobago, 1st Jan., 1900; ditto, and clk. col. sec.'s office, 8th June, 1901; shorthand writer to asphalt industry comsnr., 1902; ditto to riot inquiry comsnr., 1903; sec. to comtece. on labour question, 1905-6; ditto to Diego Martin local road bd. comsnr., 1905-6; ditto to comtece. on municipal govt. in Port-of-Spain, 1906; ditto to comtece. on extension of local govt., 1906; ditto to comsnr. on Manzanilla local road bd., 1906; sec. of industrial training bd. of Trinidad and Tobago (in addition to other duties), 1st May, 1907; editor of "The Book of Trinidad."
JACKSON, W. B.—Verderer, forest dept., E.A.P., Apr., 1904.

JACKSON, WM. HENRY.—Cadet, Ceylon, 23rd Sept., 1879; pol. mag., 1st May, 1886; asst. govt. agt., 21st June, 1895; prin. collr. of cust., 11th June, 1901; ag. controller of rev., in addition to his own duties, July, 1908, Nov., 1908, Jan., 1909, and April, 1910.

JANISCH, NOEL.—Served under the St. Helena gov't., Aug., 1870, to Apr., 1878; 3rd class clk., office of commr. of crown lands and pub. wks., Capetown, Apr., 1878; in active service with Cape Volks in the Transkei in 1879, and during Basuto war, 1881; 2nd class, clk., July, 1881; 1st class, Apr., 1887; ch. clk., 1892; sec. for pub. wks., Sept., 1892; additional mem. of tender bd., May, 1894; under col. sec., July, 1898; dir. of census, 1904; elected fellow of Royal Statistical Soc'y., 1909; prov. sec., Cape Province, 1910.
JARRETT, MICHAEL LEWIS, M.R.C.S. (Lond.), L.R.C.P. (Edin.).—Asst. col. surg., Sherbro, W. Africa, 1882; is a J.P.

JARVIS, LIEUT.-COL. ARTHUR LEONARD FITZ-GERALD, I.S.O. (1905).—B. 1852; entd. Canadian gov't. service, 1868; priv. sec. to P.M.G., 1882 to 1885; and to min. of agric., 1885 to 1892; sec. of dept. of agric., 1896; commanded Gov.'s General's Foot Guards, 1899 to 1904; asst. deputy min. of agric., 1909.

JARVIS, EDWARD BLACKWELL.—B. 1873; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Leeward Is., 1890; clk. of gov.'s office, 1891; ag. 1st clk., 1891; ag. 1st clk. col. sec.'s office, Mar. to June, 1896. On reorganisation of office, apptd. 2nd clk.; ag. ch. clk., and clk. to fed., exec., and legis. couns. and local legis. coun. of Antigua, Apr., 1897, to Jan., 1898, and July to Dec., 1899; P.S. to ag. gov., June to Oct., 1899; ch. clk. col. sec.'s off., and clk. to fed., exec., and legis. couns. and local legis. coun., Antigua, 3rd July, 1901; dir., pub. off.'s guarantee fund 6th Dec., 1902; asst. col. sec., Leeward Is., Feb., 1903; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is. and Island sec., Antigua, 24th Mch. to 15th Oct., 1905; ag. fed. treasur., Aug. to Oct., 1905; trade and income tax assessor, 27th Dec., 1905; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is. and Island sec., Antigua, 12th Nov. to 11th Dec., 1907, 11th to 22nd Jan., 1908, 20th Apr. to 19th Oct., 1908, 19th Apr. to 25th Nov., 1909; J.P., Antigua, 1908; chmn., St. John's City commsrs., 1st Jan., 1910.

JARVIS, ERNEST FREDERICK.—B. 1862; ent. civ. serv., Canada, as 3rd cls. clk. in post office dept., 1881; priv. sec. to the S. of S. and subsequently to the min. of militia and defence, 1892-1895; transf'd, to dept. of militia and defence, 1893; chief clk., 1903; sec. to militia coun., 1903, and asst. deputy min., 1909.

JARVIS, T. LESLIE H.—Dep. regis. of marriages, births and deaths, 1890-2, and census offr. for parish of St. George, Antigua, 1891; clk. and priv. sec. to admstrs. and clk. to exec. coun. from 1895; ag. clk. to bd. of health, Dominica, 1896; priv. sec. to ag. gov., Leeward Is., Sept., 1904; ag. treasur., mem. of exec. and legis. couns. and of quarantine bd., commr. of piers, recvr. of wrecks and regis. of shipping, Dominica, Aug., 1905, to Apr., 1906; and on two other occasions for short periods in 1906 and 1909; commr., Virgin Is., mem. of gen. legis. coun., Leeward Is., Aug., 1908; mem. exec. coun., Leeward Is., Jan., 1910.
JEBB, R. H.—Asst. local auditor, E.A.P., 1909.

JEFFERY, GEORGE.—Scholarship, R. Coll. Art and Science, 1872; scholarship in architecture, R. Academy of London (1874); F.R.L.B.A.; architect to Rt. Rev. Bishop in Jerusalem in the East, 1892; hon. corres. mem. of Imperial Institute of Archaeology of Russia, 1897; sec. for Cyprus Society of Antiquaries; mem. of pub. wks., Cyprus, 1898; curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, 1903; author of "Summery of Architectural Monuments of Cyprus," and several papers on the medieval archaeology of Cyprus and Jerusalem.
JEFFRIES, CHARLES WILLIAM.—Comp. Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1897; comp. Royal Observatory, Cape Colony, 1902; 1st ast. Hong Kong Observatory, 1907.

JEKYLL, LIEUT.-COL. SIR HERBERT, K.C.M.G. (1901), R.E., C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1846; lieut. R.E., Apr., 1866; employed under War Office and Trinity House in 1868 to 1870, as destruction of sunken ships, from submarine mining, and under G.P.O. in the telegraph dept., from 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1877; served in the campaign of 1873-74 on the G. Coast; priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Jan., 1877, to Feb., 1878; sec. to the defence comte., 1878; sec. to the roy. comte. on the defence of Br. possessions and commerce abroad, 1879-82; specially employed to report and on the defences of Singapore and Colombo, 1883-4; sec. col. defence comte., 1884; priv. sec. to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of India, June, 1885, to Jan., 1886; sec. to roy. comm. Melbourne centennial exhibn., 1888; priv. sec. lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-5; sec. to the roy. comte. for the Paris exhibition, 1900.

JELF, ARTHUR SELBORNE.—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford (ext.), 2nd cls. class. mods., 1897; 3rd cls. lit. hist., 1899; B.A., 1899; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1899; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1901; pass final exam. in Malay, May, 1901; ag. dist. off., Kuala Langat, Selangor, Dec., 1903; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1905; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, March, 1905; ag. dist. off., Pekan, Pahang, March, 1907; ag. asst. sec. to res., Perak, Oct., 1908.

JENKINS, SIR GEORGE HENRY, K.T., B.A. (1904), C.M.G. (1891).—B. 1843; clk. in Victoria gov't. rly., 1861-3; clk. of priv. bills and priv. sec. to speaker in legis. assem., 1865-70; clk. of comtees., 1870-78; clk. asst., 1878-82; and clk. of legis. assem., 1882-91; since when he has been a parlmt. of Victoria; sec. to royal comte. on question of payment of members of legislature, 1868; sec. to royal comte. for completion of house of parlmt., 1877; clk. of Australasian Federal conf., Melbourne, 1890; special vote of thanks for services; presented by members of legis. assem. with service of silver plate on completion of its twenty-fifth year as an offr. of their house, and unanimous resolution of legis. assem. was passed on its records in recognition of his prolonged and singularly valuable services rendered to the house and its members, on his retiring from the position of clk. of the assem. to fill the office of clk. of parlmts.; clk. to fed. coun. of Australasia during its eighth and last session, Melbourne, Jan., 1886; presented by members of legis. coun. with a gold watch and chain on leaving their services temporarily for the Commonwealth parlmt.; appointed by Prime Minister of Australia to act as sec. to Commonwealth govt. and to undertake management of... ceremony connected with opening of Commonwealth parlmt., and also of the reception given to their Royal Highnesses the Duke and Duchess of York, on 9th May, 1901; specially thanked for services by the Duke of York, the Gov.-General, and the Prime Minister; offered by the Prime Minister the position of clk. of the House of Representatives of Commonwealth parlmt., and accepted it temporarily; on retiring from clerkship of H. of R. to return to his office of clk. of parlmts. of Victoria the members unanimously accorded him a vote of thanks for his able services to the Commonwealth in respect of the inauguration of the parlmt.; elected pres. of the Society of Old Melburnians for the year 1894-5; is an hon. J.P. for Victoria, N.S. Wales and S. Australia.

JENKINS, THE HON. JOHN GREELEY.—B. 1851; mem. H. of A., S. Aust., 1887 to 1905; min. of ed. and N. territory, 1891-2; comsrr. of pub. wks., 1892; ditto, 1894-9; chief sec., 1899-1901; premier and ch. sec., 1901-5; agent-gen. in London, July, 1905, to July, 1908.

JENKS, STUART DIXON, LL.B., K.C.—B. 1869; ed. Picton Acad., Dalhousie Univ., and Cornell Univ.; called to the bar, 1896; practised law in Amherst, Nova Scotia, 1896-1908; K.C., 1908; dep. atty.-gen., Nova Scotia, 1908.

JERNINGHAM, SIR HUBERT EDWARD HENRY, F.R.G.S., K.C.M.G. (Jan., 1893), C.M.G. (1889), B. ès Lettres, Paris, Officier d'Académie, France (1904).—B. 1842; attaché, 1866; passed an exam. and obtained an hon. certif., 1886; attaché at Paris, 1867, and was transfd. to Constantinople, 1870; 3rd sec., 1870; on temporary duty at Athens, 1870; and was transfd. to Carlsruhe and Darmstadt, 1872; ag. chargé d'affaires, 1873; 2nd sec., 1873; ag. chargé d'affaires, 1874, 1875, 1876, and 1877; was transfd. to Vienna, 1877; ag. agrt. and consul.-gen. at Belgrade, 1878; was unemployed from 1879 till 1881, when he resig.; M.P. for Berwick, 1881-5; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1887; received Queen's Jubilee Medal, 1887; administered the govt. for three months in 1888; col. sec., Mauritius, 1889; administered the govt., Mar., 1892; licent.-gov., Aug., 1892; gov. and commr.-in-ch., July, 1893; gov., Trinidad, Jan., 1897, to 1900; is a J.P. and D.L. for Northumberland.

JETTE, SIR LOUIS AMABLE, K.C.M.G., LL.D.—B. 1836, at L'Assomption, Quebec, and ed. there; called to the bar, 1857; el. to H. of C., 1872; judge of the super. ct., Quebec, 1878; professor of civil law, Laval Univ., LL.D., 1878; comsrr. for revision of civil code, 1887; lieut. gov. of Quebec, 1898-1903; mem. Alaskan Boundary Tribunal, 1903; chief just. Court of King's Bench for the Prov. of Quebec, 1909.
JEWELL, M.—B. 1880; Apptd., after compet. examn., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1899.

JEX, ROBERT A. N.—Ed. St. John Berchman's Coll., Belize, Br. Hond.; apptd., after compet. exam., copyist in col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1897; ag. 4th clk., Aug. to Nov., 1897; ag. 3rd clk., May, 1898; ag. 3rd clk., Feb., 1899, to Feb., 1900; ag. clk. to reg. genl., Oct. to Dec., 1900; ag. 2nd clk., Apr. to Sept., 1901; 3rd clk., May, 1901; clk. to auditor, 1st Dec., 1902; ag. 2nd clk., C.S.O., 24th Apr., 1903, to 26th July, 1904; ag. clk. to engnr., 5th Jan. to 18th Apr., 1905; ag. 1st cls. clk., C.S.O., 21st Apr. to 31st Aug., 1905; 2nd cls. clk., gen. reglary., 1st Sept., 1906; ditto, atty.-gen.'s off., 1st Apr., 1907; ag. 1st cls. clk., treasy. and cust., Jan., 1907; 1st cls. clk., treasy. and cust., 16th Apr., 1909.

JOHN, WM. AUGUSTUS.—Ed. at Wesleyan high schl., Lagos; passed civ. ser. exam., 1902; 3rd cls. certif., Lond. Coll. Preceptors, 1903; cadet, secretariat, Lagos, Sept., 1903; transfd. to judicial dept., Apr., 1904; 6th cls. clk., Feb., 1905; 5th cls. clk., Apr., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., audit off., July, 1907.
JOHNSON, D. O.—B. 1873; entd. Bahamas civ. ser., 1904; Out Island consmr. (3rd div.), 1909.

JOHNSON, EDWARD RICHARD.—Joined post. and tel. dept., O.F.S., Oct., 1890; asst. postmnr. and telegraphist, Jagersfontein, Oct., 1890; ditto, Fauresmith, Nov., 1892; postmnr. and telegraphist, Thaba' Nchu, June, 1894; ditto, Koffyfontein, Sept., 1894; ch. clk. to P.M.G., O.F.S., Dec., 1896; relieving offr., Feb., 1897; ch. clk., secretarial branch, dep. of posts and tels., O.R.C., Mar., 1900; prin. clk. and asst. sec., ditto, July, 1902; asst. sec., ditto, July, 1903; postmnr., Bloemfontein, 1909.

JOHNSON, E. O.—Ed. Epsom Coll. and Harr. Coll., Barbados; 2nd treasy. offr., Montserrat, 1884; 1st treasy. offr., 1886; 1st clk. treasy., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1891; 1st rev. offr., 1894; ag. treasur., Dec., 1896, to Sept., 1897; asst. treasur., S. Leone', Nov., 1897; col. treasur., Mar., 1899; has acted as col. sec.
JOHNSON, F. E. G.—B.A., Cantab.; asst. dist. consmr., So. Nigeria, Apr., 1900; dist. consmr., July, 1902; asst. sec. (temporarily), Feb. to Aug., 1904; ag. pol. mag., Calabar, Aug., 1904.

JOHNSON, GEORGE CUNYNGHAME.—B. 1875; 2nd clk., treasy., St. Kitts, Apr., 1894; ag. extra rev. offr., May, 1895; 2nd clk. admistr.'s office, clk. of legis. coun., Mar., 1896; ag. acctnt. treasy., Jan., 1897; audit clk. and clk. legis. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1897; clk. to admistr., exec. and legis. couns., and clk. to bd. of health, Jan., 1901; asst. priv. sec. (unpaid) to H.E. Chas. T. Cox, C.M.G., ag. gov., Leeward Is., 1903, 1904; ag. asst. treas. and controller of cust., offl. mem., legis. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis; chrmn., Basseterre poor-law bd., Apr., 1903, to Mar., 1904; mem. quarantine bd., 1903; is priv. sec. to admistr., lieut. in def. force, and a J.P. for St. Kitts-Nevis; compiled index to the laws of Leeward Is. and St. Kitts-Nevis, 1901.

JOHNSON, GEORGE WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1905), M.A.—B. 1857; scholar of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 8th wrangler and 3rd class classics, 1880; apptd., after a compet. exam., to be a clk. in the office of the sec. of state for the cols., Mar., 1881; sec. to Eastern currency comtee., 1893; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain, May, 1896; priv. sec. to Sir R. Meade, Sept., 1896; 1st class clk., Mar., 1897; priv. clk., 30th June, 1900; mem. of S. currency comtee., 1902-3; mem. of inter-departmental comtee. on cable-landing rights; joint sec. to Imp. Conf., 1907.
JOHNSON, HENRY STAFFORD BROWNLOW.—B. 1876; cadet, Sarawak, 1897; asst. resid., 1901; 2nd cls. res. in charge of Upper Rejang, 1903.
JOHNSON, JOHN TAYLOR CONNEL.—Ed. at Edin. Univ.; med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 4th Feb., 1898; sen. med. offr., 1910.

JOHNSTON, ALEXANDER.—B. 1867; ed. at pub. schls., and St. Francis Xavier Coll., Antigonish, Nova Scotia; entd. local legis. of Nova Scotia, April, 1897, as repres. for Cape Breton Co., Nova Scotia; resig. in 1900 and entd. fed. parlmt. in same year and sat continuously until 1908; dep. min. marine and fisheries of Canada, June, 1910.

JOHNSTON, HARRY FREDERICK.—B. 1853; contract survr., W. Australia, Jan., 1874; ag. staff survr., Nov., 1883; confirmed, Feb., 1885; surveyed and triangulated Kimberley dist., and discovered Elvire and Mary rivers, where goldfields have been found; in charge of constrn. of telegraph, Roebourne to Derby, 1886-9; survr.-gen., July, 1896.

JOHNSTON, R. FLEMING.—B. 1874; M.A., Magdalen Coll., Oxford (1901); Gray prizeman, Edin. Univ., 1894; prov. acc. Lord Rector's essay; lst cl. certa. Eng. lit., mod. history and constitutional law, Edin.; exhibitioner, Magdalen Coll., 1894; mentioned hon. causa Stanhope essay, 1895; B.A. (hons.), 1895; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; ag. clk. of councils, 1899; priv. sec. to H.E. the administrator; ag. astt. col. sec., 1900; priv. sec. to the governor, 1900-1901; passed in Chinese, Oct., 1901; ag. astt. col. sec. and clk. of councils, 1901-1904; ag. sec. to gov't., Weihiwei, 1904; dist. offr. and mag., ditto, 1906.

JOHNSTON, ROBERT MACKENZIE, I.S.O.(1903), F.S.S.(1879).—Regisr.-gen.and govt. statistician of Tasmania; ed. Andersonian Univ., Glasgow; apptd. to organise and superintend the working of the acctnt.'s and traffic audit branch of the first rly. opened in Tasmania (1870); transfd. to service of Tasmanian Gov't. (1872); ch. clk. in auditor-gen.'s office (1880); apptd. to reorganise and conduct the then combined offices of regisr.-gen. and govt. statistician (1882); roy. commr. to inquire and report on the fisheries of the col. of Tasmania (1882); apptd. salmon fisheries commr. (1882); mem. of the fisheries bd. (1889); mem. of technical educn. bd. (1888-90); chmn. of boundaries bd. of advice (1890); author of "Systematic Account of the Geology of Tasmania" and other scientific wks.; fellow of the Roy. Statis. Soc. of Lond.; mem. of the coun. of the Roy. Soc. of Tasmania and of the Australasian Assoc. for Advent. of Science; fellow of the Roy. Geog. Soc. of Australasia.

JOHNSTONE, CHARLES EDWARD.—Ed. Cheltenham and Westward Ho colleges; lieut., 4th Batt. P.A. Somerset L.I., 1885-1887; astt. supt. of pol., Madras presidency, 1887-88; served in 1st Batt. Durham L.I. (68th), 1889-1895; astt. inspr., Lagos Hausa force, 1896; on spec. serv. to G. Coast Hinterland, 1897 (Samoury expdn., W. Africa, medal and clasp); priv. sec. to Sir G. C. Denton, Lt.-gov. of Lagos, 1900; comsnr. of police, Lagos, 1900; inspr.-gen. of pol., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906.

JOHNSTONE, ROBERT.—Ed. Edinburgh; temporary clk., audit office, Jamaica, 1878; 3rd class clk., col. sec.'s office, 1879; sec. to bd. of supervision, poor relief, Sept., 1886; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s off., Jan., 1902; senr. clk., Jan., 1905; ag. astt. col. sec., 1905 and 1906; astt. col. sec., Dec., 1906; ag. col. sec., May, 1907, six mons. in 1908, and three mons. in 1909; was also sec. schools commn. from 1883 to 1902; and astt. govt. meteorologist from 1880 to 1899; sec. to commn. to inquire into customs defalcations, 1889; conduct of two public offrs., 1890; Milk River baths, 1892; ed. in Jamaica, 1898; mem. and man. dir. of local bd. of Mans. Titchfield (school) trust prov. 1899 to 1902; apptd. to be one of three human loan officers, in 1903.

JOHNSTONE, ROBERT STEWART.—Ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (honors and medal in hist. and political science), 1882; LL.B., 1884; V.L., 1895; Holt scholar, Gray's Inn, 1885; called to the Irish bar 1886; called to the bar at Gray's Inn, Jan., 1889; lieut., 3rd (Militia) Batt. Chester Regt., 1883; capt., 1887; dist. exec. Lagos, 1889; served on Lagos and Porto Novo boundary comm., 1890; stip. and circuit mag. judge of ct. of com. pleas, Bahamas, Jan., 1891; ag. atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1902; ch. m. Grenada, 1909.
JOHNSTONE, HON. THOMAS C.—B. 1853; ed. pub. and high-schls., Simcoe and Brantford, and Toronto law schl.; called to the bar, Ont., 1876; judge, N.W.T., 1906; judge, supreme cts., Sask., 1907.
JOLLIVET, ARTHUR B. YVES.—L.R.C.I. (Glas.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.); health offr., Mauritius, 20th Mar., 1885; med. supt., Barkly asylum, Nov., 1885.

JONES, CHARLES HARRISON.—B. 1884; Reading and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1907; pol. nat. Puttalam, Jan., 1909; astt. govt. agt., Colombo and Negombo dists., July, 1910.
JONES, CHARLES JEROME, I.S.O., B.A.—1847; ent. civ. serv., Canada, 1875; apptd. clk., 1883; astt. govt.-gen.'s sec., 1909.

JONES, EDWARD RUTTER.—Acted adms. customs, Antigua, 1875 to 1880; apptd. astt. treas., Antigua, Oct., 1880; landed adms. customs, Antigua, 1881; acted 1st clk., audt. Leeward Is., 1887 and 1892; apptd. 1st indor. of treas., Antigua, 1895; ag. treas., Antigua, Sep., 1899, Oct., 1900, July, 1901, May, 1902, and Nov. to Dec., 1903; astt. treas. and compt. of cust., Kitts-Nevis, Mar., 1904; offr. M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1904; chmn., P.L. board and quarantine board, J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, treas., St. Kitts-Nevis, Apr., 1906.
JONES, G. A.—B. 1889; apptd., after comp. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., assigned to the C.O., Dec., 1907.

JONES, GWILYMD ARTHUR.—Agric. diploma Univ. Coll., Bangor, N. Wales, with 1st cls. agric.; awarded Wm. Griffith prize, 1909; holder of certif. in forestry; ag. agric. and science master, Antigua mag. schl., 1909; astt. curan. botanic gardens, Dominica, Aug., 1909; in charg. of agric. schl., Dominica, Aug., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. astt. sec. to permanent exhibtn. comtee, 1910.

JONES, JAMES WM.—B. 1843; entd. S. A.S.; pub. ser. as land survr., survr.-gen.'s dept., 1865; chief survr. in dept., 1877; dep. survr.-gen., 1881; conservator of water, 1883; sec. for wks., and chmn. of supply and tender bd., 1885.
JONES, JOSEPH.—Trained at Kew Gard. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, 1892; sec. to permanent exhibtn. comtee.; sec. to agric. experiments comtee.
JONES, LLEWELLYN, A. F.—Ast. dir. comnsmr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908.

JONES, PATRICK N. H., A.M.I.C.E.—King's Coll., Lond., applied sciences; ed. and astt., Edward Easton & Co., C.E., 1889; res. engrnr., Barbados Water Sup. Co., 1889 to 1890; ag. D.P.W., Barbados, Sept., 1894; thanked by legis. counc. for services drafting waterworks bill, 1894; apptd. engrnr. charge of waterworks surveys, Trinidad, 1895; ree. engrnr. of water and sewerage works, Feb., Nov., 1898; and Jan. to Oct., 1900; in charge of construction of water and sewerage works, 1900 to 1903; mem. of comttee. on tech. educn. in the colony, 1902; astt. D.P.W., Hong Kong, 3rd April, 1903; ag. D.P.W. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1904-5, and 1909; dir. of pub. wks., Gold Coast, 1910.

JONES, SAMUEL SALTUS.—B. 1857; asst. bookkeeper, Br. Guiana, Oct., 1882; 4th clk., rec. gen.'s office, Jan., 1884; 3rd clk., May, 1884; 1st class clk., Apr., 1888; cashier, July, 1888; ch. clk., audit dept., May, 1906; ag. audr.-gen., 11th Aug. to 25th Oct., 1906; ag. recvr.-gen., 13th May to 7th June, 1907.

JONES, S. T.—Ed. Wes. high schl., S. Leone; spec. prize for "marked proficiency" in studies, 1895; R. coll. of preceptors and Pitman's shorthand certificates; appointed 6th cls. clk., P.O., Niger Coast Prot., 1898; 3rd cls. clk., high comsrr.'s off., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., 1902; Aro medal and clasp, 1902; 1st cls. clk., secretariat, 1903; sen. clk., secretariat, S. Nigeria, 1904, chief clk., ditto, 1905.

JONES, SIR WM. HENRY HYNDMAN, KT. BACH. (1906).—Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; LL.B., Cantab.; b. 1847; ag. sen. pol. mag., Barbados, 1880; comsrr. to inquire into working of pol. force, 1880; ag. judge of asst. ct. of appeal, Jan., 1881; mag., 1st dist., St. Lucia, Mar., 1881; M.L.C., ditto, Oct., 1881; deleg. to W. Indian telegraph confce., May, 1882; ag. ch. just., St. Lucia and Tobago, Jan., 1883; ag. atty.-gen., and mem. of exec. coun., Feb., 1883; ag. ch. just. of St. Lucia and Tobago; mem. of Windwards ct. of appeal, Sept., 1886; mag., and mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Grenada, Mar., 1887; res. mag. for different districts of Jamaica, April, 1888, to Feb., 1893; ag. puisne judge, Jamaica, May, 1893, to June, 1895; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1895; supernumerary res. mag., Jamaica, May, 1896; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., 3rd Dec., 1896; ag. jud. comsrr., in addition to other duties, 25th Mar., 1903, to 3rd Feb., 1904; jud. comsrr., 16th June, 1904; ch. jud. comsrr., Ist. Jan., 1906; ch. just., S. Sttlmts., 22nd Aug., 1906.

JONES, SIR W. H. QUAYLE, KT. BACH. (1892).—B. 1854; temporary puisne judge, G. Coast Col., 1882 and 1883; mem. of Lond. exec. and chmn. of local comttee, for the Col. and Indian Exhibn., 1886; Queen's advoc., 1883; ch. just., W. Africa Sttlmts., 1887; ret. 1895; chmn. of sessions.

JORDAN, GREGORY PAUL.—M.B. and C.M. (Edin.), 1880; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1884; F.R.S. Med.; F.R.I. Pub. Health; late asst., eye dept., Edin. Royal Infirmy.; astt. to lect. on diseases of the eye, Edin. Univ.; late asst., Maternity hosp., Edin.; house surg., Dorchester county hosp., 1883; ag. res. accoucheur, St. Thomas's hosp., Lond., 1884; late clin. astt., hosp. for women, Soho Square, 1902; ag. col. surg., Hong Kong, 1886; J.P.; health offr. of the port, Hong Kong, 1888.
JORGENSEN, C. R. E.—Capt. 4th Batt. Roy. Irish Regt.; ed. at Uppingham; served in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (two medals with five clasps); astt. polit. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1905.
JOSCELNYE, R. H.—B. 1852; transferred from Royal Niger Company's service to C.O. as supplementary clerk, Jan., 1900.

JOSEPH, GERARD ABRAHAM.—B. 1870; priv. sec. to Mr. Just, Clarence, 1891; in charge of Colombo museum, during director's absence, 24th Mar. to 29th Apr., 1892; librarian, Colombo museum, 22nd Oct., 1893; ag. 3rd astt., P.M.G., 1st to 15th Sept., 1898; ag. director, Colombo museum, 2nd to 10th July, 1900; in charge of Colombo museum, 1st Mar. to 3rd Apr., 1901; in charge of Colombo museum, 30th Mar., 1906; ag. director, Colombo museum, 30th June, 1906, to 13th Feb., 1907; acted as librarian of museum on two occasions prior to being confirmed; hon. sec. of Ceylon branch of Royal Asiatic Society since 1893; also hon. treas. since 1907.

JOYNT, HENRY NOBLE HOLTON.—B. 1864; ed. at Portora Roy. schl. and Univ. of Dub.; prizeman, royal schl., 1st honour-man, 1st senr. modr. and gold medallist in nat. sc.; B.A., M.A., M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O., diploma in state med.; L.M., Rotunda hosp.; asst. med. offr., fever hosp., Dub.; sen. asst. med. hosp., City hosp., Birmingham; supt., City Hosp., Bradford; govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1892; dist. med. offr., Labasa, 1892; dist. med. offr. and port offr., Lautoka, 1905.

JULL, MARTIN EDWARD.—Ed. Brighton Gram. schl.; articled 3 yrs. and afterwards asst. survr. with a firm of survrs. of city of London; clk. P.W.D., W. Australia, Jan., 1887; ch. clk., Jan., 1892; under sec. P.W., July, 1895; pub. ser. comsrr., Apr., 1905.

JUST, ALBERT WOLFGANG, B.A. (1891).—Jun. officer, State of Perak, July, 1892; passed final exam. in Malay, Aug., 1893; astt. to dist. mag. and collr. of land rev., Krian, Jan., 1894; mag., Seremban, Jan., 1903.

JUST, HARTMANN WOLFGANG, C.B. (1902), C.M.G. (1900), B.A. (1877).—B. 1854; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in office of sec. of state for the col., 3rd June, 1878; ed. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; was an open scholar on the foundation; 1st class in classical mods., 1874; Taylorian exhib. for German, 1876; 2nd class in final classical school, 1877; astt. priv. sec. to the late Earl of Derby, 6th Feb., 1883, to Col. F. A. Stanley (now Earl of Derby), 24th June, 1885, and to Mr. Stanhope, 3rd Aug., 1886; priv. sec. to Mr. Osborne Morgan, 6th Feb. to 3rd Aug., 1886; astt. priv. sec. to Sir H. Holland (now Viscount Knutsford), 12th Jan., 1887, and to the Marquess of Ripon, 17th Aug., 1892; astt. sec. to the col. confce., 1887; 1st class clk., Apr., 1895; prin. clk., Mar., 1897; accompanied Rt. Hon. Joseph Chamberlain on his visit to S. Africa, 1902-3; astt. under-sec. of state, Jan., 1907; jt. sec. to Imp. Confce., 1907; sec. to Imp. Confce. on naval and mil defence, 1909; visited Canada, 1910; permanent sec. to Imp. Confce.; represents C.O. on advisory comttee. of comm. intell., B. of T.
KAINE, HON. JOHN CHARLES.—B. 1854; ed. Comml. Acad., Quebec; elec. mem. of exec. coun. for Quebec West, 1904; re-elec., 1908; min. without portfolio, 8th Jan., 1906.

KANTHACK, FRANCIS EDGAR, A.M.I.C.E.—Graduated at the Associate of Roy. Indian engnr. coll., Cooper's Hill, 1890-94; apptd. to Indian pub. wks. dept., Oct., 1894; served as astt. and exec. engr., Punjab irrigtn. branch, P.W.D., 1895 to Oct., 1906; apptd. (under agreement) dir. of irrigtn., Cape Colony, Oct., 1906, and "director" in terms of sec. 3 (1) of Act No. 32 of 1906, to advise and assist the comsrr. of pub. wks. in matters relating to irrigtn.; resignd, Indian serv. and joined Cape civ. serv., Oct., 1908; is a mem. of the geological comn.

KAYE, A. B. R.—Lieut. 3rd batt., Roy. Ir. Fus., 1879; capt., 1887; hon. major, 1894, and local lieut.-col. in the army, Feb., 1903; served during India rebellion, N.W. Territory of Canada, 1885, with Boulton's M.I., was present at the Fish Creek engagement and the taking of Batoche (medal and clasp); sub-insp. of constab., Br. Hond., 1887; ag. dist. mag., Orange Walk, Br. Hond., 1889; J.P., 1889; inspr., 1889; ag. inspr. comdt., Br. Hond. constab., and ex-officio mem. of exec. and leg. couns., Aug., 1889, to May, 1890; again ag. dist. mag., Orange Walk, Nov., 1890, to Apr., 1892; recd. gratuity and thanks of govt. for serv. during small-pox epidemic, 1891-2; ag. inspr. comdt., Apr., 1892; inspr. comdt. and mem. exec. coun., Aug., 1892; inspr. of pol. and supt. of fire brig., Barbados, Feb., 1899; inspr.-gen. of pol., Aug., 1901.
KEAN, HON. ABRAHAM.—M.H.A., Bonavista, Newfield., 1885-89, Bay de Verde, 1897; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1897; ag. min. agricul. and mines, 1898.

KEATING, HON. JOHN HENRY.—B., 1872; called to the bar, Tasmania, 1894; senator for Tasmania, C. of Aust., 1901; min. without portfolio, 5th July, 1905; vice-pres. of exec. coun., 13th Oct., 1906; min. for home affairs, Jan., 1907.

KEEBLE, JOHN BRIGHT.—B., 1879; ed. Queen Elizabeth's gram. schl.; 1st clk., sec.'s off., B. Cent. Africa Prot., Feb., 1902; ag. asst. sec., Apr. to Aug., 1903; asst. sec. to admstrn., Aug., 1903; ag. sec., Apr. to Nov., 1906; ag. supt. of native affairs, June, 1907 to Jan., 1908; asst. sec., govt. sec.'s office, Nyasaland Prot., Oct., 1907; ag. chief asst. sec., Apr. to Nov., 1909; 1st asst. sec., Apr., 1910.
KEEFER, THOMAS COLTRIN, C.M.G. (1878), C.E.—B., 1821; engnr. of several rlyws. and other wks. in Canada; was exec. comsrr. for the Dominion of Canada at the Paris Exhibn., 1878.
KEILICH, ERANUS J.—Taxidermist, state museum, Perak, 21st April, 1897; ag. curator and state geologist, Mar., 1902; ag. curator, Mar., 1906.

KEITH, A. BERRIEADLE.—B., 1879; ed. at Royal High Schl. and Univ., Edinburgh; 7 class medals, Van Dunlop scholarship in classics, 1895; M.A., 1st class hon. classics, 1897; Furguson schl., Glasgow, 1897; Guthrie Fellowship in Class. Lit., 1899; open scholarship, Balliol Coll., Oxford, 1895; Boden scholarship, 1898; 1st class hon. moderations, 1899; 1st class Lit. Ind. (Sanskrit and Pali), 1900; 1st class Lit. Hum., 1901; B.A., 1900; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 21st Oct., 1901; sec. to crown agts., July, 1903; called to the bar (1st cls. honours) 1904; B.C.L., 1905; re-transf., to C.O., 1st May, 1905; D.C.L., thesis approved, 1907; one of Brit. del. at coll. merch. shipping confce., 1907; dep. Boden Prof. Sanskrit, 1907-8; clk., Impl. Confce., 1909; joint sec., Impl. Copyright confce., 1910; junr. asst. sec. to impl. Confce., Oct., 1910; sec., trop. diseases research fund advisory bd.; and sec. man. comtee. of sleeping sickness bureau.
KRITH, ROBERT DONALD.—M.A., M.D., Aberdeen; physiologist, etc., S. Sttlnts., Mar., 1906; ag. govt. pathologist, Aug., 1906, to Mar., 1907; principal, med. schl., Feb., 1909.

KELLY, C. C.—Supernum. marshal, Essequibo, Dec., 1873; ag. clk., Suddie treasy., Oct., 1883; ordinary marshal, provost marshal's office, Georgetown, June, 1884; mag.'s clk., Essequibo, Aug., 1887; ag. oh. clk., pol. mag.'s office, Georgetown, June, 1893.

KELLY, SIR HENRY GREENE, KT. BACH. (1906).—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; honoran. and prizeman in hist. and political science; B.A. and LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dub., Mich. Term, 1884; apptd. a puisne judge in the Niger Territories, 1891; acted on several occasions as chief justice; chief justice, 1899; chief judge of Southern Nigeria, 1900; ret., 10th Febr., 1908.

KELLY, PERCY JAMES.—B.1876; M.B., Ch.B., Glasgow, 1906; house surg., W. Kent gen. hosp., Maidstone, 1907-8; certif. Lond. sci. trop. med., 1908; apptd. to W. African med. serv., G. Coast, 1908; transf., to Hong Kong med. serv., 1910; med. offr. to Victoria and visiting med. offr. to Tung Wa hospital; employed by govt. of Bengal on famine duty, 19—

KELSICK, OSCAR RICHARDSON.—Ag. cash savings bank, Antigua, 21st Aug., 1906; appr. as ditto, 16th Jan., 1908; ag. acctnt., sav. bank, 16th Jan., 1908; apptd. as ditto, 1st Sep., 1908; ag. acctnt., treasy., 1st May to 4th Aug., 1909; junr. audit clk., 12th June, 1909; ag. govt. offr. (acctnt.), treasy., Dominica, 16th Apr. to 31st Aug., 1910.
KELSON, JACOB WALTER.—Clerk, surv. dept., G. Coast, 1885; ch. warder and med. dresser, lun. asyl., Accra, 1887.

KEMP, JOSEPH HORSFORD.—B., 1874; RA Cape Univ.; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; land off., New Territory, 1899; registr., land court, May 1900; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1900 to 1904; ag. pol. mag., Sept.-Oct., 1904; asst. sec., sanitary bd., 1904; deputy-registr. and appraiser, sup. c., 1904; ag. registr., sup. c., offl. admstr. of trustee, and registr. of companies, Mar. to Nov., 1907; ag. offl. recvr. in bank., Apr., 1907, to Apr., 1908; ag. 1st pol. mag., Apr., 1908 to Feb., 1909; head of san. dept., Nov., 1908; registr. of companies, 1909.

KEMPE, JOHN ARROW, C.B. (1900), B.A.—B., 1846; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Trin. Col., Camb.; clk., treasy., 1867; priv. sec. to chancellor, excheqr., 1874-80, to Sir R. Lingen, 1880-81; Lord F. Cavendish, 1881; mem. of comsrs. agric. and dairy schls., 1887-88; prin. clk., treasy., 1888; dep. chmn., bd. of cust., 1894; mem. of royal comsrs. on elec. communication with lighthouses and light vessels, 1892-97; asst. comptir. c., auditor, 1904; comptir. and auditor-gen., 1906; hon. auditor of certain crown colonies and protectorates, ag. on behalf of S. of S. for the cols.

KEMPE, WILLIAM ALFRED.—Ed. at Haileybury Coll. and Trinity Coll., Camb.; B.A. (honours), 1903; col. audit branch of excheqr. and audit dept., 10th Oct., 1904; temp. ass. auditor, E. Africa Prot., 2nd Oct., 1905; ass. auditor, 1st Sept., 1906.
KEMPSFORD, GEORGE HENRY.—B., 1870; apptd., after exam., 3rd cls. messenger, C.O., 22nd Feb., 1904; 2nd cls., 1907.

KENNAN, THOMAS PONSONBY K.—Served F.A.M.P. and C.M.R., 1877 to 1882, during Griqu and Basuto wars (medal); temporary clk. to R.M. Leibie, Basutoland, 1882; sub-inspr., Basuto po., 1884; inspr., 1886; asst. comsrr., Quthing, 1886.

KENNAWAY, SIR WALTER, KT. BACH. (1906), C.M.G. (1891).—Mem. prov. coun., Canterbury, N.Z., 1868-74; prov. sec. and sec. pub. wrks., Cant., 1870-74; mem. bd. of educn., also bd. of govs., Cant. Coll.: 1873-4; sec., N.Z. govt. offr., London, 1874 to 1909, when he retired; comsrr. Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; apptd. offr. of French Acad. for services at Paris Exhibn., 1889; ag. agt.-gen., 1891-1896; stock and ban. agt. for N.Z. govt.; comsrr. under N.Z. Pub Rev. Acts.; custod. Pub. Trust Securities, London.

KENNY, WILLIAM ETRE.—A.M.I.C.E.; jun., 1867; jun. asst. engr., Cook county, N. Zealand, Jan., 1883; 2nd asst. engr., Gisborne har. bd., N. Zealand, July, 1885; ch. insp., Nov., 1887; ag. ch. asst. to col. marine engr., N.Z., Dec., 1890; offr. in charge, special survey dept., Sarawak, Sept., 1891; consulting engr. to Sarawak govt., Nov., 1892; in addition, agr. for water wks., Jan., 1893; asst. supt. of ks., P.W., S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1895; supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, Oct., 1897; ag. supt. of ks. and surveys, Singapore, June, 1898, to Feb., 1901; ag. dep. col. engrnr. on three occasions; ag. state engrnr., Selangor, Apr., 1903; exec. engrnr., 2nd grade, Selangor, Sept., 1903; ditto, 1st grade, Apr., 1905; ag. state engrnr., Selangor, Apr., 1903, to Sept., 1905; ditto, Pahang, May, 1907.
KENT, HON. JAS. M.—K.C., B.A.; M.H.A.; St. John's East, Newfoundland, 1904; mem. of exec. coun. and min. of just., 1907.
KENT, R. G.—Acctnt., rly. dept., Victoria, 1891.

KENYON-SLANEY, NEVILLE AGLIONBY.—Ed. at Haileybury Coll.; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 8th Oct., 1903; attached to secretariat, Mar., 1904; 3rd asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1906; dist. comsrr., 8th Jan., 1908.

KER, J. ERRINGTON.—M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); house surg., gen. hosp., Birmingham; house surg., homoeopathic hosp., Birmingham; asst. surg., col. hosp., Gibraltar, 1889; port surg., 1892; pol. surg., 1889; dist. surg., 1889; P.O. surg., 1902; public vaccinator, 1889; suptgd. med. offr., Jamaica, Oct., 1904; is also chmn. of quaran. dist. bd. and central bd. of health; mem. of bd. of supervision; nom. M.L.C., 1904.

KERNANAN, JAMES B.—B.B.1866; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trinidad, 1876 to 1883; supery. survey office, Trinidad, 1885 to 1886; acted asst. draughtsman, O. Trinidad, 1886; passed survey exam., Univ. Coll., London, 1886 to 1887; priv. survr., Grenada, 1888 to 1890; engaged by St. Vincent govt. on surveys, June, 1890, to Dec., 1894; crown and col. survr., 23rd June, 1890; staff survr., Jan., 1895; apptd. to carry out duties assigned to col. engrnr. under provisions of road ordce, of 1895, Jan., 1896; staff survr., supt., crown lands, and comnrr., Feb., 1899; dir. of surveys, still holding other offices above, Aug., 1899; J.P., Dec., 1901; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Aug., 1902; commended by gov. for relief work after Soufriere eruption, 1902.
KERR, C. M. MACLEOD.—3rd class clk., contab. office, Jamaica, Feb., 1868; 2nd class, Aug., 1869; 1st class, Oct., 1873; ch. clk., July, 1879.

KERR, HON. JAMES KIRKPATRICK, K.C.—3. 1841; ed. Hamilton and Gault gram. schls., called to the bar, 1862; Q.C. (Ontario), 1881, (Canada), 1896; elected a bencher of the Law Soc. of Ontario, 1879; contested centre Toronto in gen. elec., 1891; called to the senate, 1903; speaker of senate, 1909.

KERR, LOUIS L.—Clk. imigr. dept. Br. Guiana, Sept., 1887; clk. pol. dept., Jan., 1888; ag. insp. pol., 1891-2; ag. paymr., 1892; dist. insp. and P., 1893; in charge Uruan frontier station, June to Oct., 1895; county inspnr. of pol., 1897; ag. ep. inspnr. gen. of pol., Sept., 1899; ag. chief county inspnr., May, 1900; offr. visitor, Onderneering schl., Mar., 1905; chief county inspnr. of pol., Oct., 1908; supt., Georgetown fire brigade, Feb., 1909; ag. inspnr.-gen. of pol. and inspnr. of prisons, Mar., 1909; dep. inspnr.-gen. of pol., July, 1909.

KERSHAW, Lieut.-Col. ARTHUR EDWIN.—B. 1852; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov. of B. Hond., 1884; inspr. of constab., B. Hond., 1886; dist. mag., 1887; comdt. and ex-officio mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1888; recd. thanks of gov. for preparing itinerary of the roads of the colony, 1892; chief comdt. of mil. police, and inspr. of prisons, Cyprus, 1892; commanded col. cavalry at Queen Victoria's Aldershot Review, Diamond Jubilee; holds Jubilee and Coronation medals; inspr.-gen. of police and prisons, Jamaica, 1905.

KERSHAW, THOS. HERBERT, C.M.G. (1903).—B.A.; Trin. Coll., Oxon.; 2nd class mod. classics, 1872; 2nd class finals, 1874; Inner Temple equity scholar, 1876; called to the bar, Apr., 1877; registr. of deeds, Singapore, June, 1887; official assignee, Nov., 1890; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1894; legal adviser, Federated Malay States, June, 1896; ag. atty.-gen., S.S., March, 1901, to March, 1902; ret., 31st Dec., 1902.

KEUN, ALFRED HAVELOCK, M.B., B.Ch. (Edin.).—B. 1874; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 1st Apr., 1900; super. col. surg., S. Sttlmts., 17th Nov., 1900; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley South, 1st Mar., 1901; res. med. offr., dist. hosp., Penang, Oct., 1908.
KEYES, PERLEY GEORGE.—B. 1853; entd. civ. serv., Canada, as jun. 2nd cl. clk., 1878; promoted to 2nd cls., 1886; 1st cls., 1887; ch. clk. and sec. of dep. of the interior, 1900.

KEYT, FREDERIC THEOBALD.—B. 1866; ed. Aberdeen Univ., M.D. (honours), D.P.H., 1902; M.B., C.M., 1888; mem. Br. med. assoc.; asst. col. surg., Br. Hond., 1892; ag. inspnr. of schls., Oct., 1892 to June, 1893; dist. comnrr. and J.P., 1893 to 1902; port health offr., Hong Kong, 1902.
KIDD, HON. JOHN.—Mem. legis. assm., N.S. Wales; postmr.-gen. in Dibbs' ministry, 1891-4; min. for mines and agric., 1901-4; has retired from politics.

KIDSTON, HON. WM.—M.L.A. for Rockhampton, Queensland, since 1896; treas. and postmr.-gen. in Dawson govt., 1899; vice-pres. of exec. coun., ch. sec. and treas., Sept., 1903; premier of Queensland, Jan., 1906.
KILLINGBECK, J. J.—Asst. postmr.-gen., E.A.P., 1910.

KILPIN, Sir ERNEST FULLER, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; clk. of house of assem., Cape; clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Cape, June, 1876; priv. sec. to Hon. J. Gordon Sprigg, premier and col. sec., Feb., 1878, to June, 1880; clk. assist. of the house of assem., June, 1880; clk., 16th July, 1897; has served as sec. on the following Cape govt. comsrs.: Dorthea, 1877; war expenditure, 1881; Liesbeek municipality, 1883; diamond laws, 1887; liquor laws, 1889; lighthouses, 1890; fisheries, 1892; sech, 1893; defence, 1896; and acted as priv. sec. to premier, the Hon. Sir T. C. Scanlen, Feb. and Mar., 1883; sec. to the Anglo-German comsn. on Angra Pequena and W. Coast claims, 1886; author of parly. agents' manual, Cape, 1902, 2nd edtn., 1906; of S. African Union and private bill legislation, 1908, and editor of civil service list; prepared standing rules and orders for the legis. coun., Rhodesia, 1898; has frequently been consulted on parly. procedure in South Africa; at the invitation of the O.R.C. govt., assisted in the inauguration of parly. institutions there in Dec., 1907; is examiner for shorthand under civ. ser. comsrs.; is a J.P. for the col., chief sec., S. African National Convention, 1908.
KINDER, F. T.—Asst. engrnr., Singapore rlyw., Mar., 1900; asst. supt. of wks., S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1903; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, Mar., 1908.

KINDERSLEY, WILLIAM LORING.—B. 1868; ed. Marlborough Schl. and Exeter Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Jan., 1893; priv. sec. to lieut.-govr., Sept., 1893; off. asst. to gvt. agt., Uva Prov., Oct., 1895; addl. pol. mag., Matara, Dec., 1895; pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1897; dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1903; asst. gvt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1904; Chilaw, Nov., 1905; Puttalam, Apr., 1906; Matara, Mar., 1907.

KING, CHARLES KEARLS BERRY.—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll.; matric. Lond. Univ., 1855; 1st clk. and 2nd sub-imigrn. agt., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1871; 2nd sub-imigrn. agt., June, 1872; sen. ditto, Apr., 1881; acted as imigrn. agt.-gen., 1881-2, 1888-9, 1892-3-4-7; mem. of ct. of policy on last three occasions; also mem. poor law bd., and ag. chmn., 1889 and 1894; res., 1896. In 1895, one of the comsrs. to report on the best scheme for commutation by E. Indian imigrnts. of the right of return passages to India; and in 1896, senior mem. of special comitee, to deal with allotment of ld. to E. I. imigrnts. in lieu of return passage.

KING, F. EUSTACE.—Clt. in col. sec's office, Barbados, from May, 1866; transf'd to gvt. sec's office, Br. Guiana, in June, 1868; insptr. of pol. and just. of peace, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1871; comsry. of taxation, 1873; emigrn. agt. at Barbados for Br. Guiana, 1875; dist. comsry. of taxation, 1884; treasv. departmental inspr., Mar., 1896.

KING, GEORGE HOWARD.—Ag. clk., treasv., St. Kitts, 1894; ag. rev. offr., Nevis, 1895; clk., P.O., St. Kitts, 1895; ag. clk. regisr.'s office, Nevis, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, Jan., 1898; govt. offr., St. Kitts, 1899; rev. offr., Nevis, June, 1905; 1st clk., P.O., and sub-inspr. of schls., St. Kitts, 1906.

KING, HENRY JOSEPH.—Ed. at Ampleforth Coll., Oswaldkirk, Yorks.; admitted a solr. of sup. ct. (England), 6th April, 1905; admitted and enrolled in sup. ct. of Gibraltar, 17th May, 1905; offl. trustee in bankruptcy, Gibraltar, 27th Oct., 1909.
KING, HENRY SANDFORD.—Survr., temporary staff, W. Aust., 1884; staff survr., 1887; inspr. of mining surveys, Coolgardie goldfields, 1894; undersec. for mines, 1899.

KING, JOHN CHARLES.—B. 1848; ed. privately; articled clk. to atty.-gen., Gibraltar, Feb., 1866; transf'd to col. sec's office, June, 1870; 2nd clk., Nov., 1877; sec. to coms. to inquire into capture of two British subjects by Spanish brigands, 1877; assisted in audit duties, 1875-83; ch. clk. and sec. to the bd. of health, 1883; asst. col. sec., 1891; has acted as col. sec. on various occasions.

KING, JOHN HAMPDEN.—Entd. secretariat, Br. Guiana, 1880; priv. sec. to lieut.-govr., 1887-88; ag. supt. reformatory schl., 1894 and 1895; ch. clk. secretariat, 1895; ag. supt. penal sttlmt., 1898; ag. inpr. of prisons, 1898, 1899 and 1900; asst. gvt. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., ct. of policy and combined ct., Jan., 1900; ag. auditor-gen., 1900, 1902, 1903, 1904 and 1906; ag. gvt. sec. in 1906 and 1907; J.P. for colony, 1904; served on the following comsrs.: manufacture and storage of bitters and cordials (chmn.), adminstr. of the funds of the De Safton Trust, supplies for the lunatic asylum (sole comsnr.), reformy. and indust. schl., 1905.

KING, JOSEPH ARTHUR.—Copyist, regisr.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1878; ag. 3rd asst. sworn clk., Mar., 1882; 4th asst. sworn clk., Oct., 2nd ditto, July, 1883; passed exam. for sworn clk. and notary public, June, 1885.
KING, JOSEPH CHARLES.—Ed. Mt. St. V. Coll., Derbyshire; supernum. clk. imigrn. Br. Guiana, Jan., 1888; 5th cl. clk., Mar., 4th class clk., Jan., 1894; 3rd cl. clk., 1895.
KING, JOSEPH LEEMAN.—European gov't. English schl., S. Sttlmts., 4th Oct.; asst. treasr., Singapore, 17th Apr., 1899.

KING, M.—Asst. comsry., Limasol, (Nov., 1878; registr., high ct., Mar., 1879; comsry., Paphos dist., Mar., 1881; loc. mil. pol. and asst. comsry., Limasol, Mar.; comsry. and sheriff, Nicosia dist., Apr.; pres. of municipal and water comsrs. of Nicosia, in addition to other duties, 1884-9, and mem. legis. coun., 1893; Br. deleg. of Exec. ag. rec-gen. and mem. exec. coun., May to Aug., 1895; June, 1896, to Feb., 1897; sec. to W. Pacific comsny., 1897; British res. comsny., Hebrides, 1907.

KING, NAPLETON WALTER.—B. 1876; asst. surg-gen.'s office, Br. Guiana, Apr., 6th cl. clk., off. of insp. of prsns., 1896; 5th cl. June, 1897; ag. clk. of supplies, Mar. to Jan., 1903; ditto, June, 1903, to Apr., 4th cl. offr. of cust., Jan., 1906; ag. sub-ct. and harbour., Port of Springlands, Sept., 1906.

KING, THOS. HENRY.—Ed. at Exeter Schl.; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1894; in Cantonese, 1907; ag. asst. supt. of pol. attached to Punjab pol., Mar., 1907; pol. Hindustani, Oct., 1907; ag. asst. supt., 1908; J.P., 1908; ag.-supt. of imports and exports, Dec., 1909; asst. emigrn. offr. in charge of pol. duties, Apr., 1910; passed in July, 1910.

KING, THOS. MULHALL, I.S.O.(1903).—Entered Queensland civ. ser., customs dep., clk., Sept., 1863; passed through various dept.; apptd. collr. of customs, sept., mem. of the imigrn. bd., Aug., 1884; un. of the treasy., Nov., 1893; aud.-gen., Sept., 1894.

KING, WILLIAM FREDERICK, C.M.G., LL.D., D.T.S., F.R.S.C., F.R.A.S.C.—B. 1862; sub-asst. H.M.'s North American border comsny., 1872; inspr. of surveys, Canada; chief inspr., 1886; chief astronomer, 1890; geodetic surv., 1909; boundary comsny. treaties 1903, 1906, and 1908.

KING, HON. WILLIAM LYON MACKENZIE (1906), M.A., Toronto Univ., LL.B., Toronto Ph.D., Harvard Univ.—B. 1874; apptd. min. of labour and editor of "Labour Gazette," 1902; regisr. of boards of conciliation and arbitration, 1907; sworn of the Privy Council, Canada and min. of labour, 1909.
KINGDON, DONALD, B.A., LL.B.—Ed. Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., called to the bar, In. Tem., July, 1906; legis. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Jan., 1907; exec. and legis. couns.

KING-HARMAN, SIR CHARLES ANTE K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1899)—B. 1831; Chelt. Coll.; B.A., of Trin. Coll., Camb.; M.A., 1879; priv. sec. to the gov. of Bal., Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; priv. sec. to comsny., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. comsny., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; asst. to the ch. sec., Cyprus, Mar., 1881; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. legis. couns., June to Sept., 1882; auditor-gen., Barbados, 1883; elected mem. of house of ass.,

KNAGGS, SAMUEL WILLIAM, C.M.G. (1908).—Entered Trinidad service, Aug., 1875; ch. clk. col. sec.'s office, July, 1892, and clk. of legs. coun., Jan., 1893; sec. to comm. to inquire into road system, 1886; ag. privy sec. to Gov. Sir W. Robinson, 1886, 1889 and 1890-91; sec. to bd. of educn. and coll. coun., 1889-91; priv. sec. to Sir F. N. Broome, Aug. to Sept., 1891, and to the gov. of Hong Kong, Oct., 1891, to July, 1892; ag. comsnr., Tobago, 1897; ag. audr.-gen., Trinidad, 1898; asst. col. sec., 1900; rec. gen. and M.L.C., 1901; ag. col. sec., 1902; ch. comsnr., Port of Spain, 1899 to 1903; col. sec., Barbados, 1903; ag. gov. in 1903, 1904, 1905 and 1906; col. sec., Trinidad, 1907; ag. gov., 1907, 1908 and 1909.

KIBBY, AUSTIN HENRY, B.A. (Cantab.).—1879; agric. and science master attached, on half of Imperial dept. of agric., to the Antigua unmr. schl., 1903; lecturer in agric. science to elementary schl. teachers and to students of mining coll., Antigua.
KIRK, SIR JOHN, M.D., G.C.M.G. (1886), C.B., K.C.M.G. (1881), C.M.G. (1879).—1832; formerly H.M.'s polit. agt. and consul. at Zanzibar.
KIRKPATRICK, HON. ANDREW ALEXANDER, N.L.C., S. Australia, 1891-97 and 1900; ch. sec. and min. of industry, 1905-1909; agt.-gen. London, 1909.
KIRKPATRICK, IVONE.—Cadet Sarawak civ., Limbang, Aug., 1892; extra offr., Simanggang, Jan., 1893; res. 2nd cls., Mar., 1898, of per Rejang, May, 1899; res. 2nd cls., Sadong, Nov., 1901; Kapit, 1905.
KIRKWOOD, RICHARD HUGH.—B. 1859; col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1888-91; 3rd clk., 1892; ch. clk. audit dept., 1892-1907; asst. editor, 1907; ag. col. auditor, 9th Aug., 1909.
KIRWAN, ARTHUR CONINGSBY.—Jun. clk., sec's off., Falkland Is., 1st Jan., 1907.

KITCHENER, LT.-COL. ADAM.—B. 1838; statistical Eastern sys. of rly., Cape Town, Feb., 1881; pres examiner, July, 1882; construction bookkpr., 1883; transf. to Western system, Dec., 1884; ad bookkpr., 1888; asst. acctnt., 1892; acctnt. of agricul., Nov., 1896; ch. acctnt., May, 1898; accntng. offr., May, 1899; served as lieut. th P.A.O.C. artily., in Bechuana land, 1897; prin. paymaster, col. def. force, Apr., 1901, th rank of major; lieut.-col., Oct., 1902; accntng. offr. in comsnr. of pub. wks. off., July, 1904; chief paymaster and accntng. offr., treasry., July, 1908.

KITCHENER, LIEUT.-GEN. FREDERICK ALTER, C.B. (1902).—B. 1858; served in Afghan war, 1878-80; with Dongola expedn., 1896; 4th cls. Medjidiie; in S. African war, 1899-1902; mmgd. 3rd (Lahore) divn., Northern command, India, 1902-8; lieut.-gen., 1906; gov. of Bermuda, 9th Nov., 1908.
KITTERMMASTER, H. B.—Asst. dist. comsnr., A.P., 18th Dec., 1903.

KNAGGS, HARRY LESLIE.—B. 1867; clk., in Fernando Hosp., Trinidad, Apr., 1885; 4th k. col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1889; 3rd clk., July, 1892; 2nd clk., Nov., 1900; 3rd insp. of immigrts., 1897, to Nov., 1900; ag. asst. col. sec., 1895, 1899, 1900, 1906 and 1907; ag. col. sec., 1st to 7th May, 1907; sec. to civ. ser. coms. and to rly. and roads coms., 1894; ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., 1st sec., Mar., 1908, to Mar., 1909.
KNIBBS, GEORGE HANDLEY, C.M.G. (1911), F.R.A.S.—B. 1858; Federal statistician for Australia, under the title of Commonwealth statistician, June, 1906.

KNIGHT, F. G.—Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1901; jun. clk. prothonotary's office, Barbados, 1874; 4th clk. correspondence branch col. sec.'s office, 1879; 2nd clk. audit office, 1882; ag. ch. clk. on several occasions, and as govt. acctnt. to Barbados rly. co.; ch. clk. record branch col. sec.'s office, 1892; ag. clk. asst. of appeal, 1894 and 1895; dep. registr., 1898; ag. pol. mag. on several occasions; ag. judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., 1906; ch. clk., auditor off., Feb., 1907; ag. auditor-gen., July, 1908, to Jan., 1909, and May to Nov., 1909.

KNIGHT, J. G.—Ed. Lodge gram. schl., and Codrington College, Barbados; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Jan., 1885; prizeman in jurisprudence, 1884, and in common law, 1885; ag. judge, asst. ct. appeal, Barbados, 1888-9; judge, asst. ct. ap., July, 1889; is registr. of friendly societies, and visiting just. of Glendairy prison.

KNIGHT, JOHN LOCKE.—B. 1855; clk. and Zulu interp. to the Upper Tugela magistracy, Feb., 1876, to May, 1878; ditto, Umsinga magistracy, May, 1878, to Aug., 1881, during which time he acted for the R.M. and admstr. three times; Aug., 1881, clk. and Zulu interp., and regir. to the circuit ct., Newcastle dist.; Apr., 1882, ag. res. mag. and admstr. of native law, Newcastle; Feb., 1883, ditto, Umsinga div.; Oct., 1883, admstr. of native law, and Zulu border agt. to Lower Tugela div.; has held the hon. appts. of crown prosecutor and offr. for the sale of stamps at the magistracies named; commanded a native levy of over 1,000 strong during the Zulu war (medal); acted for two months in 1884 as R.M., Lower Tugela div.; sub-comsnr., Zululand, Apr., 1887; asst. comsnr. and R.M., Entojaneni dist., June, 1887; R.M., Nkandhla dist., Jan., 1893; mag., Inanda div., June, 1900; mag., Alexandra, June, 1905; dist. nat. comsnr., 1st July, 1909.

KNOCKER, FRED. WM., F.Z.S., Fell. Anth. Inst.—B. 1873; ed. Haberdashers' schl., Lond.; studied nat. sci. at Univ. Coll., Bristol; asst. curator, town museum, Leicester, Oct., 1892; sub-curator, civic museum, Bristol, Jan., 1898; settm. offr., Negri Sembilan, Fed. Malay States, Feb., 1901; curator, state museum, Perak, May, 1904.

KNOLLYS, MAJOR LOUIS FREDERICK, C.M.G. (1877).—B. 1847; Marlborough school; ensign, 32nd regt., 1866; capt., May, 1880; major, 26th Cameroonians, 1881; A.D.C. to the gov. of Mauritius, 1872-74, to gov. of Fiji, 1875; commanded force sent to reduce mountain tribes, 1876 (promoted to company and C.M.G.); comdt. armed native constab., and comsnr. in mountains, 1877; A.D.C. to gov. N. Zealand, 1880, and to gov. of Ceylon, 1883; inspr.-gen. of constab., and dir. of prisons, Jamaica, 1886; inspr.-gen. of pol., Ceylon, Oct., 1891; inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Sept., 1898; retired, 1902.

KNOWLES, JOSEPH.—B. 1861; schl. teacher, Fortune Island, under bd. of educn., Sept., 1884, to Oct., 1890; tide waiter, recvr.-gen.'s dept., Inagua, Bahamas, Oct., 1890, to Feb., 1897; ros. just., ex-officio collr. of rev., coroner, and schl. teacher under bd. of educn., Ragged Island, Feb., 1897, to 31st Aug., 1900; clk. to mag.'s ct., Nassau, Sept., 1903.
KNOWLING, HON. GEORGE.—B. 1842; M.L.C., Newfoundland, 1897; mem. of Cabinet, 1900; mem. of treas., bd. and gov. of savings bank, 1900.

KNUTSFORD, 1ST VISOOUNT (creat. 1895, created BARON 1888), THE RIGHT HON. SIR HENRY THURSTAN HOLLAND, BART., G.C.M.G. (1886), K.C.M.G. (1877), C.M.G. (1875).—B. 1825; succeeded to the baronetcy on the death of his father in Oct., 1873; ed. at Harrow and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inner Temp., Nov., 1849, and went to the N. Circuit; legal adviser to the sec. of state for the cols., Jan., 1867, and asst. under-sec. of state, Mar., 1870; resig., 3rd Aug., 1874, and elected mem. for Midhurst, 22nd Sept., 1874; mem. of the roy. coun., on the treatment of fugitive slaves, 1878; one of the roy. counsrs. on the defence of Br. possessions and commerce abroad, Sept., 1879; sec. to the treas., June, 1885; and vice-pres. of the coun., Sept., 1885, and again in Aug., 1886; returned to Parlmt. as mem. for the Hampstead div. of Middlesex, 1885, and again 1886 and 1887; sec. of state for the cols., Jan., 1887, to Aug., 1892; pres. col. confce., 1887.

KOCH, HENRY CHRISTIAN.—J.P., Natal; sworn translator and interp., Dutch, French, and German, 1883; also authorised to translate Spanish and Portuguese documents; atty., sup. ct., Natal, 1884; advoc., 1889; legal adviser to Impl. govt. in Zululand, 18th Apr., 1889; also crown prosecutor, regis. of deeds, and regis-gen. of Zululand; mast. of ch. mag.'s ct., and mem. of treas., bd.; recd. special thanks of S. of S., 1891; was J.P. for Zululand; on incorporation of Zululand with Natal was re-apptd. crown prosecutor and legal adviser, Zululand, 30th Dec., 1897; mag. Umvoti div., Natal, 1st July, 1899; ag. sec., law dept., Oct., 1899; master, sup. ct., 1st Aug., 1900; ag. mag., Durban, 6th Aug., 1900; spec. counsr. for Natal, apptd. by high commr. to admstr. oaths to surrendered burghers; resumed duties of mast., sup. ct., 1902; mem. of Dutch, German and French bd. of examrs., May, 1903; counsr., circuit ct., 1st crim. sess., northern dists., 9th Feb. to 31st Mar., 1904; counsr., derelict stock fund, 25th Sept., 1905; has on several occasions acted as counsr. of sup. ct. on circuit, and as puisne judge, sup. ct.

KOCH, WILFRED VINCENT MILLER, M.B.,C.M. (Edin., 1884). M.D. (Edin., 1895; highest honours, gold medal, thesis).—B. 1862; latc asst. in eye dept. of Edin. roy. infirmary; asst. to the lecturer on diseases of the eye and mental diseases, Edin. Univ.; and asst. to the gynaecological dept., Edin. schl. of medicine; clin. asst., Royal London Ophthalmic hosp., 1901; clin. asst., hosp. for diseases of ear, nose, and throat, Golden Sq., 1901; ag. asst. med. offr., W. Riding Asyl., Sheffield, 1884; asst. med. offr., Hull Borough Asyl., 1884-88; late surg. to Monckton and Carlton Main Collieries, Barnsley; asst. surg., col. hosp., Port of Spain, 1889; ag. med. supt., Leper Asyl., Trinidad, 1890-92; dist. med. offr., Diego Martin, 1890-92; ag. dist. med. offr., Port of Spain, 1892-93; ag. med. supt., Leper asyl., 1893; ag. dist. med. offr., Diego Martin, 1893; ag. med. supt. lun. avl. June to Oct., 1894; in charge of col. hosp., St. Fernando, June to Dec., 1899; and of col. hosp. Port of Spain, for various periods; ag. dist. med. offr., Couva, 1900; St. Joseph, 1902; lieut., 1902; capt., 1892; major comdg. Port of Spain art. volrs.; surg. to permanent staff, Trinidad locs. forces; major, local force reserve, 1906; asst. surg., Hong Kong, 1903; J.P.; med. offr. in charge of gaol and infectious diseases hosp., 1904; asst. supt., govt. civ. hosp., 1905; in charge of ditto, Apr., 1906, and from Dec., 1906, to Oct., 1907; ag. supt., govt. civ. hosp., 1909; lecturer surgery, Hong Kong Coll. of Medicine.

KENIG, LEON.—Senior clk., judicial dep., Seychelles, 2nd June, 1892; ag. 5th cls. col. col. sec., pub. Mauritius, in 1897, 1898 and 1899; 5th cls. clk. ditto, 1st July, 1899; 4th cls. ditto, 7th July, 1902; clk. to ex. coun. and comm. govt., 9th Nov., 1903; ag. asst. col. sec., 1904, 1907, 1908 and 1909.
KOLLER, T. W.—Clk., acctnts. dept., O.R.C., 1st July, 1900, to 31st Oct., 1900; pub. health dept., 1st to 30th Nov., 1902; exam. acct., audit dept., 1st Dec., 1902; is also tender bd.

KORFF, ADRIAN JOSEPH.—Cadet, cts., S. Aust., July, 1875; cadet, audit off., Aug., 1877; clk., audit off., Oct., 1878; clk. to registr.-gen., Dec., 1882; clk. and dist. regist. of I. and M., Dec., 1884; dep. registr.-gen., Jan., 1885; registr.-gen., July, 1908.

KORTRIGHT, L. M.—A.M.I.C.E., M.I.T., surv. of roads and supt. of pub. wks., St. Kitts, 1879; official mem. legis. coun., St. Kitts and Nevis, 1886; J.P., and visiting justice of gaol; mem. ex. coun., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1901; p. of forests, Aug., 1904.

KROHN, ALEXANDER WM.—B. 1883; ed. Coll., Bloemfontein, O.R.C.; clk., G.P.O., Bloemfontein, May, 1900; clk., imp. mil. dep., Bloemfontein, Sept., 1900; served in 3rd Army Service Corps, as spec. clk., 1st Mar. to 31st Aug., 1902; stationery dept., O.R.C., store clk., Govt. printing and stationery July, 1904.
KUPFERBURGER, JOHANN FRIEDRICH HELM.—B. 1866; ed. Stellenbosch, C.C. of the C. of G.H. (B.A.), Edin. (B.Sc., Zurich; inspr. of schlr., O.F.S., Sept., 1898, Oct., 1899; re-apptd. O.R.C., July, 1900.

LABORDE, ARTHUR LIONEL CRICHTON.—B. 1875; supernumerary, col. sec.'s off., Rand, 3rd Jan., 1893; acted as clk. to gov. in ex. ct. and to the exec. coun., May to Oct., 1893; clk., col. sec.'s off., Lagos, Apr., 1897; as registrar, July to Aug., 1901; ag. col. pt., 29th Aug., 1901, to 31st Mar., 1902, confirmed the appt.; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1903, to 1906; dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906; ag. provl. sec., Cent. Prov., May to June, 1906.

LABORDE, EDWARD DANIEL, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; 1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. regisr., ag. confidential clk. to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1882; 2nd clk., adminstr.'s office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Grenada, 1885; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1886; ch. of pol. and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1897; in charge of relief work after hurricane of 1898; specially mentioned in despatches and thanked by S. of S. for services; chmn. town bd., 1899-1901; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr., to May, 1901; ch. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1902; has served on several commsns. of enquiry in different colonies; ag. admistr., St. Vincent, June to Dec., 1904; ag. admistr., and col. sec., St. Lucia, July to Nov., 1905; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ag. admistr. and col. sec., May to Nov., 1907.

LACOSTE, THE HON. SIR ALEXANDER, K.C., KT. BACH. (1892), LL.D., D.C.L.—B. 1842; ed. at Hyacinthe Coll., Laval Univ.; called to the bar, 1863; Q.C., 1880; legis. councillor, Quebec, 1882; called to the Senate, 1884; ch. just., Prov. of Quebec, 1891; mem. of priv. coun. for Canada, 1892; ret. from ch. justiceship, 1907.

LAGDEN, SIR GODFREY YEATMAN, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1851; clerk, G.P.O., London, 1869-77, after compet. exam.; clk. to sec. to govt., Transvaal, 1878; priv. sec. to admistr., 1878-81; sec. to exec. coun. 1881-81; priv. sec. to Sir W. Bellairs and Sir E. Wood, while administering govt., 1881; sec. to Transvaal sub-comsn. for compensation claims, 1881-82; engaged as war correspondent, Egyptian campaign, 1882-3, present at all engagements, including Tel-el-Kebir and capture of Cairo; on special financial duty, G. Coast, 1883; sec. and acctnt., Basutoland, 1884; asst. comsnr., 1885; established magistracy in Masupha's country, 1886; acted as res. comsnr., May to Nov., 1890; ag. Br. comsnr., Swaziland govt. comtee. of adminstr., Apr., 1892; res. comsnr., Basutoland, 1893-1901; comsnr. for native affairs, Transvaal, Aug., 1901; mem. of ex. and leg. couns.; chrmn. of S. African inter-col. native affairs comsn., 1903-4.

LAIRD, THE HON. DAVID.—Mem. educn. coun., P.E.Ia., 1872-3; one of the delegs. who negotiated the admission of the island into the Dominion; sworn of the Canadian priv. coun. and min. of interior, 1873-6; lieut.-gov., N.W. Territories, 1876-81; was ch. comsnr. in negotiating treaty with Blackfeet Indians, 1877; apptd. Indian comsnr., 1898, which position he now holds.
LAMB, BENJAMIN.—Lieut., R.F.A.; served in S. African war, Jan., 1900, to Aug., 1902; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Barbados, Oct., 1902, to Aug., 1903.

LAMB, SIR JOHN CAMERON, KT. BACH. (1903), C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1843; entered the P.O. in 1864; prim. clk., 1877; was one of the Br. delegs. to the submarine cables protection confee. at Paris, May and Dec., 1886; asst. sec. to the P.O., 1889; 1st Br. deleg. to the internat. telegraph confee. at Paris, 1890, when he was also one of the representatives of the Cape and Natal; roy. comsnr. on electrical communication with lighthouses and lightships, June, 1892; 3rd sec. to the P.O., Apr., 1896; sen. Br. deleg. and "president de la commission des tarifs" at internat. telegraph confee. at Budapest, 1896, when he was also one of the representatives of the Cape, Natal and N. Zealand; 3rd sec. to P.O., Apr., 1896; 2nd sec., Jan., 1897; pres., internat. telegraph confee., London, 1903, and sen. deleg. of U.K., Cape and Natal; also sen. Br. deleg. at internat. confee. on wireless telegraphy, Berlin, 1903; holds Queen Victoria's Jubilee medal, and the Decoration of Nat. Lifeboat Inst.; ret., 1905.

LAMBERT, HENRY CHARLES MILLER, C.B. (1910).—B. 1868; ed. at Eton Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; 1st class in classical mods., 1889; 1st class in final classical schls., 1891; apptd. 2nd class clk. in the C.O. after open compet. exam., Feb., 1892; mem. of comtee., emigrts. information office, 1894; chrmn. of the comtee., 1897; acted as priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain for the purposes of the comtee. of the H. of C. on South Africa, 1897; 1st class clk., Nov., 1898; visited Canada, 1903; visited Australia, on behalf of E. I. O., in connection with emigration questions, 1905; prin. clk., Jan., 1907.

LAMINGTON, LORD (2nd Baron, creat. 1880), SIR CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NAPIER COCHRANE-BAILLIE, G.C.I.E. (1903), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1895).—Only son of 1st baron; born, 1860; dep. lieut. Lanarkshire; lieut. Lanarkshire yeomanry, 1883; M.P. for N. St. Pancras, 1886-90; gov. Queensland, 1895-1901; gov. of Bombay, 1903.

LAMONT, WILLIAM JAMES.—B. 1870; Grad. Moray House Coll., Edin.; entd. imp. custs. by comp. exam., 1st place in U.K., 1894; superv. of custs., G. Coast, 1896; travellg. and inspectg. superv., G. Coast, 1901; asst. collr. cust., S. Leone, 1901; ag. collr. cust., 10th May to 17th Oct., 1902, 8th Dec., 1903, to 14th Aug., 1904, and 19th June to 21st Nov., 1905; J.P. for Colony; seconded as ch. inspr. of cust. and financial adviser to Liberian govt., to reorganise customs of Liberia, Apr., 1906.
LANCZY-DAVIS, N. DE—Asst. dist. comsnr. E.A.P., 7th Aug., 1908.

LANTCOT, CHARLES, K.C.—B. 1864; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Montreal, and Laval Univ.; private tutor; called to the bar 1885; K.C., 1899; special law offr., Quebec, 1891; dep. atty-gen., Quebec, 1905; mem. of comsnr. for revision of code of civil proceed., 1893-97; comsnr. for revision of statutes of Quebec, 1909-10; published "Traité théorique et pratique du droit criminel"; "Manuel du juge de paix"; "Annotated Criminal Code."

LANDRY, HON. DAVID, M.A., M.D.—B. 1866; ed. commercial schls. and St. Joseph's Univ., New Brunswick; physician; mem. of coun. for parish of Wellington, Kent Co., New Brunswick, 1899-1900; elec. to legis., New Brunswick, 1908; comsnr. of agric., 1908.

LANDRY, PIERRE A., M.A., LL.D.—B. 1846; ed. St. Joseph's Univ., New Brunswick; called to the bar, New Brunswick, 1870; elec. to legis., New Brunswick, 1878; Q.C., 1881; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1883; judge of county ct., New Brunswick, 1890; judge of sup. ct., New Brunswick, 1893.
LANE, CHARLES ROBERT WILLIAM.—Impl. Br. E. Africa Co.; asst. dist. supt., (Oct., 1894; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 1st July, 1895; collr. 16th Sept., 1897; sub-comsnr., 1st July, 1904.

LANE, GEORGE.—Capt. R.A.M.C. militia; (hon. capt. in the army); ed. at Univ. of Edin. and Lond.; Livingstone memorial prizeman, 1889; med. offr., parish of St. Day, Cornwall, 1894; dist. med. offr., Niger Coast Prot., 1896; resigned, 15th May, 1898; med. offr., plague duty, India, 15th May, 1898; resigned, 5th July, 1900; recd. commendation of the govt. of Bombay for services; med. offr. and port surg., Somaliland Prots., 5th July, 1900; senr. med. offr., July, 1902; exchanged to E. Africa and Uganda Prots., Oct., 1904; Somaliland campaign, 1902-4 (medal and clasp).

LANGFORD, CHARLES WM.—B. 1847; issuer, commissariat dept., Barbados, 1865; asst. storekeeper, control dept., Trinidad, Oct., 1869; elk., G.P.O., Trinidad, June, 1871; 2nd ditto, Nov., 1871; 6th elk. rec.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1875; 5th ditto, Feb., 1877; 4th ditto, May, 1878; 3rd ditto, May, 1879; excise officer, Apr., 1884.

LANGHAM-CARTER, W.—Ed. at Bradfield Coll., Berks.; cadet, S. Sttltns., 1890; passed final exam in Malay, Dec., 1892; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Jan., 1896; dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, Apr., 1897; continued to act at Malacca; ag. collr. of ld. rev. and registr. of deeds, Singapore, Dec., 1898; inspr. prisons, S.S., Jan., to May, 1901; asst. P.M.G., Penang, Dec., 1901; 2nd magis., Sing., Aug., 1902; ag. collr. of land rev., and registr. of deeds, Sing., Sept., 1902; apptd. to cls. III. B, Jan., 1903; 2nd mag., Sing., Jan., 1905, but cont. to act as collr. of land rev.; ag. sen. dis. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Apr., 1907.

LANGLEY, WM. HENRY, C.M.G. (1903), F.R.C.S.I.—Asst. med. offr., W.A.F.F., 1898; sen. med. offr., N. Nigeria, 1901; dep. P.M.O., 1903; served with Kano-Sokoto expdt., 1903; P.M.O., Gold Coast, 1907; mem. of deptlnt. comte., on W. African med. staff, 1909.

LANSDOWNE, 5TH MARQUIS OF (Gt. Brit., creat. 1784), K.G., G.C.M.G. (1884), G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.: HENRY CHARLES KEITH FITZMAURICE, eldest son of the 4th Marquis.—B. 1845; succeeded his father in 1866; was under-sec. for war from Apr., 1872, to Feb., 1874, and under-secretary India Office, May to Aug., 1880; gov.-gen. of Canada, 1883; viceroy of India, Nov., 1888-94; sec. of state for war, 1895-1900; sec. of state for foreign affairs, 1900-1905.

LA ROCHELLE, MICHEL GAULTRON, K.C.B.A.—B. 1868; ed. at Nicolet Coll. and Laval Univ.; priv. sec. to Sir Wilfrid Laurier, 1866-90; barr-at-law, 1891; recorder of St. Henry, Montreal, 1895; mem. of civ. ser. coms., Canada, 1908.

LARYMORE, MAJOR H. D., C.M.G. (1896).—Late R.A.; b. 1867; ed. at Westminster; entd. Artil. Militia, 1886; lieut., R.A., Dec., 1886; capt., Sept., 1887; major, Feb., 1907; apptd. to G. Coast constab., Feb., 1891; adjt., July, 1891; served in Jehu expdn., as staff offr. and sec. to Sir F. Scott; mentioned in despatches, and received thanks of gov. and exec. and legis. couns. of Lagos (medal and clasp); served in Kumasi expdn., 1896, as A.D.C. and head-quarters camp commdt. (ment. in desps. and recd. spec. thanks for services; bronze star), ag. res., Kumasi, 1896-97; R.A. India, 1897-1901; R.A., S. Leone, 1901-02; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, Apr., 1902; 2nd cls. ditto, Oct., 1902; Hausa scholar, Christ's Coll., Camb., 1903; ret. from army, May, 1907.

LASCELLES, ALFRED GEORGE, B.A.—Univ. Coll., Oxon.; 2nd class mod. hist.; b. 1857; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1885; acted on several occasions as pres. of dist. cts. in Cyprus and as puisne judge; pres. of dist. cts., Paphos, 1892; ditto, Larneac, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1895; ag. Queen's advoc., Feb., 1896; pres. dist. cts., Nicosia, 1896; Queen's advoc., 1898; atty.-gen. of Ceylon, 1902; ag. ch. just., Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; is a K.C., Ceylon.
LASCHINGER, EDMUND HERBERT.—B. 1875; ent. civ. ser., Canada, 1896; asst. sec., P.A. dept., 1904; asst. dep. postm.-gen., 1907.
LASH, JAS. MILLER.—B. 1853; ed. upper middle schls., Peckham; schlrm., Kirkdale pris., Liverpool, 1876; gaoler, Grenada, 1886; supt. prisons, 1890; gov., Glendairy prison, Barbad., 1894.

LATCHFORD, HON. FRANCIS ROBERT, B.A.K.C.—B. 1856; ed. Ottawa Univ.; called to bar, 1886; K.C., 1898; elec. to Ontario leg., 1899; re-elec., 1902; min. of pub. wks., Ontario, 1899; atty.-gen., 1904-5; judge, chancery div., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1908.

LAUDER, JOHN ILEX.—2nd elk., registr. as prov.-marshal's offr., Dominica, Jan., 1901; ag. asst. mast., Dominica gram. schl., May to 4th Aug., 1905; govt. offr., treas., Sept., 1905; elk. to comsnnr., Montserrat, Oct., 1906; educn. dist. offr., Aug., 1907.
LAURENCE, SIR PERCIVAL MATTLAND, K.B.C.H. (1908).—Puisne judge, Griqualand W Cape Col., 1882; judge pres., 1888.

LAURIER, THE RIGHT HON. SIR WILFRID P.C., G.C.M.G. (1897).—B. at St. Lin., Quebec, Nov. 20th, 1841; became a B.C.L. of McGill, 1864, and a Q.C. in 1880; entered parlmt., 1871; being elected for Drummond and Athabasca, was re-elected in 1874 for the same constituency; min. of inland rev. in the Mackenzie mines, Sept., 1877, which office he held until the resignation of that govt. in 1878. He was defeated in the gen. election of 1878, but was immediately afterwards elected for Quebec E. He was re-elected at the gen. elections in 1878, 1882, 1887, and 1891. Became leader of the Opposition in Canada in 1890, and premier in June, 1896, on the resignation of the govt. of Sir C. Tupper; was present as the representative of Canada in London at H.M.'s Diamond Jubilee; mem. joint inter-high coms., Quebec and Washington, 1898; rep. Canada at col. confe., 1897 and 1902; at Imp. confe., 1907; re-elected for Quebec E., and also returned for Ottawa, 1908; ret. Ottawa seat, 1909.

LAVERGNE, HON. JOSEPH.—B. 1847; ed. St. Ann's Coll., Quebec; admitted at bar, 1871; practised law for twenty-six years in partnership with Sir W. Laurier in Arthabaska; mayor of town of Arthabaska, and warden of comt. of Arthabaska for some years; member of Canadian H. of C., 1887-97; judge of superior ct. for dist. of Ottawa, 1897; Montreal, 1901; judge of ct. of King's Bench, Quebec, since 1906.

LAW, SIR ARCHIBALD FITZGERALD, K.T. B.A.C.H. (1908).—B. 1853; ed. at Oriel Coll., Oxon.; called to the bar, Inner Temp., Jan., 1879; asst. coms., Cyprus, 1880; pres. dist. cts., Famagusta, 1880; passed higher standard mod. Greek, 1883; dir. of survey, prin. offr. of land regsn. and prin. forest offr., 1886; M.L.C., 1887; ag. Queen's advoc. in 1889 and 1890; ag. ch. sec., 1890-91; chrmn. of retrenchment coms., 1891; Queen's advoc., 1892; puisne judge, S. Sttltns., 1893; conducted special inquiry in Perak, 1894, and received thanks of S. of S.; has acted three times as ch. just., S. Sttltns., ch. judel. coms., F.M.S., Aug., 1906; ag. ch. just., S. Sttltns., Oct., 1907, to Oct. 1908.
LAW, JOHN CRAWFORD.—Ed. at the Royal Mil. Coll., Kingston, Canada; coms., in 10th Regt., Royal Grenadiers of Toronto, Oct., 1897;...

LAWLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR, K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1860; sec. to adminstr., S. Rhodesia, May, 1896; deputy-administr., Matabeleland, Nov., 1896; administr., Matabeleland, Dec., 1898; ag. administr., Mashonaland, July, 1899; gov. of W. Aust., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., Transvaal, 15th Aug., 1902; ag. gov. of Transvaal and O.R.C. and high commr., 1903; chmn. of ex. coun., pres. of leg. coun., mem. I.C.C.; gov. of Madras, 1905.
