YORKE.—Jun. clk., treasy., Jamaica, 1883; sen. asst. clk. cts., Clarendon 1889; dep. clk. cts., Westmoreland, 1892; 1st clk. sup. ct., 1894; ag. regiser., 1895; lieut., Jamaica militia, 1895; offr. with Jubilee contingent (medal), 1897; ag. regiser., 1898; capt., 1899; passed exam. before judges of sup. ct. equal to solrs. final, 1899; clk. of cts., Clarendon, 1900; ag. comsnr. and judge of grand court, Cayman Islands, 1906; called to the bar, hon. soc'y of Gray's Inn, 1907; ag. res. mag., Clarendon, 1908; ag. judge, sup. ct., Turks Island, 1909; ag. comsnr., Mar. to Oct., 1909; J.P. for Kingston, Jamaica; stip. mag., Kingston, Oct., 1909; res. mag., Portland, Jan., 1910, and in addtn. ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, July, 1910.

SLATER, ALEXANDER RANSFORD.—B. 1874; ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. Coll., Camb. (B.A. 1897); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra off. astt. govt. agent, W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd astt. P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec duty at camp for prisoners of war, Diyatalawa, July, 1902; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. legis. coun., May, 1904; dist. judge, Badulla, Apr., 1906; additional astt. col. sec. and clk., legis. couns., Apr., 1907; 2nd astt. col. sec., Feb., 1908; ag. prin. astt. col. sec., Dec., 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Sept., 1910.

SLOLEY, HERBERT CECIL, C.M.G. (1905).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-inspr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; asst. comsnr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. comsnr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. sec., 1898; ag. res. comsnr., Dec., 1900; res. comsnr., Sept., 1901.
SLY, HON. R. M., M.A., LL.D.—Judge of sup. ct., New South Wales, 11th Feb., 1908.

SLYNE, D.—Barrister-at-law; Lord O'Hagan medallist and David Lynch medallist, King's Inns, Ireland; entered Imperial civ. serv., open compet., 1880; until 1900 attached successively to Inland Rev. Dept. at Dublin, Sligo, Chester, Oxford, Leicester, Birmingham, Dublin, Dundee, and Birmingham; apptd. supervising excise offr., Trinidad, 1900; ag. receiver-gen., May, 1902; receiver-gen., Feb., 1903; comsnr. of currency, 1903; mem. of Port of Spain sewerage bd. and water authority, 1904; mem. of Port of Spain town bd., 1907.

SMALL, ROBERT.—Ed. at Univ. Coll. Schl., St. Thomas Hosp., King's Coll. Hosp., and Univ. Coll. Hosp.; certif. of Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med.; L.R.C.P. (Lond.); M.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1901; D.P.H. (Lond.), 1905; D.T.C. (Camb.), 1906; civ. surg., S. African field force, 1901-1902; med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 16th Feb., 1906.

SMALLWOOD, HENRY ARMSTRONG.—B. 1869; entered navy, 1885; on board H.M.S. "Victoria" at time of disaster, June, 1893; asst. comsnr., Colo West, and stip. mag., Apr., 1894; ag. stip. mag., Ba and Yasawa, Apr., 1896; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, and priv. sec. to gov. Fiji, Apr., 1896; ag. sec. to W. Pac. high comm., May, 1897, to May, 1898; stip. mag., Savu Savu and Tavuni, Jan., 1899; island treas., Cyprus, Mar., 1900; passed lower standard Greek, Dec., 1900; ag. comsnr., Nicosia, June-Sept., 1904; treas., St. Lucia, Nov., 1907; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; nominated deleg. to Canadian Reciprocity Conference at Barbados, Jan., 1908; ag. col. sec., July 1908, to Jan., 1909; ag. admnstr. and col. sec., Apr., 1909; ag. col. sec., June to Oct., 1909.

SMART, ARCHIBALD WM.—B. 1849; clk., dept. of trade and cust., Melbourne, 1863; landing waiter, 1868; landing survr., 1890; senior ditto, 1893; collr. of cust., Victoria, and regist. of shipping, Melbourne, 1903; also ch. Commonwealth survr., 1903.

SMARTT, MAJOR J. P. (3rd batt. South Lancs. Regt.).—Public wks. and roads dept., G. Coast, 1893-96; local auditor, N. C. Prot. (now S. Nigeria), Oct., 1896; chief asst. treas., Dec., 1901; ag. treas., Dec., 1901, to June, 1902, June to Dec., 1903, Sept., 1904, to Jan., 1905, Feb. to Aug., 1905; apptd. provcl. treas. on amalgtn. with Lagos, 1st May, 1906; ag. financial comsnr., mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Aug., 1906, to Mar., 1907, and Jan. to Nov., 1908; ag. adjt., G. Coast volrs., May to Nov., 1903; ag. comitt., Oct., 1905, to Mar., 1906; attached for duty with N. C. Prot. force (now S. N. Regt.), May to July, 1897, May and Aug., 1900; served with expeditn. force, Benin territories, Apr. and May, 1899 (ment. in desps., medal and clasp); major comdg. So. Nigeria volrs., July, 1907.

SMARTTTT, HON SIR THOS. WM., K.C.M.G., (1911).—L.R.C.S., Ireland, 1878; L.K.Q.C.P.I., 1880; M.L.A. Cape Colony, since 1894; col. sec., 1898; comsnr. ot pub.wks., 1900-02, and 1904-1908; ag. prime minister during periods of 1904, 1905 and 1906; attended Impl. confce., 1907; mem. of S. African Nat. Convntn., in connection with South Africa Bill, 1909; holds Cape Colony general service medal, 1879, with clasp, "Transkei," having served as surg. with P.A.O.C.V.A.; also holds medal and clasp for Kimberley siege, 1899-1900.

SMEETON, S.P., I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1842; extra clk. in office of S. of S. for Cols., June, 1862; 1st class clk. in col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, June, 1870; regisr.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, 8th Feb., 1878; suptd. the taking of census of Jamaica, 1891 and 1891; dep. keeper of records (on amalgamation of registration and record depts.), May, 1899; nom. mem. of comsn. to inquire into marriage and regisn. laws, May 1903.
SMITH, ALEXANDER.—Treasur. astt., E. Africa Prot., 19th Sept., 1904.

SMITH, ALLAN F., I.S.O. (1906).—Clk. in P.O., Bermuda, 1879; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1886; asst. col. sec., 1895; col. postmtr., 1896; ag. col. sec., July and Aug., 1899, and from May, 1900, to July, 1901; recvr. gen., 1908.

SMITH, SIR C. ABERCROMBIE, KT. BACH. (1903).—M.A. (Camb. and Glas.), fellow of St. Peter's Coll., Camb., 2nd wrangler and 2nd Smith's prize-man, 1878, &c.; mem. of the house of assem., Cape, 1866-75; mem. of the exec. coun., 1872; comsnr. of crown lands and pub. works., 1872-75; mem. of comte. under Ordnance Act, 1872-75; comsnr. of the sinking fund, 1872-74; mem. of the Univ. coun., 1873; chmn. of the meteorol. comsn., 1874; controller and audr.-gen., 1875; chmn. of the tender bd., 1875; mem. of the surety bd., 1875-86; vice-chancellor, Cape Univ., 1877-9; comsnr., civ. ser. exams. bd., 1886; chairman, 1887; ret., 1904.

SMITH, C. A. JEFFREY.—Apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Jamaica, Aug., 1870; asst. to ch. supt., 1875; 3rd class ditto, 1879; 2nd class ditto, 1881; ag. dist. engnr., W. dist., 1886; ditto, Kingston dist., 1888-9; overseer of wks., prisons dept., 1889; 1st cls. supt., pub. wks., and supt., St. Catherine waterwks., 1896; also acted on several occasions as govt. assessor, and suppt. irrigation wks.

SMITH, SIR CECIL CLEMENTTI, P.C. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1892), K.C.M.G. (1886), C.M.G. (1880).—B. 1840; ed. at St. Paul's Sch. and Corp. Chr. Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1862, M.A. 1868; elected student interp., Hong Kong, Mar., 1862; regsr.-gen., Mar., 1865; ag. pol. mag., June, 1865; ag. col. sec., 1867; mem. of exec. coun., 1869; ag. col. sec., Feb., 1872, to Dec., 1873; treas., 1871; acted as col. sec., Oct., 1874, and also from Mar., 1875; col. sec., S. Stiltsv., July, 1878; apptd. under the F.O. to be Br. comsnr. to settle certain marine claims at Manila, Sept., 1878, and received the thanks of H.M.'s govt.; also for services in connection with the "Niseru" case, 1884; administered govt. S.S., Mar., 1884, to Nov., 1885; lieut.-gov. and col. sec., Ceylon, Nov., 1885; gov. of S.S., 1887; also consul-gen. and high comsnr. for Borneo and Sarawak, 1889; ret., 1893; pres. of comsn. to inquire into Trinidad riots, 1903.

SMITH, SIR CHARLES HOLLED, K.C.M.G., C.B. —B. 1846; ensign, 1863; lieut., 1869; capt., 1877; S. African war, 1879-1881; Zulu campaign; Transvaal cam., battles of Laing's Nek, Ingogo, and Majuba Mountain; despatches, Lon. Gaz., May 3rd, 1881; Egyp. expdn., 1882; at Ramleh, Tel-el-Mahuta; action at Kassassin; battle of Tel-el-Kebir, Lon. Gaz., Nov. 2nd, 1882; major (brevet), Nov., 1882; with Egyp. army, Jan., 1883, to 1892; Soudan expdn., 1884-5; lieut.-col., 1885; col. Dec., 1888; gov.-gen., Red Sea dist. and comdt. Suakin, Sept., 1888, to Aug., 1889; Soudan, 1888-91; action of Garnaizah, Oct., Jan. 11th, 1889; occupation of Handoul, 1891; major-gen. comdg Victorian mil. Dec., 1889; several medals, clasps to bronze, and mentioned in numerous despatches.

SMITH, EDWIN MITCHELL.—Entered crown lands dept., S. Australia, as astt., 1862; cadet and jun. survr.; survr., Jan., 1874; steward and surveyor, land., Feb., 1882; ch. clk., land., Jan., 1886; dep. survr.-gen., July, 1894; mc. central pastoral and central lands bds.

SMITH, FRANCIS JAGOE.—M.A. (Oxon); Merchant Taylors schl.; scholar, St. John's Oxford, 1892; 2nd cls. class. mods., 1894; math. mods., 1894; 2nd Lit. Hum., 1895; M.A., 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1896; attached to col. sec.'s off., 1896; offic. to govt. agt., and deputy fiscal, Cent. Prov., 1898; comsnr. of requests and pol. Balapitiya, Aug., 1900; ditto, Galle, June, 1900; landing survr., customs, Colombo, Dec., 2nd astt. col. sec., Sept., 1905; dep. col. cust., Colombo, Feb., 1908.
SMITH, FRANK BRAYBROOKE.—B. 1864; adviser to govt. of Transvaal, Apr., 1902; ag. agric., Aug., 1902; M.L.C., 1902-7; and land settlmt. bd.; ag. sec. for agric., Umt. S. Africa, 1910.

SMITH, FREDERICK BONHAM, I.S.O. (1906), B. 1837; entered col. sec.'s office, Barbados, 1858; office of gov.-in-chief of the Windward Is., 1866; pol. mag. of Christchurch, Barbados, sen. pol. mag. of Bridgetown and St. Michael, ag. judge of the astt. ct. of appeal, 1878 to inspr. of prisons, 1878, held with office of prov. marshal from 1882; ag. col. sec., 1879.

SMITH, FREDERICK STANLEY.—B. 1874; at Sherborne Coll.; called to the bar in Jan., 1892; ag. pol. mag., dist. "B.", Barc, 1893; ditto, dist. "D.", Apr., 1894, to July, and from Dec., 1895, to Oct., 1896; J.P., ag. judge, astt. ct. of appeal, Apr. to Sept., pol. mag., dist. "D.", Jan., 1899; ag. pol. dist. "A.", Nov., 1901, to Dec., 1902; appnt. mag., dist. "A." and Bridgetown, Dist., ag. judge, astt. ct. of appeal and mast. in chanc. in 1906, and again in 1907.

SMITH, GEORGE B. 1858; entd. war of 10th Apr., 1878; clk., ch. sec.'s office, Cey. 24th Nov., 1879; ch. clk., ditto, 1881; ast. ch. sec., 15th Dec., 1883; ag. dir. of sur., 10th Aug., 1886, to 14th Feb., 1887; clk. & coun., 1st Mar., 1886, to 24th Nov., 1891; ag. sec., and mem. of exec. coun., 15th Mar., 10th Oct., 1888, and on other occasions: com. Papho, 25th Nov., 1891; regist.-gen. and off. of legis. coun., 7th June, 1895; has served as mem. of comte. of management, Cyprus mus., 1886; sec., Col. and Ind. Exhib. comte., sec. to Queen's Jubilee Memorial comte., mem. of land registn. comen., 1891; pres. of comsnr., Papho, 1892; mem. of ecclesiastical comsnr., 1895; mem. of mun. comsn., Nicosia, 1895; mem. of gen. hosp. bd., Nicosia, 1895; mem. of bd. of agric., 1896; ag. British deleg. of Evcaf, under conven. with Turkey, 20th July to 25th Nov., 1903; ag. prin. forest offr., 1904 and 1907; ag. dir. of agric., 1904 and 1905; British delegate of Evcaf, 20th July, 1905; recvr.-gen., and ch. collr. of cust., 1905, 1906.

SMITH, GEORGE.—Ed. at Galway House Sch., Leyton, Essex, and at Morden House, Greenwich; dist. commr., Anamaboe, G. Coast, 1877; list. commr., Dexevo, 1878; ditto, Palma and Leckie, Lagos, 1880; sub-coll. customs, Palma and Leckie, 1883; ch. clk. and warehouse keeper, customs, Lagos, Aug., 1889; ag. dist. commr., dist. Lagos, Mar., 1892.

SMITH, GEORGE DOUGLAS, C.M.G. (1905).—B. 1865; joined Imperial Brit. East Africa Co., 1890; treas., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1894; also chief of cust., from Oct., 1906, and man. and controller of treasy. savings bank, from 1st Oct., 1907; East and Cent. African medal with clasp, Uganda, 1897-8.

SMITH, CAPT. GEORGE ECHLIN.—B. 1871; ed. Rathmines Schl. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joined 5th batt. Royal Irish Regt., 1891; capt., 1895; hon. capt. in army, 1900; asst. inspr., Hausa force (G. Coast batt.), Jan., 1897; sub-inspr., B. Guiana police, Feb., 1898; dist. inspr., Oct., 1900; A.D.C. to Sir J. A. Swettenham, K.C.M.G., 1902-1904; A.D.C. to Sir F. Hodgson, K.C.M.G., 1904-1906; asst. dist. supt. of police, E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1906.

SMITH, MAJOR GEORGE EDWARD, C.M.G. (1909).—Educ. at Winchester and Woolwich; commr. in R.E., 1888; asst. on Anglo-German boundary, commr., E. Africa, 1891-2; on Mombasa to Victoria Nyanza road expdt., 1895-9; commr. and comdr., B. section, Anglo-German boundary, 1904-6; dir. of surveys, E.A.P., 26th June, 1906; reverted to R.E., 1910.

SMITH, GEORGE WHITFIELD.—F.L.S., Clk., pub. library, Barbados, Sept., 1879; 3rd treasy. clk., St. Lucia, 1882; sub-coll. of taxes, 1st dist., St. Lucia, Aug., 1882; rev. offr., Leeward dist., St. Vincent, Feb., 1885 (resigned); curator, botanic gardens, Grenada, Dec., 1890 (resigned), 16th Dec., 1893); trav. supt., Imperial dept. of agric., Dec., 1898; pol. mag. and ass. treas., N. dist. Grenada, and pol. mag., 3rd dist., St. Vincent, Apr., 1904; commr., Carriacou, Oct., 1904; was engaged as asst. botanist to W. India exploration comtce. of Royal Soc. during scientific exploration of St. Vincent, 1888-89; is a corrpdg. mem. of Royal Hort. Soc. of England.

SMITH, GERALD STANLEY WELLS.—B. 1873; graduate, Ontario business coll., Canada; supry. clk., regis.'s office, Grenada, Jan., 1890; asst. clk., post office, Mar., 1890; 4th clk., treasy., June, 1890; asst. clk., col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1890; 2nd clk., treasy., Nov., 1891; audit clk., Nov., 1897; thanked by gov. for report on condition of paupers, 1905; has held numerous acting appointments, including ag. aud. of Windward Islands, 4th June to 31st Dec., 1906; 1st Jan. to 13th Mar., 1907; and from 18th Mar., 1908, to 24th Nov., 1909.

SMITH, LIEUT.-COL. SIR GERARD, K.C.M.G. (1895).—B.1839; late lieut.-col. Roy. Scots Guards; groom-in-waiting to Her Majesty, 1883-5; M.P. for High Wycombe, 1883-5; J.P. for E. Riding of Yorkshire; gov. of W. Australia, 1895 to 1900.
SMITH, HENRY J.—B. 1859; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. messenger C.O., 12th Nov., 1883; 2nd cl. ditto, 1st June, 1897; Queen's mess., 1st Apr., 1898; asst. office keeper, 22nd May, 1907.

SMITH, LIEUT.-COL. HENRY ROBERT, C.M.G. (1911), I.S.O., J.P.—B. 1843; ed. Kingston (Ont.) gram. schl.; entd. civ. ser., Canada, 1850; apptd. deputy-sergeant-at-arms, H. of C., 1872; sergeant-at-arms, 1892; is also hon. A.D.C. to H.E. the Gov.-Gen. of Canada.

SMITH, JAMES ALFRED.—B. 1871; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1891; promoted to higher grade, 1902; asst. sec. to col. regns. comtce., 1907-8.

SMITH, JAS. CARMICHAEL.—B. 1852; ch. clk. and storekr., Imperial treasy. and commissariat depts., Bahamas, June, 1876, to Aug., 1889; mem. bd. of educ., 1886 to 1892; postmr., 1889 to 1893; J.P., 1890; elected mem. of Bahamas House of Assembly in 1882-89 and 1896; resigned 1897; asst. postmr.-gen., Sierra Leone, Dec., 1896; J.P., 1897; ag. postmr.-gen., 1897, 1899, and 1900; ag. curator of intestates, S. L., from Jan., 1899, to Jan., 1900; postmr.-gen. and man. of govt. sav. bank, 1900; ag. collr. of cust., Oct., 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. col. treas. in 1906 and 1907; author of "The Distribution of the Produce"; "Inter-Temporary Values, or the Distribution of the Produce in Time"; "The Trust and the Gold Trust."

SMITH, JAMES LANDRETH.—B. 1870; draftsman, P.W.D., St. Lucia, 13th July, 1891; draftsman survey off., Sept., 1893; warden, and supt. of water-wks. under St. George's town bd., Apr., 1895, to Aug., 1901; chief overseer of roads and works, Grenada, 19th Aug., 1901; asst. supt. of wks., 1st Apr., 1902; commr. of crown lands, 28th June, 1904; survr. of crown lands, 1904; ag. supt. of wks., 14th June, 1906, to 16th Jan., 1907.

SMITH, SIR JOHN SMALMAN, KT. BACH. (1896).—M.A., St. John's Coll., Camb.; b. 1847; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1872; special pleader Oxford circuit; puisne judge, G. Coast Col., 1883; judge, Lagos, 1886; ch. just., Lagos, 1889; ret., 1895.
SMITH, LAURENCE.—Ed. Bedford mod. schl.; clk. in accs. dept., Nyasaland prot., 1899; 3rd asst. treasr., Oct., 1902; 2nd asst. treasr., Dec., 1908.

SMITH, LINDSAY LEA.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to commr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs, postmr., cashier and regis. of shipping) commr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier, and regis. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907.
SMITH, MILES STANIFORTH.—Comsrr. for lands and dir. of agric., Papua, 16th May, 1907; adminstr., commr. for lands, and dir. of mines, agric. and pub. wks., 30th Nov., 1908.

SMITH, RICHARD WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., B.A., B.A.I.—P. W. Dept., Ceylon; b. 1856; ed. at the Grammar Schl., Chard, Somerset, 1874, and Dublin Univ. (Trinity College), 1875-79; employed in engineer's off., D.W.W. Rly., 1879; res. engr., Wicklow Harb. works, 1880-83; asst. to Mr. J. Lanyon, Belfast (ryls., waterwks., drainage, etc.), 1883-85; res. engr., Cleggan Harb. Works, 1885-88; asst. res. engr., St. Helier's Harb. Works (Jersey), 1888-89; supt. P.W., Pahang, S.S., 1889-92; D.E., irrig. dept., Ceylon, 1892-94; ag. prov. engr., N.W.P., 1894-97; dist. engr. and ag. prov. engr., S.P., 1898; seconded to railway extensions staff, 1898; seconded to "Water Supply Schemes," 1898-1900; charge of Boer Camp Works, 1900; "Water Supply Schemes," 1901-02; seconded as res. engr., proposed convict prison works, Seychelles, 1902; returned to Ceylon 13th Aug., 1902; engaged in designs of water supplies to the towns of Negombo, Jaffna, Pamban (S. India), Mannar, Galle and Matara; asst. dir. of irrigtn., Colombo, 1st Jan., 1905; dir. of irrigtn., 1909.
SMITH, ROBERT MURRAY, C.M.G. (1884).—B. 1831; agt. gen. for Victoria in United Kingdom, 1881-86.
SMITH, HON. SYDNEY.—Formerly M.P. for Macquarie, New South Wales in the Federal parlt.; postmr.-gen., Commonwealth of Australia, in Reid-McLean ministry, 1904-05.

SMITH, SYDNEY FREDERICK, F.R.G.S.—B. 1870; clk. in treas., Fiji, 22nd May, 1893; clk., native dept., 1st Jan., 1894; stip. mag., and tax insp., Kadavu (in addition to duties as clk. in native dept.), 1st Jan., 1896; ch. clk., native dept., 4th Feb., 1897; prov. inspnr., 1st Jan., 1899; ag. stip. mag., tax inspnr., and asst. nat. comsnr., Lau Archipelago, 13th Jan., 1902; ditto, Lautoka, 21st Jan., 1903; asst. nat. comsnr., 1st Jan., 1904; sub-collr. of cust. and postmr., Lautoka, 6th May, 1904; on leave from Apr., 1903, to Mar., 1905, and acted as war corres. with Gen. Oku's forces in Russo-Japanese war; ag. stip. mag. and tax inspnr., Lautoka, from 15th Apr., 1907; ag. gov't. comsnr., Naitasiri, and stip. mag., Rewa, 1st Feb., 1909; stip. mag., 1st grade, Macuata, 1st Feb., 1910.

SMITH, THOMAS SERCOMBE, B.A., LL.B. (Lond.).—Hong Kong cadet, 1882; attached to C.O., 1883; passed cadet, 1886; ag. asst. regir. gen., 1886-90, except for five months as ag. pol. mag.; asst. regir. gen., 1891; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Nov., 1893; ag. puisne judge, various dates, 1895-7, and in 1900, 1901, 1902, 1904 and 1905; treas., Dec., 1896; pol. mag., July, 1898; ag. col. sec., 1898-9, for 3 months in 1901, and from Aug., 1905, to Dec., 1906; puisne judge, S. Stithmt., 1907.

SMITH, WM. EDWARD.—Employed on L. & N.W. Rly., 1872-82; asst. traffic man., Indian guaranteed rlys. (Scinde, Punjab, and Delhi), 1883-86; ag. district traffic man., Lahore and Delhi div.; gen. man., Barbados Rly., 1889; gen. and traffic man., Trinidad Govt. Rly., Feb., 1894; visited Jamaica and reported on Banana industry, 1904; comsnr. for Trinidad, Colonial and Indian Exhibn., London, 1905; is a J.P.; gen. man. of govt. rlys., G. Coast, 1906; major comlnt., rly. volrs., May, 1907; offi. mem., Sekondi town council.

SMITH, SIR WILLIAM FREDERICK HAYNES, K.C.M.G. (1890), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1839; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., June, 1866; solr.-gen., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1865; atty.-gen., Oct., 1874; admstd. the govt., Apr. to Sept., 1884, and in 1887; gov., Leeward Is., Nov., 1888; gov., Bahamas, 1895; high comsnr., Cyprus, 1898-1904.

SMITH, SIR WILLIAM JAMES, KT. BACH. (1896).—M.A., LL.M. Trinity Hall, Camb.; b. 1853; acted as puisne judge of the G. Coast Col. from Dec. 1880, to July 1881; puisne judge of the sup. ct. of Cyprus, 1882; ch. just., 1892; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1898; puisne judge, sup. ct., Transvaal, 1st Mar., 1902.
SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—B.Sc., M.B., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr., and inspnr. of anatomy, S. Australia, 1899-1903; chrmn. cent. bd. of health, and coroner, 1903.

SMITHERS, CAPT. JAMES HENRY.—B. 1872; served in Cape Colony med. corps, 29th Oct., 1889, to 8th July, 1892; Cape Town Highlanders, 9th July, 1892, to 2nd Oct., 1897; served as lieut. in Langeberg campaign, for which recd. Cape General Ser. Medal with clasp "Bechuanaland"; capt., Green and Sea Point Town Guard, 8th Jul., 1900, to 30th May, 1901; capt., 1st Batt. (Peninsular Regt.), 1st June, 1901, to 31st Oct., 1902, when disbanded, temporarily in command afterwards adjutant; lieut. and qtrmr., Garrison Artillery, 1st Nov., 1904; capt. and qtrmr., 15th Nov., 1905.

SMITH-STEINMETZ, GERARD ARCHIBALD JOHN.—B. 1878; ed. at Stonyhurst coll.; asst. Straits Settlements, Oct., 1902; in charge of emigr. depot, S. India, June, 1904; passed exam. in Tamil, July, 1904; ag. 3rd mag., Feb., 1905; passed cadet, July, 1905; ag. asst. of Indian imigrts., Nov., 1907; lent for service F.M.S., 1909; dist. offr., Dindings, Jan., 1910.

SMUTS, GEN. THE HON. JAN CHRISTIAN.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Eben schein; Christ's Coll., Camb.; double first in law tri., 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; state-attn., S. African Republic, 1898; served with Boer forces in Cape Col. during the latter part of campaign; mem. of head comte., of Het Voortrekker sec., Transvaal, 1907; mem. of Imp. Conf. Naval and Military Defence, 1909; min. of Interior, min. of mines, and min. of defez Union of S. Africa, 1910.

SMUTS, JOHANNES, I.S.O. (1908).—C. treasy., Cape, 1882; acted as priv. sec. to Hon. J. Gordon Sprigg, 1885-6; to Sir H. Smyth, 1889; to Lieut.-Gen. H. E. Cameron, and to Sir H. Loch, 1891; sec. to Hon. J. Hofmeyr (Swaziland Convention), 1890; despatch clk., govt. house, 1886; 2nd class, 1887; ag. priv. sec. to Lieut.-Gen. Cameron, and to Sir H. B. Loch, 1891 to 1895; ch. clk. to comsnr., 1891; acted as clk. of exec. comm., 1891 and 1895; Br. consul in Swaziland, Aug., 1896; served on staff of Sir C. Warren during par. S. African war; registr. of deeds, Apr., 1900; chmn. of Swaziland concessions coms., 1904.

SMYLY, SIR PHILIP CHAMPTON, KT. BAR. (1866).—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., E. LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dub., 1886; LL.D., 1891; called to the bar, Grays' Inn, 1902; Queen's advoc., S. Leone, 1895; atty.-gen., 1896; ch. just., S. Leone, 1901.
SMYTH, C. E. OWEN, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1878; ed. at Erasmus Smith Sch., Dub.; profnl. clk., 3rd class, 1878; 1st class, 1883; supt. of pub. build. and head of works and bldg. dept., S. Aus., 1886.

SMYTH, HERBERT WARINGTON.—B. 1878; ed. at Westmstr. and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.L.L.M., F.G.S., F.R.G.S., barr.-at-law; asst. sup. ct., Transvaal; unpaid asst. to min. adviser to office of woods, 1891; sec., dep't. mines, Siam, 1891; director-gen. of mines and geological survey, Siam, 1895; sec., Stat. Legation, 1897 (order of White Elephant, cls.); Murchison award of R.G.S. for travel, Siam, 1898; sec. to mines dept., Transvaal, M.L.C., Transvaal, 1906; mem. exec. coun. ag. comsnr. of mines, Natal, 1906; sec. for mines, 1907; ag. sec. for mines, Union of S. Africa, comsnr. of mines, Natal, 1910; author "Five Years in Siam," "Mast and Sail in Europe and Asia," papers on Indo-China, etc., etc.

SMYTH, JAMES LLOYD.—B. 1871; served R.I.C. from 1889 to 1897; transferred from dep't., Belfast, to Jamaica constab., Sept., 1897; ag. supt., M. and S. co. gaol, Sept., 1901. Fe-b., 1901; asst. supt. of pol., Br. Hond., July, 1908; J.P. for colony; ag. supt. of pol., 20th Sept., to 26th Oct., 1906; vis. just. to Corozal and dist. pris., Dec., 1906; ag. supt. of pol., 4th Apr., 1907, to 3rd Mar., 1908; vis. just. to Belize pris., June, 1907; ag. dist. comsrr., Corozal, Dec., 1908, to Feb., 1909.

SMYTHE, Hon. Chas. John.—B. 1852; J.P., Natal, 1887; M.L.A., Natal, 1893; speaker, 1897-9; col. sec., 1899-1903; prime min. of Natal and col. sec., May, 1905, to Nov., 1906; admstr., Natal Prov., Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910.

SOLOMON, John.—2nd class asst. of excise, England, Apr., 1868; 1st class ast., June, 1870; rile offr., June, 1871; resig., Jan., 1876; ag. 3rd clk., imgrm. dept., Br. Guiana, July, 1877; ag. cashier in rec-gen.'s office, 1878; comsry. of taxation, 1879.
SOLOMON, Michael Claude.—Ag. 3rd cl. elk., island med. dept., Jamaica, Mar., 1885; 3rd cl. elk., Oct., 1888; 2nd cl. elk., July, 1891.

SOLOMON, Hon. Sir Richard, K.C.B. (1905), K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.V.O. (1907), C.B. (1903), K.C.—B. 1850; ed. S. African Coll., Capetown; St. Peter's Coll., Camb., 23rd wrangler; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1879; legal adviser to late Lord Rosmead on his mission to Mauritius, 1886; mem. of native law comsrr.; chrmn. of mining comsrr.; mem. of House of Ass. for division of Tembuland, Cape; atty.-gen., Cape, 1898-1900; legal adviser to Transvaal adminstr., 1901-1902; atty.-gen., Transvaal, 19th June, 1902; mem. ex. and leg. couns.; mem. I.C.C.; ag. lieut.-gov., Transvaal, 1905; agent-gen. for Transvaal in London, 1907; high comsrr. in London of the Union of S. Africa, 1910.

SOLOMON, Sir Wm. Henry, Kt. Bach. (1907). B.A., Cantab.; admitted to the bar, Inner Temple, 1877; practised in Cape Colony; asst. legal adviser, Cape, 1883; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1887; pres., pres. treason court, 1900; 1st puisne judge, Transvaal, 1902; judge of sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1910.

SOUZA, Simon Isidoro de.—B. 1863; ed. at the R. Catholic gram. schl., Lagos; 1st dispatch elk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Lagos, June, 1895; acted as elk. of the legis. coun., 1900; registr. of correspondence, June, 1900; conf. elk. to gov. and elk. of leg. coun., July, 1901; ag. priv. sec. to gov., and elk. to exec. coun., Feb., 1902; in charge of Ibadan residency, Nov., 1903.
SORZANO, Tilueber—Draughtsman, crown lands office, Trinidad, 1874; first ditto, survey lept., Aug., 1881.

SOUTHORN, Wilfrid Thomas.—B. 1879; ed. Warwick schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; off. ass't. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1904; N.C. prov., May, 1905; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr., customs, Colombo, July, 1909.

SPALDING, Col. Warner Wright, C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1844; lieut., Royal Marines, 1862-1869; carried colours in operations at Simonosehie, Japan, 1864; N.S. Wales artil., 1871-1896; 2nd in cmd. of N.S.W. Soudan conting., 1885; ch. mag. Norfolk Is., 1896-1898.

SPED, Edwin Arney.—M.A., LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prize-man of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. class, tripso, 1890; 2nd cls. law tripso, Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. comsrr., Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edtn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just. N. Nigeria, 1908.

SPENCE, Frank.—B. 1864; 3rd clk. col. sec.'s off., Fiji, 1880; clk. to comsrrs. of wka., Oct., 1884; ag. priv. sec. to gov., 1884, 1885, and 1887 to 1896; sec. to consul-gen. for W. Pacific, 1889 to 1896; stip. mag., Cakandrove and Bua, 1896 to 1898; provincial inspr. and stip. mag., Cakandrove and Bua, 1899; ditto, Cakandrove, 1901; gov.'s comsrr. or Namasi and stip. mag., Navua, Jan., 1904.
SPENCE, J. B.—Med. supt. of Colombo lun. asyl., Ceylon, 1886.

SPENCE, R. O. H.—Cler. asst., P.W. dept., Br. Guiana, 16th Aug., 1886; ag. 3rd clk., P.W. dept., Aug., 1889; ag. 3rd cl. clk., treasy., Jan., 1890; gov. offr. gov't. ld. dept., Apr., 1890; clk. gov't. ld. dept., May, 1890; sec., Bartika comsrrs., June, 1891, to May, 1893; clk. dept. mines, Oct., 1892; ag. 3rd cl. clk., dept. mines, Apr., 1893; gov't. offr., dept. mines, July, 1893; J.P., May, 1894; comsrr. to admnr. oaths, June, 1894; dist. gov't. offr., dept. mines, Aug., 1896; warden, dept. mines, Nov., 1896; obt'd. certif. as sworn land surv., June, 1898; ag. ch. clk., dept. mines, Feb., 1900; obtained 1st cls. certifs. in assaying and mine survg., Camborne schl. of mines, 1902; elected assoc., Inst. of M. and M., Lond., Apr., 1902; 1st cls. offr., dept. of lands and mines, Apr., 1903; ag. asst. comsrr. of lands and mines, Apr. to Dec., 1904; 1st cls. elk., dept. of lands and mines, July, 1905; ch. elk., ditto, Oct., 1905; ag. comsrr., ditto, June to Aug., 1906.

SPENCER, Cyril Edward.—B. 1873; entd. col. ser., Cyprus, 1st Mar., 1891; in secretariat from Apr., 1892, to May, 1898; also clk. to legis. coun., Mar., 1894, to May, 1898; priv. sec. to high comsrr. on several occasions, 1898-1900; inspr., mil. pol., 1st May, 1898; ag. dist. comd., July, 1900, to Jan., 1904; apptd. in cmd. of divs. of pol., Jan., 1904; also gov. of pris. and asst. to dist. comsrr., and dep. coroner; ag. dist. comsrr., Apr. to Dec., 1905; passed in mod. Greek, higher standard, 1904; and in Turkish, lower standard, 1906; transf'd. to E. Africa, as asst. dist. comsrr., Nov., 1906; ag. supt., inland rev., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. dist. comsrr., Mombasa, from July, 1907.

SPICER, Quinten Herbert.—B. 1865; 2nd elk., col. sec.'s dept., Tobago, 1884; 1st elk., audit and registry dept., 1884; 2nd rev. offr., 1885; harbrmr. and landing waiter, 1880; supervisor of excise, 1892; inspr. of pol., 1896; in charge of prison, 1897; asst. harbrmr., Trinidad, 1899; sub-registr., sup. ct., and elk. of the peace, 1907.
SPIRE, John.—B. 1850; apptd., after exam., 3rd class messenger, C.O., Jan., 1883; 2nd class, July, 1896; Queen's mess., Nov., 1899; ret., 1911.

SPRIGG, Thk Rt. Hon. Sir John Gordon, P.C. (1897), G.C.M.G. (1902), K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1830; represented E. London in Cape assem. 1869-1904; col. sec. and premier, Feb., 1878, to May, 1881; col. treas., May, 1884, to Nov., 1886; premier, Nov., 1886; apptd. comdr. logion of honour for services in Paris Exhibn., 1889; resig. office, July, 1890; again treas., 1893, and also premier, 1896; represented the col. in London at H.M.'s Diam. Jub.; resig. premiership, Oct., 1898; again premier 1900-04; M.L.A. for E. London, 1908.

SPROULE, Percy Julian, B.A., Camb.—B. 1873; barrister-at-law (Mid.Tem.); cadet, S.S., Nov., 1895; ag. dep. regisr., sup. ct., Penang Mar., 1897; passed final in Malay, Mar., 1899; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, June, 1899; dep. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1899; ag. asst. regisr., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Sept., 1904; ag. dep. pub. pros., May, 1905; ag. sol.-gen., Penang, May-Aug., 1906; dep. pub. prosecutor, Sing., Aug., 1906; ag. solr.-gen., Penang, Mar., 1908.

STANFORD, WALTER ERNEST MORTIMER, C.B. (1900), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1850; mag.'s clk., Cape Col., 1863; mag., 1876; capt. of levies in Gcaleka war, 1877-8 (medal); mem. native laws and customs comsn., 1890-2; comdt. in war of 1880-1; on special service to Pondoland, 1884; ch. mag. Griqualand E., 1885; negotiated treaty with Pondos, 1886; sec. nat. affairs dept., 1898; ditto and ch. mag., July, 1904; mem. of S. African native affairs comsn., Sept., 1903, to Jan., 1905; holds rank of colonel in Cape colonial forces; ret., May, 1907; M.L.A. for Tembuland, 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with South Africa Bill, 1909.
STANLEY, GEOFFREY ARMSTRONG.—Ed. at Bath and City of London Schl.; G.W. rly., 1897 to 1901; asst. traffic man., Uganda rly., 23rd May, 1901.

STANLEY, HERBERT JAMES.—B. 1872; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (B.A.); priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden and Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1897-1902; asst. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Coun., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gov. gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910.

STANLEY, W. B.—Served with 1st Border regt., occupation of Crete 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (medal and 4 clasps); 2nd lieut., W. India regt., 1900; lieut., 1901; ag. adjut., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia expdn., 1901, (medal and clasp); ag. trav. comsnr., Gambia, May and June, 1901; trav. comsnr., Gambia, Aug., 1901; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Gambia, Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1902; passed course of survey, schl. of mil. engineering, Chatham, 1905; resig. comsnr., 1906; capt., 3rd Yorks regt., 1906; qualified in native language; dist. comsnr., S. Leone, 1910.

STANMORE, THE RIGHT HON. SIR ARTHUR HAMILTON GORDON, 1st Baron (creat. 1893), G.C.M.G. (1878), K.C.M.G. (1871), C.M.G. (1859).—B. 1829; priv. sec. to his father, Earl of Aberdeen, when 1st lord of the treasury, 1852 to 1855; M.P. for Beverley from July, 1854, till Mar., 1857; accompanied Mr. Gladstone on his special mission as lord high comsnr. extraord. to the Ionian Is., Nov., 1858; capt. comdt. of the 1st Aberdeenshire R.V., Feb., 1860; lieut.-gov. of N. Brunswick, Oct., 1861; gov. of Trinidad, Nov., 1866; gov. of Mauritius, 1870; ret., 1874; gov. of the new col. of Fiji, Jan., 1875; H.M.'s high comsnr. and consul-gen. for the W. Pacific, 1877; gov. of N. Zealand, 1880; gov. of Ceylon, 1883; ret., 1890.
STANNUS, HUGH STANNUS.—M.B. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1901; med. offr., Nyassaland Prot., May, 1905.

STANTON, CAMERON.—B. 1861; 3rd cls. clk., dept. of finance, Canada, 1879; promoted to 2nd cls., 1883; transf. to dept. of marine and fisheries, 1888; promoted to 1st cls., 1892; ch. clerkship, 1901; asst. dep. min., marine and fisheries, 1908.

STANTON, LIONEL WM.—B. 1843; inspr. of schls., S. Aust., 1876; asst. inspr.-gen., 1892; clmn. of bd. of insprs., 1896; inspr.-gen. of schls., 1902; sec. to min. of educn. and sec. of educn. dept., 1906.

STEADMAN, HUGH JOHN HARRY.—Ed. Weymouth Coll.; articled to borough engr., sury. of Dorchester, 1895 to 1898; asst. to 1898 to 1903; on staff of superintending engr., H.M. Breakwater, Portland, 1903 to 1905; asst. engr., P.W.D., E. Africa, 27th July, 1905.

STEEL, THOMAS MELLEPONTRE.—B. B.A. Roy. Univ., Dublin; ed. at Wesley Dublin; 2nd div. clk., nat. debt. off., Dec., transfd. to P.W.D., Ireland, 1896-1904; exam. accts., audit dept., O.R.C., Sept., 1904; examr. of accts., July, 1906.

STEEL, WALTER MATTHEW.—B. clerical asst., treasy., Br. Guiana, Feb., 5th class clk., Feb., 1886; 4th class, July, 3rd class, Apr., 1890; ag. clk.-in-charge, treasy. and savings bk., Dec., 1891, to 1893; 2nd class clk. (acctnt.), govt. land, Apr., 1896; asst. treas., So. Nigeria, July, ag. treas., Cent. or Niger Prov., 1st May, ditto, Calabar Prov., 23rd July, 1907; Lagos or W. Prov., 7th Dec., 1908; ditto, or Niger Prov., 16th June, 1909.

STEERE, CHAS. JAS. LEE.—B. 1868; prob. clk., col. sec.'s off., W. Aust., Feb., 1885; clk., Aug., 1885; ag. clk., gov.'s off., 1889; clk. of legis. comn. and clk. of parts, Dec., to June, 1901; clk. of leg. assem., June, 1901.

STEIN, JOHN.—Ed. at Christ's Coll., Finch and at Neuwilld, Germany; sec. to consulsate Germany, Sweden and Norway, and Denm. in Mauritius, May, 1889, to Dec., 1896; asst. S. of S., F.O., an asst. dist. comsnr. in H.E.H. Niger Coast Protectorate, Apr., 1897; ag. dist. comsnr. Opopo, June, 1897, to June, 1898; Bon Jan., 1899, to Jan., 1900; promoted dist. comn., Jan., 1900; asst. sec. to govt. Southern Ngr., June, 1900; ag. sec. to govt., Sept., 1900, to 1901, and from June to Nov., 1901; West Air medal with clasp, 1899; ment. in des. services in connection with Aro experi., 1901-2; ret., 1904.
STEPHEN, CHELVUM.—Ind. interp. to mag., Durban, Natal, Jan., 1875; clk., Ind. interp. to res. mag., Umlazi div., o. Durban, Mar., 1875; Tamil interp., sup. ct.

STEPHEN, GUY NEVILLE.—Ed. Paris, seilles, London; M.R.C.S., Eng., 1881; lic. of medicine, France, 1882; ag. house surg. Linco. hosp.; ditto, asst. med. offr., Middlesex asylum (Colney Hatch); surg. to the consuls Norway and Sweden, Marseilles, 1881; sur. the Br. Consulate and Seamen's Home, March, 1882; dist. med. offr., Nicosia, Cyprus, 1883; offr., central prison, gen. and ophthalmic hos. med. offr. of health, Nicosia; pres. of the municipality, Nicosia, 1889; ag. ch. med. offr., 1889 and 1890; surg. to Smyrna Hosp., 1894.
STEPHEN, SIR MATTHEW HENRY, K.T. R. (1904).—B. 1828; mem. legis. assem., N.S., 1860-71; judge, sup. ct., May, 1887; ret. bench, 1903.
STEPHEN, WILLIAM WILBERFORCE.—Clerk secretariat, N.S. Wales, 1852; priv. sec. to premiers, 1856-58; clk., land dept., 1858; sec. for lands, 1870; sec., atty.-gen.'s de., 1880.

STEPHENS, THOMAS NOAKES.—Jun. offr., Australia; customs, 1864; clk., marine bd., boarding offr., customs, 1866; asst. landw. 1867; confill. clk., 1870; ch. clk., treasy., N. marine bd., 1877; sec. of customs, 1889; univ. 1890; collr. of customs, regsr. of shipping, pr. of distilleries and excise, pres. of marine 894; is a J.P.; transf'd. with cust. dept. v. of Commonwealth of Aust., 1st Jan., collr. of cust., S. Aust., 1907.

EPHENSON, ALBERT EDWARD.—B. 1864; and A. dept., Feb., 1884; local auditor, Aug., 1888, also ag. local auditor, G. Coast, 1888, to Aug., 1889; returned to E. and A. and apptd. to col. audit branch, Jan., 1891; n charge of acts., May, 1893; asst. supt., 1897; apptd. sen. clk. on amalgamation 1. audit branch with E. and A. dept., 1st 1906; dir. of col. audit, C.O., 1910; ed in preparation of colonial financial auctions, 1889, for which reed. thanks of S.

EVENS, PERCIVAL, Assoc. M.I.C.E.—B.; asst. engrnr. govt. rlys., Trinidad, 1874; engrnr., Couva extension rly., 1878; res. r. in charge of construction, San Fernando Guaiacara rly., 1879; 1st asst. engrnr., P.W., Jan., 1885; has acted as asst. dir. of P.W., 6, 8, 9, and 90; engrnr., N. div., P.W.D., 1892; 1st engrnr. in charge of rds. and bdges., V.D., Jan., 1894; engrnr. in charge of rds. and rds. and bdges. dept., Jan., 1895; asst. dir. pub. wks. and sen. div. engrnr., Jan., 1897; acted as D.P.W. with seat in legis. coun., 4, 5, 8, 9, 1900, 2, 5, 6, and 7.

TEVENSON, MALCOLM.—B. 1878; ed. Trin., Dublin (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 1901; off. asst. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, 1901; Badulla, May, 1904; addl. asst.-govt. agt., N. Prov., and addl. dist. judge, &c., unar in connection with the Pearl Fishery, 1906; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Apr., 1906; unar, Aug., 1908; asst. land attlmt. offr., 1910.
TEVENSON, W. B.—B. 1874; entd. Bahamas ser., 1903; Out Island comsrr. (4th div.), 1903.

STEWARD, MAJ.-GEN. EDWARD HARDING, M.G. (1887).—B. 1835; entered army, Roy. grns., Oct., 1854; employed from 1865 to 9 in the fortification branch of the W.O.; vices lent in 1866 to 1869, and in 1877 to the S. Australian govt., and in 1877 till 9 to the govt. of Victoria. In charge of defence of the Natal frontier in 1879, and ndt. of Natal and mem. of exec. coun. of col. in 1880; services lent to govt. of Victoria d N. S. Wales in 1883; and in 1884, on retire- ment from the army, became military adviser to agts.-gen. of Victoria, N. S. Wales, Queens- ld, S. Australia, N. Zealand, and Tasmania.

STEWARD, GEORGE.—B. 1866; elk., Tas- manian govt. rlys., June, 1892; ch. elk. and tnt., educn. dept., July, 1893; sec. to premier Tasmania, Aug., 1894; elk. to ex. coun., Jan., 95; and sec. to defence comttee, June, 1896, in dition; under sec. for Tasmania, Jan., 1896; and inspr. of explosives and magazines, 1st Sept., 97, in addition; raised and commanded first company of mtd. infantry in Tasmania, and holds rank of captain; ch. elk., dept. of external affairs, commonwealth govt., 18th May, 1901; off. sec. to iv.-gen., and sec. to fed. ex. coun., 24th Dec., 1902; apptd. to command No. 5 squadron, Aust. flight Horse, 29th Jan., 1907.
STEWARD, GRAHAM.—Asst. survr., rly. staff, ignr.-in-ch.'s dept., S. Aust., 1870; survr., 73; suptdg. survr., 1883; engrnr.-in-ch., 1909.

STEWARD, MAJ.-GEN. SIR ROBERT MAC- REGOR, K.C.B. (1902).—B. 1842; served in Hazara campaign, 1868; Afghan war, 1878-9; Soudan, 1885; A.D.C. to Queen Victoria, 1887- 97; late commdr., R.A., southn. dist., Portsmouth; gov. of Bermuda, 1904-07.

STEWART, ROBT. PETTER.—Exhibtnr., Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; ed. at St. John's Coll., Camb.; ag. asst. mast., Queen's Coll., B. Guiana, 1892-93 and 1893-94; clk., immigr. dept., 1894; seconded to govt. secretariat, 1897-98, and 1900-02; passed govt. exam. in Hindustani, July, 1903; immigr. agt., Dec., 1906.

STIGAND, ALMAR GORDON.—2nd clk. to res. comsrr. for Bech. Prot. at Mafeking, 1898; 1st clk. and registr. to res. comsrr., 1899; served in town guard during siege of Mafeking, Oct., 1899, to May, 1900; clk. of ct. and clk. to asst. comsrr., Gaberones, 1902; J.P., Bech. Prot., 1903; ag. asst. comsrr., Jan. to Feb., 1904; 4th Dec., 1905, to 28th Feb., 1906, and 11th May, 1906, to 29th Aug., 1906; asst. res. mag. for Southern dist., Bechuanaland Prot., 15th Jan., 1907; passed Cape civ. unar. lower law exam., 1909; seconded for ser. as res. mag., N'gamiland, 1910.
STILES, ALFRED.—B. 1850; apptd., after exam., messenger C.O., Dec., 1875; Queen's mess., Aug., 1882; asst. off. keeper, 17th Feb., 1900; recd. coronation medal, 1902; off. keeper, 22nd May, 1907.
STILWELL, E. R.—Sarawak civ. ser., roads and bridges dept., Mar., 1888; asst. res., Baram, Oct., 1894; of Sadong, May, 1895; res., 2nd class, Jan., 1897; ditto, Bau, Upper Sarawak, 15th Oct., 1900.
STIRLING, SIR JOHN LANCELOT, K.C.M.G. (1909); KT. BACH. (1902), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1849; mem. legis. coun., S. Aust., 1891; chief sec., 1899; pres. legis. coun., 1901.
STIRLING, RIGHT REV. W. H., D.D.—Bishop of the Falklands.

STOCKDALE, FRANK ARTHUR, B.A., F.L.S.—B. 1883; ed. Wisbech and Magdalen Coll., Camb.; Holmes exhibnr., Magdalen Coll., Camb., 1901; B.A. (1st cls. Nat. Sc. Trip.) 1904; mycologist and lecturer in agric. science, Impl. dept. of agric. for the West Indies, May, 1905; asst. dir., dept. of science and agric., and govt. botanist, British Guiana, Aug., 1908; is dep. chmn. of bd. of agric., B.G.; author of several papers and articles relating to fungus diseases of W. Indian crops, agric., and the breeding and selection of sugar cane seedlings.

STOCKER, JAMES EDWARD.—Cape Mounted Rifles, S. Africa, 1897-1902; S. African war, 1899-1902; medal (King's and Queen's), clasps, Transvaal, Free State, and Cape Col.; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 27th May, 1904.

STOKER, WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.—Called to the bar, Mid. Temp.; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Mar., 1898; mem. fed. and island ex. and leg. couns.; ag. 1st puisne judge conjointly with atty.- gen., Nov. to Dec., 1901; atty.-gen., Barbados, July, 1902; chmn., Barbados quarantine coman., 1902-3; K.C., Barbados, 3rd Apr., 1903; M.L.C., Apr. to Oct., 1903; ag. pres. of educn. bd., May to June, 1903; ag. ch. just., 1st Aug. to 30th Oct., 1903; deleg. for Barbados, and elec. pres. at Brit. W. India conf. on quarantine, Apr. to May, 1904; M.L.A. for St. Michael's, 4th July, 1905; re-elected, 23rd July, 1906; chmn. of spec. comttee. on liquor licensing system, and as to tobacco industry, 1906-7; pres. of W. Indian cent. quarantine authority, May, 1907; puisne judge, S. Nigeria, 31st Aug., 1907; ag. chief just., E. and C. Provs., Sept., 1907, to 31st Mar., 1908; ag. atty.-gen., 1st June to 14th Sept., 1908, and in Nov., 1909; ag. ch. just., 1st Dec., 1909.

STONE, Sir E. A., K.T. BACH. (1902).—B. 1844; clk. to atty.-gen., W. Australia, 1860; called to bar, 1865; clk. to legis. coun., 1870; nominee mem. of ditto, 1880-2; crown solr., 1882; puisne judge, 1884; acted as ch. just., 1880, 1881, 1887, and 1889; ch. just., 1901; admstr., 1901-2; retired from bench, 1906; lieut.-gov. of W. Aust., 7th May, 1906.

STONE, E. C. M.—Clerk to regir., Trinidad, 1875; ch. clk., 1878; clk. of complaint ct., 1878; ch. clk. to regir., sup. ct., 1880; regir., ct. of survey, 1883; ag. regir. sup. ct., 1880, 1881, 1883, and Apr., 1884, to July, 1885, and June to Sept., 1886; ch. clk. to regir.-gen., Sept., 1887; dep. regir.-gen., Oct., 1889; ag. regir.-gen., 1892-93; is a commnr. of affidavits, and sup. regir., births and deaths for Port of Spain.
STONE, ROBERT GEORGE.—Asst. paymaster, 1st King's African rifles, E. Africa Prot., 1st June, 1906.
STORDY, ROBERT JOHN.—Uganda transport service, 1st Jan., 1898; chief veterinary offir., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 1st Apr., 1901.
STORR, IRA WILLIAM.—B. 1847; entd. ser. of the old govt. of Canada at Quebec, 1864; 3rd cls. clk., dept. of the S. of S., Ottawa, 1878; 1st cls. clk., 1891; ch. clk., 1905.
STORRS, FRANCIS JOHN TOWNSEND.—Apptd. asst. collr., B. C. Africa Prot., July, 1899; 2nd cls. dist. res., Apr., 1906.

STOUT, THE HON. SIR ROBERT, K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1844; admitted a barrister of sup. ct., N. Zealand; entered the prov. coun. of Otago, N.Z., 1872; prov. solr., 1873; elected to the gen. assem., 1875; atty.-gen., Mar., 1878, to June, 1879; min. of lands, etc., for immigr., 1878; pres. of Dunedin Freethought Soc.; prime min. and atty.-gen., and min. for educn., 1884-87; fellow and chancellor of N. Z. Univ.; chief justice of N.Z., 1899; mem. of coun. of Victoria Coll.

STRACHAN, W. HENRY W., C.M.G. (1902), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), F.L.S.—Jun. res. med. offir., pub. hosp., Kingston, Jamaica, 1882; sen. res. med. offir., 1885; sen. med. offir., 1892; mem. of bd. of govrs., Inst. of Jamaica, 1892; chief med. offir. of Lagos, Dec., 1897; acted as col. sec., Dec., 1899, to Mar., 1900; P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906.

STRACHEY, CHARLES.—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. jun. clk., F.O., 2nd Apr., 1885; 1st cl. jun. clk., 1st May, 1885; sec. Uganda rly. comttee., 10th Sept., 1895; 1st cl. clk., C.O., 20th Nov., 1898; principal clk., 1st Jan., 1907.

STRANGE, WM. LUMISDEN, M.I.C.E.—B. 1857; served in P.W.D., Bombay, 1879 to 1901; under-sec., P.W.D., Govt. of India, July, 1901; suptdng. engrnr., 2nd cl., Dec., 1906; seconded as dir. of irrigtn. and water supply, Transval, 25th Apr., 1903; author of "Indian Storage Reservoirs with Earthen Dams"; recd. Telford premium, Inst. C.E., 1897.
STRATHAIRN, GEORGE CECIL, M.B. Ch. (Edin.).—Med. offir., E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 20th July, 1903.

STRATHCONA AND MOUNT ROYAL, LORD, 1ST BARON (U.K. creat., 1897), SIR DONALD A. SMITH, G.C.M.G. (1896), G.C.V.O. (1908); K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1820; Gov. of Hudson's Bay Territory; special commr., Red River, 1869; mem. of Canadian parlmt., 1871, dir. of Canadian Pacific Rly., 1880; high commr. for Canada, 1896; mem. of Pacific Cable Comtees., 1896 and 1899; Lord Rector of Aberdeen Univ., 1899; chancellor, Aberdeen Univ., 1903.

STRATTON, GEORGE BERNARD.—Ed. wick and Durham Univ.; cadet, S. S., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec.; ag. supt. govt. printing office, Jan., 1901; mag., Sing., Mar., 1901; ag. supt. of ed., Penang, Dec., 1901; on spec. mission to Weh, Jan., 1904; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr.; ag. supt. of educn., Penang, Nov., 1904; asst. col. sec., Apr., 1905; ag. asst. col. sec., clk. of couns., Sept., 1905; dist. offir., Nov., Oct., 1905, but cont. to act as asst. col. sec., 2nd asst. col. sec., 24th Jan., 1906; 2nd Sing. vol. artily., July, 1906; lieut., 1909.

STRAWBRIDGE, WILLIAM, I.S.O. (1)—Entered survey and crown lands dept., S. Aus. as cadet, June, 1862; after several promotions apptd. examr. of licensed survr.'s work and man., June, 1872; and ch. draftsman, Apr.; dep. survr.-gen., July, 1896; survr.-gen., 1894; chmn. of pastoral, central past. central land bds.
STREET, PHILIP WHISTLER.—Puissne N.S. Wales; judge in bankruptcy and jurisdictions, Feb., 1907.

STRICKLAND, SIR GERALD, COUNT CATENA, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1898), 1861; ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; C.U.R.V., and elected mem. coun. of govt., 1886; B.A. and LL.B., Trin. Coll., honours law trips; attended col. conf. behalf of Malta; called to the bar, Inn. Temp., and pres. cholera comm., Malta, 1887; thn. by govt. for negotiations on Imp. vote of See of Malta; unoffic. mem., ex. comm., asst. sec. to govt., 1888; vice-pres., bd. of pres., comttee. of privileges, ch. sec. to govt. major, Royal Malta militia, which he estab., 1889; re-organized Malta rly., 1891; breakwater, 1894; pres. coun. of Malta, 1900; chmn. mil. and civ. drainage bd.; comttee. on Malta naval reserves; gov. and exec. in-chief, Leeward Is., 1902; estab. central inst. for sugar and cotton in Antigua, 1903; Tasmania, 23rd July, 1904; gov., W. Aust., 6th Apr., 1909.
STRICKLAND, R. B., M.A. (Dublin).—b. of schools, Jamaica, 1st Mar., 1894, to 28th Apr., 1902; ditto, Ceylon, 29th Aug., 1902.
STRONACH, F. L.—Land purchase P.W.D., W. Aust., 1896; under sec. for wks., May, 1905.

STUART, ALEXR.—B. 1861; ed. St. And Univ.; regist. of impts. and expts., Sept., 1890; temporarily attached to H.M. toma, and trade marks branch of the patent London, 1897-8; comm. correspondent for and F.M.S. to intell. branch of B. of T., 1901.

STUART, HON. CHARLES ALLAN, B.A. (1)—B. 1864; ed. High schl., Strathroy, Ont., and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ont., 1896; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1898; elec. first prov. legis., Alberta, 1905; judge of sup. ct., N.W.T., 1906; judge of supreme ct., Alberta, 1907; chancellor of univ. of Alberta, 1908.

STUART, JAMES.—Ed. St. John's C. Hurstpierpoint; matri. Lond. and Cape medal, Natal, Eng. lit. exam., 1891; clk. G.F. Pietermaritzburg, Feb., 1888; clk. and intell. M., Eshowe, May, 1888; 1st clk. and interp. to s. comsnr. and ch. mag., Zululand, Feb., 1889; acted on various occasions as sec., res. comsnr. and ch. mag. sct., Zululand; interp. to Br. comsnr., Swaziland, 1894 to 1895; and in charge of Swazi reputations to Capetown and England in 1894; elected as Br. consul, Swaziland, 1895; R.M., Inga numa dist., Zululand, 1895; mag., Lower Tugela div., 1899; ast. mag., Durban div., 1901; mag. of colony, 1902; ast. sec. for native affairs, and sec. to the coun. for native affairs, 1st July, 1909.

STUBBS, REGINALD EDWARD.—B. 1876; ed. at Radley and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner); 1st cl. classical mods., 1897; 1st lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1900; ag. ast. cls. clk., May, 1907; 1st cls. clk., Dec., 1910; visited S. Sttlnts., F.M.S. and Hong Kong to inquire into question of revision of civ. ser. salaries, 1910-11; edited "Lucas' Historical Geography of the British Colonies," vol. i., 2nd edition, 1906.

SUETER, EDWARD BEAUMONT FRANK.—B. 1880; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; cadet, Seylon civ. ser., Nov., 1903; off. ast. to gov't. gt., Colombo, Apr., 1905; ast. govt. agt., Kegalla, Nov., 1907; dist. judge, Kegalla, Feb., 1908; pol. mag., Kandy, Jan., 1910.

SULLIVAN, WILLIAM WILFRED, K.C.—Born P.E.I., 1843; called to the bar 1867; elected to legislature in 1872, and has continuously represented the same constituency; created a Q.C. by the gov't. of P.E.I., 1876, and by the gov't. gen. of Canada 1879; is a dep. judge in the admity. ct., was a mem. of the exec. coun., holding the office of regis.-gen., Apr., 1873, till Sept., 1876; leader of the Opposition in the legislature in 1877; prime min. and atty. and advoc.-gen. in 1879, has been a deleg. representing the prov. gov't. on several occasions in Canada, and was a deleg. to England in 1886, to confer with the impl. gov't. regarding the terms of confederation between P.E.I. and Canada; ch. just., P.E.I., 1889.
SURMON, WILLIAM BOWKER.—Sub.-inspr., Bechuana land Prot. pol., 1897; ast. res. mag. in Western Kalahari, 1907-1908; inspr. Bech. Prot. police, 1909.

SUTER, WM. CHAS.—Ast. master, govt. English schls., Singapore, 22nd Nov., 1884; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, 1st July, 1888; shorthand reporter to leg. coun. in addition to other duties, 1st July, 1897.

SUTHERLAND, A. R.—M.A., Aberdeen Univ.; Science mast., Glen Urquhart H.G. schl., 1903-5; ast. mast., Campbelltown gram. schl., 1905-6; ast. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1906; lecturer in biology, Hong Kong Coll. of med., 1909-1910; ag. ast. master, pol. schl., 1909.
SUTHERLAND, GEORGE.—B. 1877; ast. man., Barbuda, Leeward Is., 1st Oct., 1908; ag. man. from 15th June, 1910.
SUTHERLAND, REAY MACKAY.—Sub.-inspr., Bechuana land Prot. pol., 1906.

SUTHERLAND, HON. ROBERT FRANKLIN, K.C., B.A.—B. 1859; ed. pub. and high schls., Newmarket and Windsor, Ontario, and Toronto and Western Unive.; called to the Bar, 1886; K.C., 1898; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1900; re-elected 1904 and 1908; speaker of H. of C., 1905-1908; judge, High Court of Just., Ontario, 1909.

SUTHERLAND, WM.—Apptd. to Fiji civ. ser., Jan., 1880; clk. recr.-gen.'s office, Sept., 1880; clk., registr.-gen., Jan., 1882; stip. mag. of the col., Apr., 1883; ag. collr. of cust., Sept., 1897; ag. recr.-gen., Mar., 1898; native comsnr., 1898; ag. col. sec. and recr.-gen., Jan., 1903.

SUTTOR, THE HON. SIR FRANCIS B., KT. BACH. (1903).—B. 1839; postmr.-gen., N.S. Wales, Aug., 1880, to Nov., 1881; min. of pub. instruction, 1881 to 1883, and again, 1891-4; representative of col. at col. confce., Ottawa, 1894; pres. of legis. coun. of N.S. Wales, June, 1903.

SWAIN, ALFRED WILLIAM.—Clerical ast., regis.'s off., Barbados, 1879; jun. clk., G.P.O., Br. Guiana, June, 1881; 3rd cls. clk., Jan., 1883; 2nd cls., Dec., 1889; 1st cls., Apr., 1900; ch. clk., Sept., 1900; ag. postmr.-gen., May, 1901, to May, 1902; and Aug. to Dec., 1904; postmr.-gen., 18th May, 1907; 2nd lieut. Br. Guiana volunteers, 1889, ditto militia, 1891; lieut., 1892; capt., 1895; maj., 1900; ag. comdt. of local forces, July, 1901, to Feb., 1902; Hythe certif., 1893; Colonial and Aux. forces long service medal; recd. thanks of govt. on retiring in 1902, and retains rank of Major.
SWAIN, ARTHUR CLAUDE.—2nd clk., crown lands dept., Br. Guiana, Oct., 1879; ast. comsry., May, 1882; dist. ditto, July, 1881; is a J.P.

SWAIN, GEORGE LLEWELLYN DOUGLAS.—B. 1858; 1st clk. and bookkeeper, pol. dept., British Guiana, Apr., 1882; inspr. of pol., May, 1884; ch. inspr., Mar., 1892; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., July to Sept., 1893; on special service with Mr. McTurk, Feb., 1895, to take over Uruan from Venezuelans, remaining in command there till July, 1895; volr. in Ashanti expdn., Nov., 1896, to Mar., 1896; mentioned in despatches (star); two months' training with R.I.C. at Dublin, 1890; Hythe, P.S., and 1st cl. ambulance certifs.; ag. dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1898, to Jan., 1899; promoted ch. county inspr., and 2nd in command, 18th Nov., 1899; ag. inspr.-gen., 10th May to 21st Aug., 1900; servd. on comsn. apptd. to inquire into admstrm. of poor law relief, 1900; ag. inspr.-gen. of police, and col. comdt. of militia, 8th May to 3rd Sept., 1902; deputy inspr.-gen. of police, Trinidad, 30th September, 1903; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and comdt. of local forces with local rank of lieut.-col. and seat in exec. and legis. couns., 7th May to 10th Nov., 1903; ditto, with seat in legis. coun., 15th Apr. to 16th Sept., 1907; inspr.-gen. of constab. and comdt. local forces, 17th Sept., 1907.

SWAN, ROBERT ABRAHAM.—B. 1849; matric. at Madras Univ., Dec., 1868; passed special law test prescribed for uncoventional supts. and asst.-supts. of pol.; 2nd class inspr. of pol. in the Cuddapah dist., Madras, May, 1870; in Aug., 1871, 1st class; May, 1874, passed in Telugu; Oct., 1876, special ast. to sub-collr. and joint mag. of Cuddapah for famine relief duty, and in same month apptd. mag. with 1st class powers, and placed in charge of the Kadri and Madnapully Taluqs of Cuddapah dist.; in Feb., 1877, stip. mag. Mauritius; Jan., 1878, stip. mag. of Flaqc; and in Aug., 1879, Grand Port and Savanne; mem. of comsn. to frame reguls. under "labour ordinance of 1878"; member of Lincoln's Inn, 1882; called to the bar, 1887; stip. mag. Br. Guiana, Nov., 1883; in July, 1884, sent on special duty to Madras to organise an emigrn. agency for Br. Guiana; sheriff, Essequibo co., Dec., 1887; chmn. lands titles inquiry comsn., Feb., 1890; a revising barrister under Br. Guiana Constitn. Order, 1891; sheriff of Berbice and mag. New Amsterdam, 1892; acted as pol. imag., Georgetown, Feb., 1890, and Oct., 1894, to Apr., 1895; mem. of comm. on poor-relief, 6th Apr., 1900; chairman of ditto, 7th Sept., 1900; appt. as pol. mag., Georgetown, confirmed by S. of S., 1st Oct., 1900; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1st Oct., 1900; puisne judge, Trinidad, 3rd Aug., 1903; ag. ch. just., Trinidad and Tobago, 7th July to 12th Oct., 1904, and 26th June to 30th Oct., 1906; 1st puisne judge, Oct., 1906.

SWAYNE, CHARLES RICHARD, C.M.G.(1906).—Stip. mag., Fiji; inspr. of native taxes, 1876; stip. mag. and commr. of sup. ct., Lau prov., 1878; sent to Kadavu with special authority in native matters, 1879 and 1880; inspr. of Indian and Polynesian labourers, Lau prov., 1883; stip. mag., reg'ar, and commr. of sup. ct. at Levuka, 1885; returned to Lau, 1886; asst. native commr., Lau, 1888; ag. Br. res., Gilbert and Ellice Is., and special judicial commr. for trial of certain cases, Oct., 1893, to Nov., 1895; stip. mag., Rewa, and commr., Naitasiri, 1898; ag. native commr., and mem. exec. coun., 29th July, 1901, to 11th Sept., 1902; commr., Naitasiri, Sept., 1902; ag. commr., Rewa, 27th May to 31st Dec., 1903; offl. mem. legis. coun., 7th Apr., 1905; ret. 1906.

SWAYNE, COLONEL ERIC JOHN EAGLES, K.C.M.G. (1910).—C.B.—Joined Indian staff corps; entd. army, 1883; served in Burmah, 1886-87 (medal with clasp); spec. duty, Somaliland reconnaissance, 1890-92; served Uganda Mutiny and Jubaland, 1898 (medal with clasp); commanded Somaliland field force, 1901-2 and 1902-3 (brevet, medal and clasp); O.C. troops, Somaliland, 1904, with local rank brig.-gen.; intell. branch, headqrs., India, 1902-3 (McGregor medallist); commr., comdr.-in-chief and consul-gen., Somaliland Prot., 1st Mar., 1902; commr. and comdr.-in-chief, ditto, 23rd June, 1904; gov., B. Honduras, 21st Mar., 1906.

SWEENEY, GEORGE WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—Asst. mast. Boys' Model Primary Schi., Pietermaritzburg, Natal, Feb., 1887; house mast., Maritzburg Coll., 1st Aug., 1888; resig. 30th Sept., 1895; clerical asst., col. office, 30th Oct., 1896; 3rd cl. clk., crown sol'r's office, 1st Feb., 1897; 2nd cls. clk., atty.-gen.'s office, 1st July, 1898; accnting. offr., atty.-gen.'s dept., 1st July, 1898; apptd. ch. clk. of the legis. assem., 1st Feb., 1901; sec. to party reprsntn. comm., Dec., 1903; law lecturer to Natal law soc., June, 1904; has acted as sec., law dept., and as asst. under sec.
SWEENEY, J. W.—Asst. traffic man., Uganda rly., Aug., 1898.

SWEET-ESCOTT, SIR E. B., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1857; ed. Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and at Balliol Coll., Oxon.; exhibitioner, Worcestershire Coll., Oxon., June, 1876; 2nd class classical mods., June, 1878; 3rd class in mod. hist. finals, June, 1880; B.A., July, 1880; classical prof. at the Roy. Coll., Mauritius, June, 1881; prec. writer, col. sec.'s office, Feb., and 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1886; ag. col. sec., 1889; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Mar., 1893; admst. the govt. of that col., Apr. to Nov., 1893; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1894; admst. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1895, again in 1897; ag. 1st class clk., C.O., Jan., 1898; admstr. of the Seychelles Is., Aug., 1899; assumed govt., 20th Nov., 1899; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Oct., 1903; gov., B. Honduras, 15th Apr., 1904; gov., Leeward Is., 30th Jan., 1906.

SWETTENHAM, SIR F. A. THELSTANE, G.C.M.G. (1909), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1850; cadet, S. Stiltsm., July, 1870; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1872; asst. collr. of land rev. for P. and Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1872; collr. of rev., July, 1873; J.P. and mag. and commr. of requests, Penang, May, 1874; sent on commissions to Perak in Jan., June, and July, 1874; asst. res., Selangor, Dec., 1874; gazetted temporarily as res. of Selangor, 22nd Oct., 1875; took charge of the residency in Perak, the murder of the res., Nov., 1875; dep. comm. Perak, Nov., 1875; mentioned in despatches; asst. col. sec. for native states, Mar., 1876; col. sec., July, 1881; Br. res., Selangor, 1882; commr. for S. S. and Malay States Exhibition, 1883-84; ag. Br. res., Perak, 1884, to Jan., 1886; exec. commr. for S. S. Malay States, Col. and Ind. Exhibition, 1886; res., Perak, 1889; res.-gen., Malacca States, 1896; admst. govt. S. S., Feb., 1901; gov., Perak, 1904; mem. of royal comm. on Mauritius, 1909.

SWETTENHAM, SIR J. A., K.C.M.G. (1892), C.M.G. (1892).—B. 1846; ed. Clare Coll.; scholarship, May, 1867; writer, Ceylon service; ag. pol. mag., Harrisspatu, Feb., 1870; pol. Kayts, Sept., 1870, continuing to act at Harrisspatu; ag. asst. govt. agt., Galle, Dec., 1871; landing and tide survr., Galle, June, 1872; collr. of customs, Jaffna, Oct., 1872, to coll. to act at Galle; ag. dist. judge, Matara, 1873; 2nd asst. col. sec., and clk. of the coun., 1876; rec.-gen., Cyprus, 1883; audt., Ceylon, 1891; col. sec., S. S., 1895; admst. June to Aug., 1895, from Mar. to Dec., 1896; from Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; gov., Br. Gen., 1901; govr., Jamaica, 14th July, 1904; ret. 1907.

SWINBURNE, HON. GEORGE, C.E.—B. M.I.A. for Hawthorn, Victoria, since 1892; mem. of pub. acct. comm., 1902-3; mem. Hawthorn comm., 1898-1904; mayor, 1904; min. of water supply, Victoria, since Apr., 1904; also min. of agric. since Nov., 1904.
SWINDELL, REV. FRANK GUTHRIE, D.D. (Oxon.).—Chap., Selangor, May, 1902; chap., Malacca, June, 1906; ag. col. chap., Singapore, Apr., 1907, to Jan., 1908.

SYDNEY, LORD ARCHBISHOP OF MONTREAL, JOHN CHARLES WRIGHT, B.A., M.A., D.D. Formerly curate of Kilworth-Beauchamp, 1888; Bradford, 1888-1893; lect., 1893-1895; vicar of Ulverston, 1893-1895; St. George's, Leeds, 1895-1904; can. res. of Manchester Cathedral, 1904-1909; rector of St. George's, Hulme, Manchester, 1904-1909; chaplain to the Duke of Manchester, 1904-1909; archdeacon of Manchester, 1909; consecrated Lord Archbishop Sydney, 24th Aug., 1909; Primate of Australia, 1910; author of "Thoughts on Modern Church Life and Work."

SYER, WM. CHAVALLIER.—B. 1873; ed. Eastbourne; qualified at asch. of musk., Hertford, Feb., 1901; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1901; ag. dist. commr., Degema, Jan., 1902; asst. transport offr., Aro field force, No. column, 1901-1902 (medal with clasp); ag. dist. commr., Degema, July, 1903, to Jan., 1904; polit. offr., Ekpaflia field force, No. 2 column, Oct.-Dec., 1904; promoted dist. commr., Ist April, 1905; dist. commr., Akpiko, Aug. to Oct., 1905; polit. offr., Abakaliki, Oct., 1905, to Jan., 1906; dist. commr., Degema, Jan. to May, 1906; dist. commr., Abeokuta, May to Sept., 1906; dist. commr., Sapele, Feb. to June, 1907; ag. prov. sec. and ag. asst. prov. commr., Cent. Prov.

TARRANT, HUMPHREY MILNER.—Civ. servant, dept. of civ. instr., N.S. Wales, 1892-96; clk., Uganda Prot., 1897; in charge of transport at Wakoli, Busoga, 1898; in charge of Luba's station, 1899-1900; ag. collr., Busoga and Bukedi, 1900; ag. collr., Toro, 1901; asst. treas., 1902; 2nd asst. treas., 1904; 1st asst. treas., 1909; ag. dep. treas., Uganda Prot., from June, 1909, to Jan., 1910; and from May to Dec., 1910; mutiny medal with 2 clasps (Uganda, 1897-98).

TASCHEREAU, RIGHT HON. SIR H. E., KT. BACH. (1902), K.C., LL.D., P.C. (1904).—B. 1836 puisne judge, sup. ct., Quebec, 1871; puisne judge, supreme ct. of Canada, 1878; ch. just. of Canada, 1902-6; mem. of privy coun., 1904.
TASMANIA, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. JOHN EDWARD MERCER, D.D., OXON.—B. 1856; rector of St. Michael, Manchester, 1889-1896; of Gorton, Manchester, 1897-1902; bishop of Tasmania since 1902.
TATE, FRANK, I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; director of education, Victoria, Apr., 1902.

TATE, HARRY RUSSELL.—Ed. at Sherborne schl.; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 16th Dec., 1897; offr. in charge, E. Africa Transport, Ashanti Field Force, Aug., 1900; transport offr., Ogaden Punitive Force, Jan., 1901; collr., 8th May, 1901; Ashanti medal, 1900; Jubaland medal, 1901.
TAUBMAN-GOLDIE, P.C. (1898). THE RT. HON. SIR GEORGE DASHWOOD, K.C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1846; founder and dep. chmn. of the Royal Niger Co.; chmn., 1895.

TAVERNER, HON. SIR JOHN WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1909).—B. 1854; mem. of Swan Hill Shire coun., Victoria, 1879; twice pres. and resig. in 1889; M.L.A. for Donald and Swan Hill, 1898 to 1904; mem. of first rlyw. standing comttee., min. of agric., comntr., of pub. wks., and vice-pres. of bd. of lands and wks., 1894-1899, min. of lands and agric., and pres. of bd. of lands and wks., 1902 to 1904; agent-gen. for Victoria in London, Feb., 1904; re-apptd. ditto, Feb., 1907.
TAYLOR, ARTHUR WM. FORBES.—B. 1857; 4th clk., treas., Natal, June, 1878; 3rd clk., Apr., 1879; 2nd elk., July, 1881; 1st elk., audit office, Mar., 1884; inspr., audit dept., 1897; ch. inspr., 1903.

TAYLOR, BASIL REGINALD HAMILTON, F.R.A.S.—B. 1865; entd. R.N. 1878; served in Egyptian war, 1882 (medal and clasp, Khedive's bronze star); lieut., 1888; served on Mediterranean, N. America, and W. India, China, and home stations; res. comm., 1898; rejoined R.N. as comdr. (emergency list), 1906; asst. harbmr., Hong Kong, July, 1899; ag. harbmr., Mar., 1900, to June, 1901, Sept., 1903, to Feb., 1914, and Aug. to Nov., 1905; M.L.C., June, 1900, to June, 1901, Sept., 1903, to Feb., 1904, and Aug. to Nov., 1905; ag. A.S.P. in addition, Oct., 1904, to Nov., 1905; harbmr., marine mag., emigrn. and cust. offr., supt. of imports and exports, regist. of shipping, supt., mercantile marine off., agent of C.I.B., B. of T., and fiscal authority under Brussels Sugar Convention, Mar., 1907; M.L.C., Sept., 1907, to Sept., 1908.

TAYLOR, EDWIN.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., elk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., Jan., 1901; served with I.Y. in S. African war, 1901-02 (medal and four clasps); re-apptd. to 2nd div., and assigned to B. of T., 1902; clk., P.W.D., B.C. Africa Prot., July, 1906; treasy. ast., Uganda, Jan., 1909.
TAYLOR, FRANCIS BRYANT OLUDELE.—Ed. at C.M.S. Collegiate schl., Lagos; 3rd cls. certif., Lond. Coll. of Preceptors, 1903; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., Lagos, Feb., 1907.

TAYLOR, FRANK.—Architectural ast., P.W.D., Cape Town, 3rd Nov., 1909, to 8th Jan., 1901; on active serv., lieut. in irregular corps, 8th Jan., 1901, to 7th Apr., 1902; chief draughtsman, P.W.D., O.R.C., 18th Apr., 1902; ch. architect, 1st July, 1905.

TAYLOR, JOHN.—B. 1863; entd. Royal Navy, 1883; served in Soudan war, 1884-5 (medal and Khedive's bronze star); impl. prison serv., Lond., Dec., 1887, to June, 1897; ch. warder, Belize pris., B. Hond., July, 1897; keeper of prisons, 1898; ag. dist. comsnr., Toledo, 1st Apr. to 5th Sept., 1905; ditto, Stann Creek, 12th Jan. to 29th May, 1906; ditto, El Cayo, 6th July, 1906, to 15th Apr., 1907; ditto, Stann Creek, 19th June to 17th July, 1907; ditto, Orange Walk, 18th July to 4th Sept., 1907; ditto, Stann Creek, from 6th Sept. to 30th Dec., 1907; ditto, Orange Walk, 7th Jan. to 13th Oct., 1908; ditto, Belize, offl. recr. in bank., and offl. adminstr., 23rd Oct., 1908, to 22nd May, 1909; J.P. for the colony, 1908; ag. dist. comsnr., Stann Creek, 12th July to 31st Aug., 1909; ditto, Toledo, 3rd to 13th Sept., 1909; ditto, El Cayo, 1st Oct., 1909.

TAYLOR, SIR W. T., K.C.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1848; collr. of customs and excise, Larnaca, 1879; ch. collr. of customs, Cyprus, 1882; ag. recvr.-gen., Nov., 1883, to Feb., 1884; ag. comsnr., Larnaca, in addition to his own duties, 1883 and 1885; ag. recvr.-gen. in addition to duties as ch. collr. of cust., Jan. to Nov., 1887; recvr.-gen. and ch. collr. of cust. and excise, 1891; audr.-gen., Ceylon, May, 1895; ag. col. sec., Ceylon, Oct., 1895, to Feb., 1896, Mar. to Dec., 1896, Mar. to Nov., 1899, and Apr., 1900, to Nov., 1901; col. sec., S. Settns., June, 1901; admntd. govt. of S. S., Oct., 1903, to Apr., 1904, and Feb. to June, 1906; ag. recvr.-gen., F.M.S., Sept., 1904; confirmed, Jan., 1905; agent in London for F.M.S., Oct., 1910.

TEETZEL, HON. JAMES, LL.D.—B. 1853; ed. Woodstock and Galt (Ontario) collegiate insts.; called to the bar, 1877; K.C., 1890; elec. bencher, law soc., Upper Canada, 1891, 1896 and 1900; mayor of Hamilton, Ontario, 1890-1900; unsuccessful candidate for H. of C., 1900; justice, high ct., Ontario, 1903; LL.D., MacMaster Univ., 1907; chrmn., Ontario parole bd., 1910; comsnr. for revision of Ontario statutes, 1908-09-10.

TEMPANY, HAROLD AUGUSTINE, B.Sc.(Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1881; ast. agric. chemist, Leeward Is., 1903; ag. govt. chemist and ast. of agric., July to Dec., 1906, and Jan. to Nov., 1908; govt. chemist and ast. of agric., Leeward Is., Nov., 1909.

TEMPLEMAN, HON. WILLIAM.—B. 1844; apptd. a senator of Canada, 1897; sworn of the priv. coun. and min. without portfolio, 1902; min. of inland rev. and mem. for Victoria, B.C., 1906; min. of inland rev. and of mines, 1907; mem. for Comox-Athi, B.C., 1909.

TEMPLER, FREDERIC GORDON.—Ed. at Harrow and at Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Inn. Tem., Nov., 1872; went the W. circuit; apptd. dist. judge, Kyrenia, Cyprus, 1882; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., May to Oct., 1888; Nov., 1888, to Feb., 1889; Jan. to Mar., 1890; and May to Sept., 1891; dist. judge, Larnaca, 1891; Queen's advoc., Cyprus, 1893; county ct. judge, 1898.

TENNANT, HERCULES, C.M.G. (1876); Barrister-at-law, Inn. Tem.; advoc., sup. Cape Col., 1873; sec. of law comsnr., represented Caledon in house of assm., lieutenant, D.E.O.V.R., 1878-81; extra A. to gov. and comdr-in-chief, July, 1879; w. in Basuto war, 1880-81 (medal), with capt., as ast. staff offr. to Gen. Clarke, and afterwards to Col. Carrington, C.M.G., to ch. just., 1882; librarian of the sup. ct.; ast. regis., sup. ct., and official rev.-authorised edition of Cape statutes, 1884; regis., ct. of appeal, Mar., 1886; J.P. for town and dist., and Capt. dist., July, 1886; taxing offr., sup. ct., Sept., 1884, and A. Dec., 1885; Apr. and Oct., 1886; Mar. and 1887; and Oct., 1887, to Jan., 1889; taxing Feb., 1889; high sheriff, Oct., 1889; regis., sup. ct., 1894; sec. to law dept., Transvaal, June, 1901; J.P., 1901; advocate of sup. 1902; examiner of candidates for admissible conveyancers, 1902; M.L.C., 1906; editor compiler of "The Notary's Manual"; "Justice of the Peace's Manual"; "Rules of the Chronological Table and Index of the Law of the Colony, 1714-1883"; "Master Servants Laws of the Colony"; joint editor authority) of a revised edition of "The Statutes, 1652-1886, and of the Griqualand Statutes"; also compiled (under auth.) "The Index of Government Proclamations Notices, 1803-1881."

TENNYSON, Rt. Hon. Lord, 2nd B. (U.K.), creat. 1884), G.C.M.G. (1903), K.C. (1889), HALLAM TENNYSON, succeeded 1902; poet laureate, 1850-92.—B. 1852; ed. Marl. Trin. Coll., Camb., and Inner Temple; J. Hants; mem. of Marl. Coll. exec. compared his father's memoir, published in S. Australia, 1899; gov.-gen., Commonwealth Australia, 1902-1904.
TEW, GEORGE McLEOD.—Indian pol., 1893-7; Uganda rly. pol., 1899-1903; ast. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., 1st Apr., 1904.

THAINE, ROBERT NIRMANN.—B. 1877; Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1898; ast. to govt. agt. S. Prov., Apr., 1899; Prov., Aug., 1900; comsnr. of requests and mag., Balapitiya, June, 1901; off. ast. to agt., Ratnapura, Dec., 1901; ast. govt., Kalutara, Mar., 1902; dist. judge, Badulla, 1902; pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1904; landings cust., Colombo, Sept., 1904; ast. govt., Trincomalee, June, 1906; Puttalam, Aug., dist. judge, Jaffna, July, 1908.
THATCHER, J.—Ed. at Bristol Gram., examr. of loan acts., audit dept., O.R.C., Jan., 1904; examr. of accts., dist. 1st July, 1904.
THOMAS, E. J.—Entered Victoria College, 1852; receiver of revenue, etc., 1863; sec. to numerous boards and comsnrs.; privy to Sir B. O'Loughlin, Bart., 1881; sec., privy dept., 1883.
THOMAS, HON. JOSIAH.—B. 1863; M.L. New South Wales, 1894-1901; elected to H. of R., C. of A., 1901; re-elected in 1903; 1906; P.M.G., C. of A., Apr., 1910.
THOMLINSON, ROBT.—Intermediate Ed. Lond. (economics and polit. science); scholar of Lond. Schl. of Economics and Pol. Science; 2nd div. clk., nat. educn. off., Irel.

THOMPSON, WILLIAM AUSTIN.—2nd cl. supervsr. G. Coast, Apr., 1894; ag. ast. treas., Cape Coast Castle, Mar., 1895; cashr., Accra, Mar., 1896; ag. ast. treas., Apr., 1896; 1st cl. supervsr., Sept., 1897; sent on serv. in hinterland, Feb., 1898; invalided and retd. from West Afr., Feb., 1900; treas., etc., Falklands, July, 1901; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., in addition to duties as treasr., May to Nov., 1902, Apr., 1903, to Jan., 1904, July to Sept., 1905; and from June to Nov., 1909; in charge of headqrs. during govr.'s visit to W. Falklands, Aug., 1909.

THOMPSTONE, SYDNEY WILSON, C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1863; F.R.C.P. (Edin.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Camb.); form. house surg., Roy. Infir., Glasgow; apptd. dist. med. offr., Oil Riv. Proteo. (now S. Niger), Mar. 22nd, 1893; ast. prin. med. offr., 1897; ag. prin. med. offr., Aug.-Nov., 1898; Nov., 1899, to June, 1900, and June, 1901, to Oct., 1902; prin. med. offr., N. Nigeria, May, 1903.

THOMSON, ALEX. MCDONALD.—Ed. Abdn. Univ., M.A.; 1st class hon. math., 1883; ast. prof. of math., Aberdeen, 1886-7; Bacon scholar, Gray's Inn, 1888; cadet, Hong Kong, 1887; attached to C.O., 1888; passed cadet, Dec., 1890; ag. supt., Victoria gaol, Apr., 1891, to Mar., 1892; ag. clk. of couns. Jan., 1891, to Oct., 1892; ag. ast. col. sec., Mar. to Oct., 1892; ag. registr.-gen. and provisional mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1893; ag. ast. col. sec., 1884; ag. col. treas., Mar., 1895; and mem. exec. and legis. couns.; mem. governing body of Queen's Coll., 1894; postmr.-gen., 1897; col. treas., and mem. exec. and legis. couns., 1898; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1892, Nov., 1903, to July, 1904, and in 1907, 1909 and 1910; has served on various comtees. and commsn.
THOMSON, HON. DUGALD.—B. 1848; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894, 1895, and 1898; mem. of Commonw. parl., Mar., 1901, Dec., 1903, and Dec., 1906; min. for home affairs, ditto, Oct., 1904.

THOMSON, JOHN HASTINGS.—B. 1873; ed. Wallace Hall Academy, Dumfries-shire, Scotland; apptd. ast. dist. offr., Somaliland, May, 1901; ag. consul, Berbera, Sept., 1901, to Aug., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Bulhar, Jan., 1904-5; polit. offr. with Gen. Swayne in Abyssinia, Mar. to Apr., 1905; polit. offr. with troops on Abyssinian border, May to July, 1905; ag. sub-comsnr., Zeyla, 2nd June, 1905; Somaliland medal with clasp, 1902-4; dist. offr., Makhir Coast, Aug., 1906; ag. sub-comsnr., Zeyla, 5th June, 1907.

THORBURN, J. JAMIESON, C.M.G. (1907).—Writer, Ceylon, Oct., 1886; ag. govt. agt., N. Cent. Prov., Mar. to Aug., 1889; ag. pol. mag., Galle, Dec., 1893; ag. off. ast. to govt. agt., in various dists. from Oct., 1889, to 1895; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., Jan., 1896; 2nd ast. col. sec., Jan. and Dec., 1898, and June, 1899; ag. ast. postmr.-gen., Jan., 1899; 2nd ast. col. sec., June, 1899; ag. sec. central irrigation board, Jan., 1900; principal ast. col. sec. and joint sec., loan board, May, 1900; senior prov. comsnr. (in anticipation of proposed amalgamation of the admstrs. of Lagos and S. Nigeria), performed duties of sec. to admstr. of S. Nigeria, dep. high comsnr., S. Nigeria, Mar. to June, 1905; ag. gov., Lagos, and ag. high comsnr., S. Nigeria, July, 1905; ag. col. sec., Lagos, 12th Feb. to 30th Apr., 1906; lieut.-gov. and col. sec., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906; dep. gov., 24th Dec., 1906, to 4th Jan., 1907, 6th-14th Jan., 1907, and 27th Jan. to 17th Mar., 1907; ag. gov., 14th May to 27th Oct., 1907; govr., Gold Coast, 24th Oct., 1910.

THORNHILL, PONSONBY.—Supernumerary offr., cust., B. Guiana, Aug.-Sept., 1887; astt. record clk., P.W.D., Oct., 1887; astt. to clk. of wks. (1st cls. offr.), P.W.D., New Amsterdam, May, 1888; 5th cls. offr., P.W.D., Apr., 1892; ag. clk. of wks. (1st cls. offr.) Berbice and distcts., Feb. to Aug., 1893; 5th cls. clk., G.P.O., June, 1896; clk., P.O. savings bank, Jan., 1899; 4th cls. clk., G.P.O., Jan., 1900; 3rd cls. clk., treasy., Georgetown, Jan., 1901; ag. cashier, June, 1904, to July, 1905, and May to Nov., 1908.

THORNTON, GEORGE.—M.D. (Edin.); M.R.C.P.(Lond.); D.P.H.(Oxford); house physcn., Edin. Royal Infirmy., 1890; house surg., Metropolitan hosp., Lond.; house surg., children's hosp., Hull; astt. med. offr., Tooting Fever hosp., 1893-99; civil surg. to H.M. forces in S. Africa; med. supt., Pretoria hosp., 1900-1908; med. supt., gen. hosp., Colombo, Ceylon, Nov., 1908.

THORNTON, SWINFORD LESLIE, B.A.—B.1853; Lincoln Coll., Oxon.; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Nov., 1877; admitted to the bar, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1886; registr., sup. ct.; comanr., ct. of requests, and collr. of stamps, Malacca, June, 1887; ag. sen. mag., Singapore, July, 1888; reg. sup. ct., and mag., Malacca, July, 1892; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, 1894; ag. admstr., Nov., 1894, to Mar., 1895; ag. ch. just., Nov., 1895; res. mag., Jamaica, 1896; mem. comtee. to revise rules and form res. mag. etc., 1897; mem. parochial bds. comanr.; and chrmn. house and land tax comtee., 1899; ag. puisne judge, 1901; puisne judge, S. Sttlmts., 22nd Mar., 1904; sen. puisne judge, Aug., 1906.

THORPE, WM. EDWARD.—Offr., cls. III., Ceylon civ. ser.; ed. St. Edmund's schl., Canterbury; cadet, Ceylon, 1891; office astt., Anuradhapura, 1892; ditto, Jaffna, 1894; ditto, Ratnapura, 1896; ditto, Colombo, 1896, with additional duties as astt. supt. pol.; astt. govt. agt., Kalutara, 1898; ditto, Chilaw, 1899; dist. judge, Matare, 1900; pol. mag., Colombo, 1903; dep. collr. cust., Colombo, 1905; dist. judge, Matare, May, 1908; ditto, Galle, Dec., 1908.

THREIFALL, WM. HERBERT.—B. 1875; apptd., after open compet., exam., to controller's off., London postal service, Apr., 1896; supt. of registn., G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1905; ag. supt. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore, Nov., 1905; supt. of registn., ditto, Sept., 1906; supt. of mails, Penang, Sept., 1907.

THUNDER, MAURICE.—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Sept., 1906; ag. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Nov., 1907; passed final exam.in Malay, Jan., 1908; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Apr., 1908; ag. astt. to atty.-gen., Dec., 1908.

TIBBITS, CHARLES KENT.—B. 1877; employed col. sec.'s off.; treas. and aud. off., Antigua, Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. sav. bank, Jan., 1895; 1st clk., post off., Jan., 1898; ag. postmtr. in 1899, 1900, 1901, 1903, 1904, 1906; and from June, 1906, to Mar., 1907; postmtr. of Antigua, and federal postmtr. of Leeward Is., 1st Apr., 1907; apptd. a library trustee and a J.P., Antigua, 1910.

TIRBITS, ELWOOD D'ARCY.—B. 1880; ag. 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, Jan., 1899, to Nov., 1900 (almost continuously); jun. audit clk., 1st Dec., 1900; 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., 8th July, 1901; ag. 3rd clk., 29th Aug., 1901, to 1st June, 1902, and 5th June, 1902, to 5th Mar., 1903; ag. priv. sec. to gov., 17th Oct., 1903, to 5th May, 1904; ag. 3rd clk., 24th Mar. to 1st Aug., 1905; ag. clk. to adminstr. and clk. to exec. coun., Dominica, 2nd Aug., 1905, to 5th Apr., 1906; ag. 2nd clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, and clk. to exec. coun., Antigua, from 6th Apr., 1906; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, 23rd Sept., 1906, to 23rd Jan., 1907; ag. 2nd clk., ditto, and clk. to exec. sec. and supt. of govt. printing off., from 5th Sep., 1907, to 8th Mar., 1908; ag. astt. col. sec., 13th Nov. to 11th Dec., 1907, and from 11th 22nd Jan., 1908; ag. 2nd clk., 20th Apr., 19th Oct., 1908; and from 19th April, 1909.
