WINDHAM, WM.—Ck., res. comsrr.'s office, Zulu native reserve, Apr., 1883; studt. interp. native affairs office, Natal, Mar., 1884; ck. and regstr. to special comsrr. for Zulu affairs, Oct., 1885; ck. and interp. to res. comsrr. and ch. mag., Zululand, June, 1887; sec. to ditto, Jan., 1889; ck., exec. coun., Natal, Sept., 1889; priv. sec. to gov., Natal, Oct., 1889; sec. for Zululand, Dec., 1889; priv. sec. to gov., Zululand, Feb., 1890; gov't. sec., Zululand, Feb., 1894; ast. under-seer. for Zulu and affairs, 1897; regstr. of deeds and regstr.-gen., Natal, Mar., 1898; (compiled abstract of opinions and court decisions upon transactions in deeds office, 1878-1898); sec. to native affairs dept., Transvaal, 23rd Sept., 1901; M.L.C., 1905.

WINGFIELD, MAURICE E.—Ed. at Winchester and Trin. Coll. Camb.; M.A. 1896; sec. to royal comsrr., hut tax, S. Leone, 1898; ag. treas., collr. of cust., postmr. and mem. ex. and leg. couns., Falkland Is., 1899-1900; priv. sec. to gov., of Queensland, 1902; priv. sec. to gov.-gen. of Australia, 1903-4; ast. sec., S. African constitutions comtce., 1906.

WINKFIELD, JOHN, M.A. (Oxon.) (Worcester Coll.)—B. 1862; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1889; dist. comsrr., Lagos, 1896; ag. Queen's advocate, July, 1898, to May, 1899; ag. atty.-gen., July-Sept., 1900; atty.-gen., So. Nig., Dec., 1900; compiled laws of S. Nig., 1900-1901; puisne judge, Jan., 1905; ag. ch. just., Lagos, Jan., 1905; ditto, Calabar, Aug., 1906, to Jan., 1906; ditto, Lagos, May to Oct., 1906; ditto, Calabar, Apr. to July, 1907; ditto, Lagos, July, 1907, to May, 1908; Nov., 1908, to Jan., 1909, and from Mar. to Sept., 1910.

WINTER, SIR FRANCIS PRATT, KT. (1900), M.G.(1892).—B. 1848; ag. atty.-gen., Fiji, 1887-88; ch. judicial offr. and mem. exec. and legis. couns., Br. N. Guinea, 1888; ag. admstr., Sept., 1894, to June, 1895, and Oct., 1898, to Mar., 1899; et., 1903.

WINTER, SIR J. S., K.C.M.G. (1888), K.C.—B. 1845; speaker, house of assem., Newfoundland, 1877-8; mem. exec. coun., Feb., 1879; solr.-gen., Oct., 1882; atty.-gen., 1886; agt. for Newfoundland fishery confce., Washington, 1887; resig. office of atty.-gen., 1889; puisne judge, 1893; resig., 1896; prime min., 1897-1900; atty.-gen., 1897; mem. of delegn. to imperial gov't. re French treaties and other matters, 1898; min. of just. and mem. of reasy. bd., 1898; high comsrr. to internat. confce. between Gt. Brit. and U.S.A. at Quebec and Washington, 1898.
WISE BERNHARD RINGROSE, K.C.—Attyn., N.S. Wales, May, 1887, to Feb., 1888, 1899-901, and 1901-4; mem. of Austn. Fed. Conv., 1897-8.
WITHERS, A. K.—Ed. at Bedford schl.; served in survey dept. under Govt. of Egypt; confld. clk. to admstr. of Gambia, 1897.

WITTEENOOM, HON. SIR E. H., K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1854; elected M.L.C., W. Australia, 1883; resig.in Dec.; re-elected 1884; resig. 1886; elected, M.L.C., July, 1894; min. for mines, Dee., 1894; agent-gen., July, 1898; resigned, 1901; M.L.C.

WOODEHOUSE, CLARENCE B.—Entd. Sandhurst Coll., 1875; gazetted to 77th Regt., 1876; resigned comm., 1880; local commdt. of police, Cyprus, 1st Jan., 1894; ag. comsrr., Limassol, 12th July, 1894; ditto, Papho, 1st Apr., 1895; comsrr., Papho, 7th June, 1895.

WOODEHOUSE, H. E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1846; ed. Repton Schl.; Hong Kong cadet, 1867; student interp., 1869; supt. of Chinese pol., 1870; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1875; pol. mag., 1880; hon. sec. to the local comn. in connection with the Indian and Col. Exhibn., and special comsrr. in London, 1886; mem. exec. coun., May, 1895; retired.

WOODEHOUSE, Lieut.-Gen. Sir JOSCELIN HENNEAGE, K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; comsrr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov't. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; comd. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Argin; infantry div. at Toski; Presidency dist., 1898-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in comd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasps); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cls. Medjidie; 2nd cls. Osmanieh; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Gov't; gov' of Bermuda, 30th May, 1907.

WOODEHOUSE, P. P. J.—Ed. Eliz. Coll., Guernsey; first clk., reg.-gen.'s dept., Hong Kong, 1897-1900; passed in Chinese, 1899; census offir., 1900; ast. supt. of police, 1901; attached Punjab police, 1901-2; passed in Urdu, 1902; J.P., 1902; census offir., 1906; passed in Punjabi, 1908; mem. of bd. of examnrs., 1907; ag. dep. supt. of pol. and fire brig., Oct., 1907, Aug., 1908, May, 1909, and Oct., 1909.

WOLFE, E. D. C.—Tonbridge schl. (1890-94); exhib. in mod. langa., Calus Coll., Camb. (1894); exhib. from Tonbridge schl. (1894); 3rd class mod. langa., tripos. (1896); 1st class Pt. I, and 2nd class Pt. II, Law Special (1897); B.A. (1897); cadet, F. M.S., 1898; sent to Canton to study Cantonese (1899); transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; regstr., land ct., Nov., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., Oct., 1902; ag. ast. regstr.-gen., Nov., 1902; sec. to pub. wks. inquiry comm., 1902; collr. of Crown rent, New Territory, Feb., 1903; regstr., land ct., Mar., 1903; ag. pol. mag., N.T., and ast. supt. of pol., 1903-4; collr. of Crown rent, N.T., 1904; seconded to Transvaal gov't. serv., 1905; ag. 2nd pol. mag., Apr. and May, 1907; ag. inspr. of schls., 1907; ag. regstr.-gen., Aug., 1907; ast. regstr.-gen., Oct., 1907; ag. inspr. of schls., Oct., 1907, to Mar., 1909; ast. regstr.-gen., Mar. to June, 1909; ag. head of sanitary dept., June, 1909; head of sanitary dept., Oct., 1909.

WOLFERSTAN, LITTLETON EDWARD PIPE.—Ed. Eton and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1888; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1889; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1891; ag. priv. sec. to Sir C. Smith, Jan. to Apr., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Feb. to Mar., and June to Nov., 1892; ag. dist. offr., S.W. dist., Penang, Mar., 1893; 3rd mag., Penang, Apr., 1895; ag. dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, June, 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore Aug., 1897; sheriff and dep. regis., sup. ct., Singapore, 1898, but still ag. 2nd mag., Singapore; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Feb., 1900; ag. collr. ld. rev., Penang, May, 1900; ag. sec. to H. Cr. for Fed. Malay States, May, 1901; ag. sen. dist. offr. Prov. Wellesley, May, 1902; collector of land rev. and regis. of deeds, Penang, Aug., 1902; offl. assignee, S.S., Sept., 1903; sen. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Feb., 1907; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1907; ag. inspr. of prisons, S.S., Apr., 1909.

WOLFF, ERNEST CHARTERIS HOLFORD.—F.M.S. civ. ser.; b. 1875; ed. at Merchiston and Trin. Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1897; cadet, Pahang, F.M.S., Nov., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1899; ag. treas., supt. of poste and tels., and collr. of stamp duties, Pahang, Jan., 1898; ag. ast. dist. offr., Pekan, May, 1899; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, May, 1901; sec. to ditto, Oct., 1901; ast. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1903; dist. treas., Telok Anson, Jan., 1905, but still ag. sec. to res., N. Semb.; sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1905; ag. ast. sec. to res.-gen., F.M.S., May, 1906; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1909, but still ag. ast. sec. to res.-gen.

WOLLASTON, HARRY NEWTON PHILLIPS, C.M.G. (1907); I.S.O. (1903); LL.D., J.P.—B. 1846; apptd. (after exam.), to dept. of trade and custs., 1863; grad. with hona., in law, Melb. Univ., 1884; called to the bar, 1884, and apptd. counsel to marine bd., Victoria, which apptm. he held till 1901; promoted to bech. clk. of dept., 1899; apptd. permanent head of Victorian dept. of trade and cust., 1891; compt.-gen. of Commonwealth dept., of trade and cust., and permanent head, 1901; represented Australia at Impl. Shipping Conference, London, 1907.

WOLSELEY, FIELD-MARSHAL VISCOUNT (created 1885), SIR GARNET JOSEPH WOLSELEY, G.C.B. (1880), K.C.M.G. (1870), G.C.M.G. and K.C.B. (1874), BARON (1882), K.F. (1885).—B. 1833; ensign, Mar., 1852; capt., Jan., 1855; major, 90th foot, Mar., 1858; lieut.-col., Apr., 1859; col., June, 1865; maj.-gen., 1874; lieut.-gen., Apr., 1878; gen., 1882; field-marshal, 1895; served with 80th foot in the Burmese war of 1852-53, severely wounded (medal); served with distinction in the Crimea and taking of Sebastopol, with the 90th L.I., and was severely wounded in a sortie, after which he received the Legion of Honour, the 5th class of the Medjidie, etc.; was also at the siege and capture of Lucknow and defence of Alumbagh, and numerous actions in Oudh, when he was made brevet lieut.-col.; served in China on the staff of the qrtmr.-gen. throughout the Chinese campaign of 1860, for which he also received a medal and two clasps; dep. qrtmr.-gen. in Canada, Oct., 1867; ast. adjt.-gen. at headqrs., 1871; apptd. for special service on the G. Coast in connection with the Ashantee war, Aug., 1873; brought the Ashantee war to a successful conclusion, and received the thanks of the House of Lords and the House of Commons for his services; was made a G.C.M.G. and a K.C.B., and a major-gen.; and inspr.-gen. of auxiliary forces, Apr., 1874; specially apptd. to administer temporarily the govt. of Natal from Feb. to Sept., 1875; acted as vice-pres. of the London S. African confce., Aug., 1876; mem. of the Indian coun., 1876; comnrr. and comdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, July, 1878; gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Natal, and of the Transvaal, and comdr. of the forces in S. Africa, May, 1879, and high comnrr. for S. E. Africa; qrtmr.-gen., Apr., 1880; adjt.-gen., 1882; commanded the forces in Egypt in the temporary rank of gen., in 1882, and recd. the thanks of both houses of parlmt., and peerage; and from the Khedive of Egypt, Grand Order of the Osmanlieh; commanded Nile expdn. and other operations in Egypt and the Soudan for the relief of Gen. Gordon, recd. received the thanks of both houses of parlmt., was raised to the rank of viscount, and made author of "The Soldier's Pocket Book Field Service"; comdr.-in-ch. in Ireland, 1890; comdr.-in-ch., 1895-1900.

WOOD, A. E.—Ed. at Liverpool Coll.; mas. of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1903; 1st mods.; lit. hum.; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offl. exam. of male assisted emigrants, July to Nov., 1908; ag. ast. regis.-gen., Nov., 1908, to 1909, and in May-June, 1909; passed cert. 1st Apr., 1910; ag. ast. regis.-gen., Apr., 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., N.T., 18th May, 1910.

WOOD, DAVID.—Ed. Gordon's Coll., deen; ch. acctnt., P.W.D., Hong Kong; supt. of accts., corres., and stores, P.W.D., was sec. to comsn. on housing the Chinese during the plague, 1894; ag. gov't. assessor of taxes, 1900 to Nov., 1901, May to Oct., 1902; and to Oct., 1905, to Oct., 1906; J.P., 1902.

WOOD, FIELD-MARSHAL SIR HENRY EVANS, G.C.B., G.C.M.G. (1882), B. 1838; entered navy 1832, and the army 1835; served in Crimea; received the V.C. for his gallantry hunting down the rebels in the Seronge jungle, admstd. the govt. of Natal from Feb. to Dec., commanded the forces in Natal and the Transvaal, and was a mem. of the roy. comsn. for the settlement of the affairs of the Transvaal, 1881, but dismissed from the report; commanded the 2nd brig. 2nd div., in Egypt, 1882; apptd. by the Khedive to the command of the Egyptian troops, served in the Nile expdn., 1884; comdr.-in-ch., E. dist., 1886; qrtmr.-gen. of the forces, 1893; apptd. to command of army, 1901.

WOOD, JOHN R.—Ed. City of Lond. schl., Camb.; foundation scholar of Jesus Coll., 1st cl., class. tripus, 1899; B.A., 1899; ed. Hong Kong, 1899; registr. of land ct., Nov., 1901; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, May to Oct., 1902; J.P., 1902; passed cadet, 1902; mem. of comsn., 1903-4; ast. land offr. in N. T., Jan., 1904; ast. reg.-gen., Apr. to Aug., 1905; mem. squatters' bd., May, 1906; ag. 2nd pol. mag., Mar., 1908; dep. regis. and appraiser, supr. Nov., 1908; 2nd pol. mag., 21st Mar., 1909.
WOOD, JOHN T.—M.A. (Oxon); F.R.G.S.; registrar of mining rights, Transvaal.
WOODARD, HENRY.—Govt. storekeeper, B. Cent. Africa Prot., 1893; ch. commiss.-offr., 1894; ch. transport offr., 1906.

WOODCOCK, GEORGE ALBERT.—Ed. at John's Coll., Battersea; apptd. ast. ms. Victoria Coll., Hong Kong, 15th Mar., 1890; bar, Gray's Inn, 1897; ag. sec., sanitary com., Mar., 1900, to July, 1901; sec. of san. bd., J. 1901; J.P., 1902; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 5th July, 1905; ag. dep. regis. and acctnt., etc., Hong Kong, Dec., 1908; 1st clk., magistrate, Sept., 1909.

WOODE, BENJAMIN TREGASKIS, M.R.P.S., B. 1869; ed. at Wesleyan High school, Freetown, S. Leone; jun.clk., Queen's advocate offr., S. Leone, Apr., 1890, to July, 1891; cust. offr., former Benin and Warri dists., in Oil Rivers Prot., Aug., 1891, to July, 1892; cust. offr., Bonny and Yor.

WRIGHT, W. S.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin (B.A.), 1905; 1st place, LL.B. degree; cert. of hon. incorp. law soc.; asst. collr., Uganda, 1907; 1st cls. mag., Uganda, 1908-9; regis. of high ct., E.A.P., Aug., 1909.

WRIGHTSON, WALSH, C.M.G. (1900), M.I.C.E.—B. 1852; served articles with Chas. Fowler, Leeds; asst. to boro' engineer, Leeds, 1872; dist. engineer, P.W.D., Ceylon, 1875; seconded for serv. on Nanuoya Rly. extensions, 1883; spec. serv. to restore Kalawewa irrigation tank, 1884; on successful completion, promoted prov. engr., N.W. Prov., 1888; asst. to dir. pub. wks., 1892; prov. engr., E. Prov., 1894; dir. pub. wks., Trinidad, and offl. mem. legis. coun., Jan., 1985; apptd. mem. ex. coun., June, 1895; res. engr., harbour, water and sewerage wks., 1896; ret., 1907.

WRIXON, THE HON. Sir HENRY JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1892)—B. 1839; atty.-gen., Victoria, Feb., 1886; resig., Nov., 1890; mem. of Federation Confce., 1891; represented Victoria at Col. Confce., 1894; author of "Socialism: Notes on a Political Tour."
WROUGHTON, LEWIS.—Sub-inspr. Basutoland mtd. pol., 1884; ag. asst. comsnr., Quthing, 1890; asst. comsnr., Basutoland, 1891; ag. govt. sec., 1901; govt. sec., 1902.

WYATT, ROBT.—B. 1870; Lond. Metro. pol., 1891 to 1895; B. Hond. pol., June, 1895; asst. supt., Feb., 1902; ag. supt., May, 1902, to May, 1903; ag. dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, July to Aug., 1903; ag. dist. comsnr., Corosol, 26th Apr. to 11th July, 1905; ditto, Cays, Feb., 1906; ag. supt. pol., 14th July, 1905: J.P. for Colony; Hythe extra certif., 1899; supt. of pol., 1st Apr., 1906; certif. of proficiency for field offrs. with spec. distinction, schl. of instr., Chelsea barracks, 1907; certif. for management of English convict and local prisons, 1907; attached to London fire brigade for training, 1907.
WYKESMITH, PELHAM, L.R.C.P. and S. (Edin); med. offr., Nyasaland Prot., Apr., 1900.

WYLIE, THOS. ALEXANDER.—B. 1866; asst. traffic supt., Ceylon rlys., 17th May, 1888; ag. traffic supt., on several occasions; ag. acctnt., 27th Apr., 1893; acctnt., 11th May, 1894; traffic supt., 1st Feb., 1906; ag. gen. man., 16th Apr., 1907.
WYNNE, CHAS. OWEN, L.R.C.P. and S.E.—B. 1871; dist. med. offr., St. Kitts, Leeward Is., May, 1903; ag. comsnr., mag., and med. offr., Virgin Is., May, 1906; dist. med. offr., Nevis, Feb., 1907.

YOUNG, ALFRED KARNEY.—B.A., Magd. Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., May, 1889; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admnstr. of the govt., 1895; ag. registr.-gen., 1895-7; dist. comsnr., Orange Walk, 1898; gen., June, 1898; ag. supt. of police, May to 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal ad. and police mag., Seychelles, 23rd Sept., 1899; atty.-gen., B.C. Africa Prot., 17th Mr., 1906; ch. just., Seychelles, Aug., 1909.

YOUNG, CAPT. SIR ARTHUR HENDERSON, K.C.M.G. (1908); C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1871; late 27th Inniskillings; ed. at Edin., Am. Rugby, and Sandhurst; local comdt., mil. m. Kyrena, Aug., 1878; asst. comsnr., Paphos, 1878; comsnr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prin. offr., 1892; mem. legis. coun., 1892; ch. sec., admtd. the govt., July to Dec., 1895, Jan. to 1898; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connection with relief work, Nov., 1902; col. S. Stlmta., June, 1906; admtd. govt., Feb. 7 May, 1909; ch. sec., F.M.S., 1910.
YOUNG, HON. HENRY ALFRED.—Ed. by Derby Sch. and Edin. Univ.; called to the bar (Scot.), July, 1899; asst. crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 22nd June, 1906.
